# Awesome AI/ML Applications in Pharmacometrics 🧬🤖

A curated list of research papers on AI/ML applications in pharmacometrics and clinical pharmacology, regularly updated.

**Last Updated**: 2026-03-14

Interactive explorer: <https://victoriapb.github.io/awesome-ai-ml-pharmacometrics/>

## Adherence to drug regimen

- **[Use of a Novel Artificial Intelligence Platform on Mobile Devices to Assess Dosing Compliance in a Phase 2 Clinical Trial in Subjects With Schizophrenia.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/28223265/)**
	- Published: 2017Feb21
	- Summary: The paper describes using an AI platform on mobile devices to assess dosing compliance in a Phase 2 schizophrenia clinical trial, improving upon traditional pill counts and self-reports.

- **[Pharmacokinetics-Informed Neural Network for Predicting Opioid Administration Moments with Wearable Sensors.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38646089/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Mechanism-informed machine learning
	- Published: 2024Feb
	- Summary: Uses pharmacokinetics-informed neural networks with wearable sensor data to predict timing of opioid administrations for monitoring drug use and detecting potential misuse.

- **[Prediction of Thiopurine Metabolite Levels Based on Haematological and Biochemical Parameters.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31568041/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2019Oct
	- Summary: The paper builds predictive models using supervised learning to predict low thiopurine metabolite levels and nonadherence in pediatric IBD patients based on clinical parameters.

- **[Machine learning-integrated electrochemical sensing of ciprofloxacin for digital point-of-care therapeutic drug monitoring.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41361653/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Neural networks, Tree-based models, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2025Dec09
	- Summary: Machine learning models (MLP, SVM, decision tree, random forest) are integrated with electrochemical sensors to accurately quantify ciprofloxacin in urine for therapeutic drug monitoring.

- **[Imatinib adherence prediction using machine learning approach in patients with gastrointestinal stromal tumor.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39238433/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Deep learning
	- Published: 2025Jan01
	- Summary: This study develops machine learning and deep learning models to predict imatinib nonadherence risk in GIST patients and identify associated factors.

- **[Individualized Adherence Benchmarks for HIV Pre-Exposure Prophylaxis.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33191774/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models
	- Published: 2021Jun
	- Summary: Random forest analysis is used to rank covariates for improving pharmacokinetic modeling of tenofovir concentrations to create individualized adherence benchmarks for HIV PrEP.

- **[Physicochemical properties determining drug detection in skin.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34793633/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2022Mar
	- Summary: Random forest classifier predicts drug detection on skin using molecular descriptors, with SHAP for model interpretation to enable noninvasive therapeutic drug monitoring.

## Automation of PK/PD modeling

- **[Deep learning prediction of patient response time course from early data via neural-pharmacokinetic/pharmacodynamic modelling.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40271424/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Neural networks, Time-series modeling, Mechanism-informed machine learning
	- Published: 2021Aug
	- Summary: Neural-PK/PD framework combines deep learning with pharmacological principles using neural ODEs to predict patient response time courses and simulate untested dosing regimens.

- **[Automatic determination of arterial input function for dynamic contrast enhanced MRI in tumor assessment.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/18979795/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2008
	- Summary: The paper presents an automatic method using detection and segmentation algorithms to determine arterial input function for pharmacokinetic modeling in DCE-MRI tumor assessment.

- **[Deep learning quantification of vascular pharmacokinetic parameters in mouse brain tumor models.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35345331/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2022Mar16
	- Summary: Deep learning is used to automate generation of pharmacokinetic parameter maps from DCE MRI as a surrogate to conventional PK modeling in mouse brain tumor models.

- **[Towards pathway curation through literature mining--a case study using PharmGKB.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/24297561/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2014
	- Summary: A rule-based text mining system extracts pharmacokinetic and pharmacodynamic pathway information from literature to automate pathway curation in PharmGKB.

- **[A proof of concept reinforcement learning based tool for non parametric population pharmacokinetics workflow optimization.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36478350/)**
	- Methodology: Reinforcement learning
	- Published: 2023Feb
	- Summary: This paper presents a proof of concept using SARSA reinforcement learning to automate the iterative process of building population pharmacokinetic models.

- **[Fully-automated deep learning-powered system for DCE-MRI analysis of brain tumors.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31980106/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2020Jan
	- Summary: Deep learning system automates DCE-MRI analysis for brain tumor pharmacokinetic modeling and biomarker extraction, eliminating manual processes and human error.

- **[Learning pharmacometric covariate model structures with symbolic regression networks.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37864654/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Feature selection, Model selection
	- Published: 2024Apr
	- Summary: The paper uses symbolic regression networks to automatically identify covariate model structures in pharmacokinetic modeling, demonstrated on propofol clinical data.

- **[Assessing the Potential of Generative Artificial Intelligence Models to Assist Experts in the Development of Pharmacokinetic Models.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40922744/)**
	- Methodology: LLM
	- Published: 2025Jul
	- Summary: This study explores using generative AI models to assist experts in developing scripts for pharmacokinetic models, specifically for constructing population PK models.

- **[Shap-Cov: An Explainable Machine Learning Based Workflow for Rapid Covariate Identification in Population Modeling.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40601439/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2025Aug
	- Summary: The paper presents Shap-Cov, an explainable ML workflow using SHAP analysis for automated covariate identification in population PK/PD modeling with uncertainty quantification.

- **[Automatic detection of arterial input function for brain DCE-MRI in multi-site cohorts.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40808286/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2025Dec
	- Summary: A deep learning model is developed to automatically extract arterial input functions from DCE-MRI images for quantitative pharmacokinetic modeling in multi-site cohorts.

- **[Fine-tuning BERT for automatic ADME semantic labeling in FDA drug labeling to enhance product-specific guidance assessment.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36632860/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, LLM
	- Published: 2023Feb
	- Summary: Fine-tuned BERT model for automatic semantic labeling of ADME paragraphs in FDA drug labeling to automate retrieval of pharmacokinetic information for PSG assessment.

- **[QSP-Copilot: An AI-Augmented Platform for Accelerating Quantitative Systems Pharmacology Model Development.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41159846/)**
	- Methodology: LLM, AI Agents
	- Published: 2025Nov
	- Summary: QSP-Copilot uses large language models and multi-agent systems to automate quantitative systems pharmacology model development workflows, reducing development time by 40%.

- **[pyDarwin machine learning algorithms application and comparison in nonlinear mixed-effect model selection and optimization.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38941056/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models, Gaussian processes, Model selection, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2024Dec
	- Summary: This paper compares five ML algorithms (GA, GP, RF, GBRT, PSO) for automated population pharmacokinetic model selection as alternatives to traditional FABE methods.

- **[Fast screening of covariates in population models empowered by machine learning.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34019213/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Neural networks, Feature selection
	- Published: 2021Aug
	- Summary: ML methods including random forest, neural networks, and SVR are used to accelerate covariate selection in population pharmacokinetic modeling, outperforming traditional approaches.

- **[Leveraging large language models in pharmacometrics: evaluation of NONMEM output interpretation and simulation capabilities.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40464844/)**
	- Methodology: LLM
	- Published: 2025Jun04
	- Summary: This study evaluates large language models (Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini, Llama) for automating pharmacometric tasks including generating model diagrams, tables, and reports from NONMEM outputs.

- **[Stochastic Gates for Covariate Selection in Population Pharmacokinetics Modeling.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41637692/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Feature selection
	- Published: 2026Feb
	- Summary: Neural networks with stochastic gates are used for automated covariate selection in population pharmacokinetics modeling to efficiently identify relevant covariates.

- **[Synthesis of pharmacokinetic pathways through knowledge acquisition and automated reasoning.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/19908398/)**
	- Methodology: AI Agents
	- Published: 2010
	- Summary: The paper presents an automated system using knowledge acquisition and logical reasoning to synthesize pharmacokinetic pathways from curated databases and literature extraction.

- **[A sensitive and efficient method to systematically detect two biophenols in medicinal herb, herbal products and rat plasma based on thorough study of derivatization and its convenient application to pharmacokinetics with semi-automated device.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/22749364/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2012Aug03
	- Summary: The paper uses artificial neural networks coupled with genetic algorithms to optimize derivatization conditions for detecting biophenols in pharmacokinetic studies.

- **[Accelerating virtual patient generation with a Bayesian optimization and machine learning surrogate model.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39630593/)**
	- Methodology: Bayesian ML, Surrogate modeling
	- Published: 2025Mar
	- Summary: The paper uses Bayesian optimization with machine learning surrogate models to accelerate virtual patient generation for QSP model validation in clinical trial simulations.

- **[Deep learning for automatic organ and tumor segmentation in nanomedicine pharmacokinetics.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38250039/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2024
	- Summary: Deep learning using 3D U-Net for automatic organ segmentation in multimodal imaging to automate pharmacokinetic analysis and dosimetry calculations in nanomedicine development.

- **[A Deep Learning-Based Framework for Highly Accelerated Prostate MR Dispersion Imaging.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39272841/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2024Aug27
	- Summary: Deep neural networks are used to accelerate pharmacokinetic parameter estimation in MR dispersion imaging for prostate cancer, replacing time-consuming nonlinear curve fitting.

- **[Development of an HPLC-UV method for quantification of posaconazole in low-volume plasma samples: design of experiments and machine learning models.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39633472/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024Dec04
	- Summary: Machine learning models were used alongside experimental design to optimize HPLC-UV method development for posaconazole quantification in plasma samples.

- **[From data to QSP models: a pipeline for using Boolean networks for hypothesis inference and dynamic model building.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34988912/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2022Feb
	- Summary: The paper presents a pipeline using k-means clustering and Boolean networks to convert data into QSP models, combining ML with mechanistic modeling for drug response prediction.

- **[Application of Deep Neural Networks as a Prescreening Tool to Assign Individualized Absorption Models in Pharmacokinetic Analysis.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34073609/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2021May26
	- Summary: Deep neural networks are used as a prescreening tool to automatically classify and assign individualized absorption models in population pharmacokinetic analyses.

- **[Population pharmacokinetic model selection assisted by machine learning.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34708337/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Neural networks, Model selection
	- Published: 2022Apr
	- Summary: This paper compares machine learning methods (genetic algorithms and neural networks) to classical approaches for automated selection of population pharmacokinetic models.

- **[Convolutional neural network for accelerating the computation of the extended Tofts model in dynamic contrast-enhanced magnetic resonance imaging.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33382513/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2021Jun
	- Summary: A CNN is developed to accelerate computation of the extended Tofts pharmacokinetic model for DCE-MRI analysis, replacing time-consuming nonlinear fitting methods.

- **[Fully Automated Deep Learning Enabled Miniature Mass Spectrometry System for Psychoactive Therapeutic Drug Monitoring.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40405757/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2025Aug
	- Summary: Deep learning U-net algorithm automates peak area recognition in miniature mass spectrometry for psychoactive drug monitoring with >98% identification accuracy.

- **[A pharmacokinetic-pharmacodynamic model based on the SSA-1DCNN-Attention network and the semicompartment method.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37018446/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2024Nov
	- Summary: The paper develops a PK-PD model using 1D CNN with attention mechanism and sparrow search algorithm optimization to predict drug concentrations and model concentration-effect relationships.

- **[Introduction of an artificial neural network-based method for concentration-time predictions.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35582964/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Deep learning, Time-series modeling
	- Published: 2022Jun
	- Summary: This paper introduces artificial neural networks for predicting concentration-time curves in pharmacokinetics, using simulated data and transfer learning to supplement traditional PK modeling.

- **[AI-Aristotle: A physics-informed framework for systems biology gray-box identification.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38470870/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Mechanism-informed machine learning
	- Published: 2024Mar
	- Summary: AI-Aristotle combines physics-informed neural networks with symbolic regression for parameter estimation and missing physics identification in systems biology models.

- **[Redefining Parameter Estimation and Covariate Selection via Variational Autoencoders: One Run Is All You Need.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41186137/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Model selection
	- Published: 2025Dec
	- Summary: A VAE framework is developed for NLME modeling that automates parameter estimation and covariate selection in pharmacometrics within a single run.

- **[Opportunities for AI-based Model-informed Drug Development: A Comparative Analysis of NONMEM and AI-based Models for Population Pharmacokinetic Prediction.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41254220/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2025Nov18
	- Summary: This study compares AI/ML models including deep learning and neural ODEs against traditional NONMEM for population pharmacokinetic modeling to evaluate predictive performance improvements.

- **[Development of a genetic algorithm and NONMEM workbench for automating and improving population pharmacokinetic/pharmacodynamic model selection.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34604941/)**
	- Methodology: Model selection
	- Published: 2022Apr
	- Summary: The paper presents a genetic algorithm integrated with NONMEM workbench to automate population PK/PD model selection by finding globally optimal solutions for model components.

- **[Extraction of a vascular function for a fully automated dynamic contrast-enhanced magnetic resonance brain image processing pipeline.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34708417/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2022Mar
	- Summary: Deep learning model extracts vascular function from DCE-MR brain imaging to automate quantitative pharmacokinetic modeling by leveraging spatial and temporal information.

- **[Learning Chemotherapy Drug Action via Universal Physics-Informed Neural Networks.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40244511/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Mechanism-informed machine learning
	- Published: 2025Apr
	- Summary: The paper applies Universal Physics-Informed Neural Networks to learn unknown components of differential equations modeling chemotherapy pharmacodynamics in QSP.

- **[A machine learning approach to population pharmacokinetic modelling automation.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40745042/)**
	- Methodology: Model selection
	- Published: 2025Jul31
	- Summary: This paper presents an automated machine learning approach using optimization algorithms (pyDarwin) to streamline population pharmacokinetic model development for extravascular drugs.

## Causal inference

- **[Model-informed approach to estimate treatment effect in placebo-controlled clinical trials using an artificial intelligence-based propensity weighting methodology to account for non-specific responses to treatment.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39656323/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024Dec10
	- Summary: Uses AI-based propensity score weighting to control for non-specific treatment response confounding in placebo-controlled clinical trials for major depressive disorder.

- **[A Generative and Causal Pharmacokinetic Model for Factor VIII in Hemophilia A: A Machine Learning Framework for Continuous Model Refinement.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38372445/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Bayesian ML
	- Published: 2024Apr
	- Summary: Develops a hybrid ML-PK model using causal inference to correct for assay differences and generative modeling to simulate virtual patients and impute missing data in hemophilia A.

- **[Application of machine learning based methods in exposure-response analysis.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35275315/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2022Aug
	- Summary: This simulation study evaluates ML methods for exposure-response analysis using propensity score estimation and neural networks to improve confounding adjustment and E-R relationship estimation.

## Covariate selection / confounding adjustment

- **[Toward a Combinatorial Approach for the Prediction of IgG Half-Life and Clearance.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30232177/)**
	- Methodology: Feature selection, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2018Dec
	- Summary: Uses LASSO regression for feature selection to identify optimal combinations of biophysical parameters that predict monoclonal antibody half-life and clearance.

- **[Supervised Machine-Learning Reveals That Old and Obese People Achieve Low Dapsone Concentrations.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/28575552/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2017Aug
	- Summary: Supervised ML using multivariate adaptive regression splines for covariate selection to identify complex weight/age effects on dapsone PK parameters for personalized dosing.

- **[Machine learning advances the integration of covariates in population pharmacokinetic models: Valproic acid as an example.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36324679/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2022
	- Summary: XGBoost model with SHAP interpretation integrates covariates from population pharmacokinetic models to predict valproic acid steady-state concentrations in epileptic patients.

- **[Microdose Cocktail Study Reveals the Activity and Key Influencing Factors of OATP1B, P-Gp, BCRP, and CYP3A in End-Stage Renal Disease Patients.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39789999/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025May
	- Summary: Machine learning models were used alongside population pharmacokinetic modeling to identify key factors influencing drug transporter/enzyme activities in ESRD patients.

- **[A Systems-Based Analysis of Mono- and Combination Therapy for Carbapenem-Resistant Klebsiella pneumoniae Bloodstream Infections.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36125299/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models
	- Published: 2022Oct18
	- Summary: Random Forest was used to rank variables important for predicting 30-day mortality in carbapenem-resistant Klebsiella pneumoniae bloodstream infections treated with antimicrobial therapies.

- **[Using Machine Learning To Define the Impact of Beta-Lactam Early and Cumulative Target Attainment on Outcomes in Intensive Care Unit Patients with Hospital-Acquired and Ventilator-Associated Pneumonia.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35699444/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2022Jul19
	- Summary: Machine learning is used alongside regression analyses to evaluate the impact of beta-lactam PK/PD parameters on therapy outcomes in ICU pneumonia patients.

- **[Learning pharmacometric covariate model structures with symbolic regression networks.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37864654/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Feature selection, Model selection
	- Published: 2024Apr
	- Summary: The paper uses symbolic regression networks to automatically identify covariate model structures in pharmacokinetic modeling, demonstrated on propofol clinical data.

- **[Improving Genotype Imputation in High-Dimensional Pharmacogenomics Using Multiple Imputation: Evaluation with Machine Learning Approaches.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41407383/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Feature selection
	- Published: 2025Dec17
	- Summary: The paper develops a multiple imputation framework using machine learning approaches including random forest and penalized regression for genotype imputation and covariate selection in pharmacogenomics.

- **[Go beyond the limits of genetic algorithm in daily covariate selection practice.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37493851/)**
	- Methodology: Feature selection
	- Published: 2024Apr
	- Summary: The paper proposes an improved genetic algorithm for covariate selection in population pharmacokinetic modeling to overcome limitations of stepwise covariate models.

- **[Application of SHAP values for inferring the optimal functional form of covariates in pharmacokinetic modeling.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38100100/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2022Aug
	- Summary: Uses SHAP values with random forest and XGBoost models to infer optimal functional forms of covariates in population pharmacokinetic modeling for hemophilia A patients.

- **[Shap-Cov: An Explainable Machine Learning Based Workflow for Rapid Covariate Identification in Population Modeling.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40601439/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2025Aug
	- Summary: The paper presents Shap-Cov, an explainable ML workflow using SHAP analysis for automated covariate identification in population PK/PD modeling with uncertainty quantification.

- **[Identification of eight genomic protective alleles for mitochondrial diabetes by Kinship-graph convolutional network.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38157301/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2024Jan
	- Summary: Uses kinship-graph convolutional networks to identify nuclear genome variants that influence clinical phenotypes in mitochondrial diabetes patients with m.3243A>G mutation.

- **[Tacrolimus in the treatment of childhood nephrotic syndrome: Machine learning detects novel biomarkers and predicts efficacy.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36521865/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2023Jan
	- Summary: Machine learning algorithms are used to identify biomarkers and develop prediction models for tacrolimus efficacy in childhood nephrotic syndrome patients.

- **[Model-informed approach to estimate treatment effect in placebo-controlled clinical trials using an artificial intelligence-based propensity weighting methodology to account for non-specific responses to treatment.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39656323/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024Dec10
	- Summary: Uses AI-based propensity score weighting to control for non-specific treatment response confounding in placebo-controlled clinical trials for major depressive disorder.

- **[Pharmacogenetic Analysis of the Model-Based Pharmacokinetics of Five Anti-HIV Drugs: How Does This Influence the Effect of Aging?](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/29205871/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2018Mar
	- Summary: Random Forest ensemble methods were used to analyze pharmacogenetic-aging interactions affecting antiretroviral drug pharmacokinetics for precision dosing in HIV patients.

- **[Contribution of machine learning to tumor growth inhibition modeling for hepatocellular carcinoma patients under Roblitinib (FGF401) drug treatment.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35728123/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2022Aug
	- Summary: Machine learning with elastic net modeling is combined with population PK/PD modeling to identify predictive factors and create a risk score for tumor growth inhibition in hepatocellular carcinoma patients.

- **[Fast screening of covariates in population models empowered by machine learning.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34019213/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Neural networks, Feature selection
	- Published: 2021Aug
	- Summary: ML methods including random forest, neural networks, and SVR are used to accelerate covariate selection in population pharmacokinetic modeling, outperforming traditional approaches.

- **[Tumor growth inhibition modeling in patients with second line biliary tract cancer and first line non-small cell lung cancer based on bintrafusp alfa trials.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38087967/)**
	- Methodology: Feature selection, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2024Jan
	- Summary: The paper uses elastic net and random forest ML approaches alongside NLME modeling for covariate evaluation in tumor dynamics modeling of cancer patients receiving immunotherapy.

- **[Utilization of Machine Learning Approaches for Drug Clearance Prediction and Population Pharmacokinetic Covariate Analysis.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40968579/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Deep learning, Tree-based models, Explainable AI, Neural networks
	- Published: 2025Sep
	- Summary: This study applies multiple ML models including CNNs and gradient boosting with SHAP-based XAI for drug clearance prediction and covariate analysis in population pharmacokinetics.

- **[Stochastic Gates for Covariate Selection in Population Pharmacokinetics Modeling.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41637692/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Feature selection
	- Published: 2026Feb
	- Summary: Neural networks with stochastic gates are used for automated covariate selection in population pharmacokinetics modeling to efficiently identify relevant covariates.

- **[Preparation of aspirin inhalable powder by ultrasound-intensified anti-solvent crystallization for pulmonary drug delivery.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40680606/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2025Sep
	- Summary: Decision Tree Regressor with Shapley Value analysis was used to elucidate the influence of critical process parameters in aspirin inhalable powder preparation.

- **[Model-based prediction of progression-free survival for combination therapies in oncology.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37300376/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2023Sep
	- Summary: Uses machine learning-guided analysis to reduce unexplained variability in joint models predicting progression-free survival for oncology combination therapies.

- **[A new approach to modeling covariate effects and individualization in population pharmacokinetics-pharmacodynamics.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/16402288/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Model selection
	- Published: 2006Feb
	- Summary: The paper develops a new estimation approach for nonlinear mixed effects models in population PK/PD that uses neural networks as basis functions for nonparametric covariate modeling.

- **[Model-Based Virtual PK/PD Exploration and Machine Learning Approach to Define PK Drivers in Early Drug Discovery.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38375820/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2024Mar14
	- Summary: Uses machine learning to identify PK drivers and structural features influencing drug response through model-based virtual PK/PD exploration in early drug discovery.

- **[Modeling tumor size dynamics based on real-world electronic health records and image data in advanced melanoma patients receiving immunotherapy.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37328961/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2023Aug
	- Summary: The paper combines machine learning with pharmacometric approaches for covariate selection using radiomics features to model tumor growth dynamics in melanoma patients receiving immunotherapy.

- **[Machine-Learning Assisted Screening of Correlated Covariates: Application to Clinical Data of Desipramine.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38816519/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2024May30
	- Summary: Ridge regression is used as a machine learning screening tool to improve covariate selection efficiency in population pharmacokinetic modeling of desipramine clinical data.

- **[From chemical to mechanism-driven quality control of Glehniae Radix: spectrum effect - chemometrics-pharmacology integration decoding antioxidant-anti-inflammatory crosstalk.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40922625/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models, Feature selection
	- Published: 2025Sep25
	- Summary: Random forest algorithm is used to optimize spectrum-effect relationships and predict bioactive constituents for quality control of Glehniae Radix herbal medicine.

- **[Uncovering Population PK Covariates from VAE-Generated Latent Spaces.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41336851/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Feature selection, Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2025Jul
	- Summary: Uses VAEs and LASSO regression to identify key covariates from PK profiles for improved population pharmacokinetic modeling and personalized dosing strategies.

- **[Big Data Toolsets to Pharmacometrics: Application of Machine Learning for Time-to-Event Analysis.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/29536640/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2018May
	- Summary: This paper compares machine learning methods to Cox regression for time-to-event analysis using simulated data, showing ML outperforms in high-dimensional scenarios.

- **[Application of machine learning based methods in exposure-response analysis.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35275315/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2022Aug
	- Summary: This simulation study evaluates ML methods for exposure-response analysis using propensity score estimation and neural networks to improve confounding adjustment and E-R relationship estimation.

- **[Individualized Adherence Benchmarks for HIV Pre-Exposure Prophylaxis.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33191774/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models
	- Published: 2021Jun
	- Summary: Random forest analysis is used to rank covariates for improving pharmacokinetic modeling of tenofovir concentrations to create individualized adherence benchmarks for HIV PrEP.

- **[AI-NLME: A New Artificial Intelligence-Driven Nonlinear Mixed Effect Modeling Approach for Analyzing Longitudinal Data in Randomized Placebo-Controlled Clinical Trials.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40898887/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2025Sep
	- Summary: Proposes AI-NLME approach using artificial neural networks to estimate probability of non-specific treatment response for controlling confounding effects in clinical trials.

- **[A Machine Learning-Based Identification of Genes Affecting the Pharmacokinetics of Tacrolimus Using the DMETTM Plus Platform.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32260456/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Feature selection
	- Published: 2020Apr04
	- Summary: Machine learning algorithms including decision trees, random forest, and LASSO regression were used to identify genetic variants affecting tacrolimus pharmacokinetics for individualized dosing.

- **[Redefining Parameter Estimation and Covariate Selection via Variational Autoencoders: One Run Is All You Need.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41186137/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Model selection
	- Published: 2025Dec
	- Summary: A VAE framework is developed for NLME modeling that automates parameter estimation and covariate selection in pharmacometrics within a single run.

- **[Artificial Neural Network Analysis of Determinants of Tacrolimus Pharmacokinetics in Liver Transplant Recipients.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37559252/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2024May
	- Summary: This paper uses artificial neural networks to analyze factors influencing tacrolimus pharmacokinetics in liver transplant recipients to optimize individualized dosing protocols.

- **[Identifying biophysical assays and in silico properties that enrich for slow clearance in clinical-stage therapeutic antibodies.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34116620/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models, Feature selection
	- Published: 2021Jan-Dec
	- Summary: Uses Random Forest classifier and statistical analysis to identify biophysical properties that influence human clearance of therapeutic antibodies for early optimization.

- **[Associations of novel genetic variations in the folate-related and ARID5B genes with the pharmacokinetics and toxicity of high-dose methotrexate in paediatric acute lymphoblastic leukaemia.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/24712521/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models, Feature selection
	- Published: 2014Aug
	- Summary: Random forest and regression trees were used for variable selection to identify genetic variations associated with methotrexate pharmacokinetics and toxicity in pediatric patients.

- **[Development of PBPK Population Model for End-Stage Renal Disease Patients to Inform OATP1B-, BCRP-, P-gp-, and CYP3A4-Mediated Drug Disposition with Individual Influencing Factors.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40871097/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Aug20
	- Summary: Machine learning was employed to identify factors influencing individual clearance in a PBPK population model for end-stage renal disease patients to enable precision dosing.

- **[Machine learning-guided covariate selection for time-to-event models developed from a small sample of real-world patients receiving bevacizumab treatment.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35851999/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Feature selection, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2022Oct
	- Summary: Machine learning methods including LASSO, survival trees, and random forests are used to select important covariates for time-to-event models predicting survival outcomes in cancer patients.

## Data imputation

- **[Improving Genotype Imputation in High-Dimensional Pharmacogenomics Using Multiple Imputation: Evaluation with Machine Learning Approaches.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41407383/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Feature selection
	- Published: 2025Dec17
	- Summary: The paper develops a multiple imputation framework using machine learning approaches including random forest and penalized regression for genotype imputation and covariate selection in pharmacogenomics.

- **[A Generative and Causal Pharmacokinetic Model for Factor VIII in Hemophilia A: A Machine Learning Framework for Continuous Model Refinement.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38372445/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Bayesian ML
	- Published: 2024Apr
	- Summary: Develops a hybrid ML-PK model using causal inference to correct for assay differences and generative modeling to simulate virtual patients and impute missing data in hemophilia A.

- **[Development of Novel Methods for QSAR Modeling by Machine Learning Repeatedly: A Case Study on Drug Distribution to Each Tissue.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38639496/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models, Feature selection
	- Published: 2024May13
	- Summary: The paper develops a novel QSAR method using random forest models to predict tissue-to-plasma partition coefficients from incomplete datasets by imputing missing values.

- **[MMPK: A Multimodal Deep Learning Framework to Predict Human Oral Pharmacokinetic Parameters.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40741939/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Aug14
	- Summary: MMPK uses multimodal deep learning to predict human oral pharmacokinetic parameters by integrating molecular graphs, substructures, and SMILES sequences.

- **[Large-Scale Data Mining of Rapid Residue Detection Assay Data From HTML and PDF Documents: Improving Data Access and Visualization for Veterinarians.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34368270/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2021
	- Summary: The paper uses AI/ML for data mining and extraction of rapid residue detection assay data from HTML and PDF documents to improve data access for veterinarians.

- **[Evaluation of machine learning methods for covariate data imputation in pharmacometrics.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36346135/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models, Neural networks
	- Published: 2022Dec
	- Summary: This paper evaluates machine learning methods (random forest and artificial neural networks) for imputing missing covariate data in pharmacometric analyses.

- **[Generative models for age, race/ethnicity, and disease state dependence of physiological determinants of drug dosing.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36565395/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2023Apr
	- Summary: GANs are used to generate synthetic physiological data that captures complex inter-relationships among patient factors affecting drug dosing across age, race, and disease states.

- **[Subsampled brain MRI reconstruction by generative adversarial neural networks.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32593933/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2020Oct
	- Summary: Uses generative adversarial networks to reconstruct missing k-space samples in subsampled brain MRI scans for accelerated imaging acquisition.

- **[Variational autoencoders for generative modeling of drug dosing determinants in renal, hepatic, metabolic, and cardiac disease states.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38949489/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2024Jul
	- Summary: This study uses variational autoencoders to generate synthetic data for physiological determinants of drug dosing across multiple disease states for precision medicine applications.

- **[Modeling cannabinoids from a large-scale sample of Cannabis sativa chemotypes.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32870907/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2020
	- Summary: Machine learning techniques are used to impute missing cannabinoid chemotype data and cluster Cannabis cultivars into groups based on chemical similarity.

- **[Generative models for synthetic data generation: application to pharmacokinetic/pharmacodynamic data.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39192091/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2024Dec
	- Summary: This paper benchmarks deep learning generative models (GANs, TimeGAN, PAR) for generating synthetic pharmacokinetic/pharmacodynamic patient data to enable data access and augmentation.

- **[Predicting Total Drug Clearance and Volumes of Distribution Using the Machine Learning-Mediated Multimodal Method through the Imputation of Various Nonclinical Data.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35993595/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2022Sep12
	- Summary: Machine learning methods with missing-value imputation and feature selection are used to predict human drug clearance and volume of distribution from nonclinical data.

## Discovery of subpatient groups

- **[Single-cell peripheral immunoprofiling of Alzheimer's and Parkinson's diseases.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33239300/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2020Nov
	- Summary: Machine learning models were developed to predict Alzheimer's and Parkinson's diseases from single-cell immune profiling data to identify disease-specific biomarkers.

- **[Machine learning-guided clinical pharmacist interventions improve treatment outcomes in tuberculosis patients: a precision medicine approach.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41487272/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2025
	- Summary: Machine learning techniques are used to identify TB patient subtypes and optimize clinical pharmacist interventions for precision medicine treatment approaches.

- **[Machine learning reveals that Mycobacterium tuberculosis genotypes and anatomic disease site impacts drug resistance and disease transmission among patients with proven extra-pulmonary tuberculosis.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32736602/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2020Jul31
	- Summary: Machine learning is used to analyze how Mycobacterium tuberculosis genotypes and anatomic disease sites impact drug resistance and transmission patterns in extrapulmonary TB patients.

- **[Application of Machine Learning to Assess Interindividual Variability in Rapid-Acting Insulin Responses After Subcutaneous Injection in People With Type 1 Diabetes.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35568422/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2022Apr
	- Summary: Unsupervised machine learning is applied to identify subgroups of type 1 diabetes patients based on interindividual differences in rapid-acting insulin responses and vascular health parameters.

- **[Clustering of critically ill patients using an individualized learning approach enables dose optimization of mobilization in the ICU.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36597110/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2023Jan03
	- Summary: AI-based clustering is used to identify subgroups of ICU patients and provide individualized mobilization dose recommendations to optimize treatment outcomes.

- **[Molecular models of multiple sclerosis severity identify heterogeneity of pathogenic mechanisms.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36509784/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2022Dec12
	- Summary: Machine learning models using CSF biomarkers predict disability accumulation speed in MS patients and uncover molecular heterogeneity of pathogenic mechanisms.

- **[Learning pharmacokinetic models for in vivo glucocorticoid activation.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30048717/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning, Bayesian ML, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2018Oct14
	- Summary: A latent variable mixture model combines ML clustering with pharmacokinetic modeling to discover subpopulations with different glucocorticoid conversion patterns.

- **[Clustering plasma concentration-time curves: applications of unsupervised learning in pharmacogenomics.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38888431/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning, Time-series modeling
	- Published: 2025
	- Summary: This paper applies unsupervised clustering methods to group similar plasma concentration-time curves for pharmacogenomic analysis and patient subgroup discovery.

- **[Virtual patients inspired by multiomics predict the efficacy of an anti-IFNα mAb in cutaneous lupus.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39925417/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning, Supervised learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2025Feb21
	- Summary: Uses multiomics clustering to identify patient subgroups, then applies ML with mechanistic modeling to predict anti-IFNα antibody treatment responses in virtual lupus patients.

- **[Network-based disease fingerprinting with neuroinflammation PET imaging.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41282214/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2025Oct21
	- Summary: Machine learning classifiers are applied to network-based TSPO PET imaging data to identify disease-specific neuroinflammatory patterns and classify different neurological conditions.

- **[DCE-MRI defined subvolumes of a brain metastatic lesion by principle component analysis and fuzzy-c-means clustering for response assessment of radiation therapy.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/24387500/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2014Jan
	- Summary: Uses PCA and fuzzy c-means clustering to analyze DCE-MRI data for assessing brain metastases response to radiation therapy through subvolume identification.

- **[Population Pharmacokinetic Modelling of the Complex Release Kinetics of Octreotide LAR: Defining Sub-Populations by Cluster Analysis.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34683871/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2021Sep28
	- Summary: Uses cluster analysis to identify subpopulations with different absorption patterns in population pharmacokinetic modeling of octreotide LAR formulation.

- **[Understanding virtual patients efficiently and rigorously by combining machine learning with dynamical modelling.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34985622/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2022Feb
	- Summary: The paper combines machine learning with bifurcation analysis to efficiently analyze virtual patient populations in quantitative systems pharmacology modeling.

- **[Effects of motion correction, sampling rate and parametric modelling in dynamic contrast enhanced MRI of the temporomandibular joint in children affected with juvenile idiopathic arthritis.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33359424/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2021Apr
	- Summary: Uses k-means clustering and random forest classification to analyze DCE-MRI data for optimizing diagnosis of juvenile idiopathic arthritis in children's temporomandibular joints.

- **[Molecular features of human pathological tau distinguish tauopathy-associated dementias.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41616780/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2026Feb05
	- Summary: Uses unsupervised and supervised ML to identify distinct molecular features of pathological tau protein for disease stratification and biomarker discovery across tauopathies.

- **[Novel clinical phenotypes, drug categorization, and outcome prediction in drug-induced cholestasis: Analysis of a database of 432 patients developed by literature review and machine learning support.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38574623/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2024May
	- Summary: Machine learning is used to stratify drug-induced cholestasis patients, categorize drugs, and develop prognostic models to identify high-risk patients and concerning drugs.

- **[Cluster-Based Toxicity Estimation of Osteoradionecrosis via Unsupervised Machine Learning: Moving Beyond Single Dose-Parameter Normal Tissue Complication Probability by Using Whole Dose-Volume Histograms for Cohort Risk Stratification.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37034700/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2023Mar29
	- Summary: Uses unsupervised machine learning to develop cluster-based models for predicting osteoradionecrosis toxicity risk by analyzing whole dose-volume histograms for patient stratification.

- **[Unraveling the impact of therapeutic drug monitoring via machine learning for patients with sepsis.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39127039/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2024Aug20
	- Summary: Machine learning algorithms identify informative features to distinguish healthy/sick states and demonstrate TDM's positive impact on sepsis patient recovery through data-driven analysis.

- **[Modeling cannabinoids from a large-scale sample of Cannabis sativa chemotypes.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32870907/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2020
	- Summary: Machine learning techniques are used to impute missing cannabinoid chemotype data and cluster Cannabis cultivars into groups based on chemical similarity.

## Disease progression modeling

- **[Data-Driven Discovery of Mathematical and Physical Relations in Oncology Data Using Human-Understandable Machine Learning.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34901835/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2021
	- Summary: The paper introduces a neural network system that discovers human-understandable mathematical relations in oncology data to model cancer progression and tumor-host-drug interactions.

- **[Deep reinforcement learning identifies personalized intermittent androgen deprivation therapy for prostate cancer.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38493345/)**
	- Methodology: Reinforcement learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2024Jan22
	- Summary: Deep reinforcement learning is used to develop personalized intermittent androgen deprivation therapy for prostate cancer by learning patient-specific tumor dynamics and optimizing treatment policies.

- **[Building virtual patients using simulation-based inference.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40809131/)**
	- Methodology: Bayesian ML, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2024
	- Summary: Uses simulation-based inference (SBI) to generate virtual patients from individual patient data for QSP models, demonstrated on rheumatoid arthritis treatment response prediction.

- **[Quantifying Intratumoral Heterogeneity and Immunoarchitecture Generated In-Silico by a Spatial Quantitative Systems Pharmacology Model.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37345087/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2023May13
	- Summary: Uses hybrid QSP-ABM model with spatial metrics to quantify intratumoral heterogeneity and classify tumor microenvironment immunoarchitecture for anti-PD-1 therapy response.

- **[Contribution of machine learning to tumor growth inhibition modeling for hepatocellular carcinoma patients under Roblitinib (FGF401) drug treatment.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35728123/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2022Aug
	- Summary: Machine learning with elastic net modeling is combined with population PK/PD modeling to identify predictive factors and create a risk score for tumor growth inhibition in hepatocellular carcinoma patients.

- **[Riemannian Nonlinear Mixed Effects Models: Analyzing Longitudinal Deformations in Neuroimaging.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/29430166/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Bayesian ML
	- Published: 2017Jul
	- Summary: Develops Riemannian nonlinear mixed effects models using statistical ML for longitudinal analysis of manifold-valued neuroimaging data to model brain changes over time.

- **[Tumor growth inhibition modeling in patients with second line biliary tract cancer and first line non-small cell lung cancer based on bintrafusp alfa trials.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38087967/)**
	- Methodology: Feature selection, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2024Jan
	- Summary: The paper uses elastic net and random forest ML approaches alongside NLME modeling for covariate evaluation in tumor dynamics modeling of cancer patients receiving immunotherapy.

- **[Harnessing machine learning and multi-omics to explore tumor evolutionary characteristics and the role of AMOTL1 in prostate cancer.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39643184/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2025Jan
	- Summary: Machine learning integrated with multi-omics data to develop TECPI for predicting prostate cancer progression and guiding personalized treatment decisions.

- **[Machine learning aided multiscale modelling of the HIV-1 infection in the presence of NRTI therapy.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37020854/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2023
	- Summary: Machine learning is combined with multi-strain ODE models to predict HIV-1 dynamics and optimize NRTI therapy protocols based on genotype-phenotype relationships and drug resistance patterns.

- **[Machine learning-guided, big data-enabled, biomarker-based systems pharmacology: modeling the stochasticity of natural history and disease progression.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34611796/)**
	- Methodology: Bayesian ML, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Time-series modeling
	- Published: 2022Feb
	- Summary: Uses Bayesian networks and stochastic VAR models to create hybrid ML-mechanistic systems pharmacology models for predicting biomarker dynamics and disease progression during aging.

- **[Molecular models of multiple sclerosis severity identify heterogeneity of pathogenic mechanisms.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36509784/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2022Dec12
	- Summary: Machine learning models using CSF biomarkers predict disability accumulation speed in MS patients and uncover molecular heterogeneity of pathogenic mechanisms.

- **[Predicting overall survival from tumor dynamics metrics using parametric statistical and machine learning models: application to patients with RET-altered solid tumors.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38919267/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024
	- Summary: Machine learning models are used to predict overall survival from tumor dynamics metrics in a pan-indication approach for RET-altered solid tumor patients.

- **[Neural network-longitudinal assessment of the Electronic Anti-Retroviral THerapy (EARTH) cohort to follow response to HIV-treatment.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/16779050/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Time-series modeling
	- Published: 2005
	- Summary: Uses Jordan-Elman neural networks to longitudinally model drug-virus-host interactions and predict treatment success/failure in HIV patients over 20 years of follow-up data.

- **[Model-based prediction of progression-free survival for combination therapies in oncology.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37300376/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2023Sep
	- Summary: Uses machine learning-guided analysis to reduce unexplained variability in joint models predicting progression-free survival for oncology combination therapies.

- **[Modeling tumor size dynamics based on real-world electronic health records and image data in advanced melanoma patients receiving immunotherapy.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37328961/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2023Aug
	- Summary: The paper combines machine learning with pharmacometric approaches for covariate selection using radiomics features to model tumor growth dynamics in melanoma patients receiving immunotherapy.

- **[Robust temporal knowledge inference via pathway snapshots with liquid neural network.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40349883/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Reinforcement learning, Neural networks, AI Agents, Time-series modeling
	- Published: 2025Sep
	- Summary: The paper develops liquid neural network-based AI agents for temporal knowledge inference in disease pathways and drug pharmacokinetic processes using imitation learning.

- **[DN-ODE: Data-driven neural-ODE modeling for breast cancer tumor dynamics and progression-free survivals.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39089112/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2024Sep
	- Summary: Uses Neural ODEs to model individual breast cancer tumor dynamics and progression-free survival from early clinical trial data to predict later phase outcomes.

- **[Emulation of Quantitative Systems Pharmacology models to accelerate virtual population inference in immuno-oncology.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38246229/)**
	- Methodology: Surrogate modeling, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2024Mar
	- Summary: The paper uses machine learning to create surrogate models that emulate QSP models for faster virtual population inference in immuno-oncology drug development.

- **[Machine-learning and mechanistic modeling of metastatic breast cancer after neoadjuvant treatment.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38701089/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024May
	- Summary: The paper combines mechanistic kinetics-pharmacodynamics modeling with machine learning to predict neoadjuvant treatment response and metastatic progression in breast cancer.

- **[Type 1 diabetes prevention clinical trial simulator: Case reports of model-informed drug development tool.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38961520/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models
	- Published: 2024Aug
	- Summary: The paper develops a clinical trial simulation tool for type 1 diabetes prevention using ctree machine learning algorithms to generate virtual populations for trial design optimization.

## Dose selection / optimization

- **[Automatic Segmentation Using Deep Learning to Enable Online Dose Optimization During Adaptive Radiation Therapy of Cervical Cancer.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33181248/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2021Mar15
	- Summary: Deep learning models are used for automatic segmentation to enable daily online dose optimization in adaptive radiation therapy for cervical cancer treatment.

- **[Accurate and Fast Deep Learning Dose Prediction for a Preclinical Microbeam Radiation Therapy Study Using Low-Statistics Monte Carlo Simulations.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37046798/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2023Apr04
	- Summary: Deep learning models are used to predict radiation doses in microbeam radiation therapy for preclinical studies, enabling fast and accurate dose estimation for treatment planning.

- **[Ensemble Machine Learning Model for Real-Time Valproic Acid Prediction in Epilepsy Treatment.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40456293/)**
	- Methodology: Ensemble learning, Tree-based models, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2025Jun02
	- Summary: Develops ensemble ML model using gradient boosting algorithms with SHAP interpretation to predict valproic acid concentrations for optimized epilepsy treatment dosing.

- **[Artificial intelligence-guided precision treatment of chronic kidney disease-mineral bone disorder.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35920131/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Reinforcement learning
	- Published: 2022Oct
	- Summary: The paper uses reinforcement learning combined with quantitative systems pharmacology modeling to optimize dosing of multiple medications for chronic kidney disease-mineral bone disorder treatment.

- **[The Application of the Design of Experiments and Artificial Neural Networks in the Development of a Fast and Straightforward HPLC-UV Method for Fluconazole Determination in Hemato-Oncologic Pediatric Patients and Its Adaptation to Therapeutic Drug Monitoring.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39770521/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2024Dec12
	- Summary: This paper uses artificial neural networks (multilayer perceptron) alongside design of experiments to optimize HPLC-UV method development for fluconazole determination in pediatric patients.

- **[Clozapine Toxicity Predictor: Deep neural network model predicting clozapine toxicity and its therapeutic dose range.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39522262/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2024Dec
	- Summary: Deep neural network model predicts clozapine toxicity and therapeutic dose range using clinical data to guide treatment when therapeutic drug monitoring is unavailable.

- **[Application of data mining to predict the dosage of vancomycin as an outcome variable in a teaching hospital population.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/17176619/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2006Nov
	- Summary: This study applies supervised learning data mining techniques to predict vancomycin dosage in a hospital population for dose optimization purposes.

- **[Prediction of infliximab and anti-drug antibody concentrations in patients with inflammatory bowel disease using machine learning models with real-world data from a prospective cohort study.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41685197/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2026
	- Summary: Machine learning models were developed to predict infliximab and anti-drug antibody concentrations in IBD patients as an alternative to complex population PK models.

- **[Drug Clearance in Neonates: A Combination of Population Pharmacokinetic Modelling and Machine Learning Approaches to Improve Individual Prediction.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34041714/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2021Nov
	- Summary: The paper combines population pharmacokinetic modeling with machine learning approaches to improve individual drug clearance prediction in neonates.

- **[A machine learning approach to personalized dose adjustment of lamotrigine using noninvasive clinical parameters.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33692435/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Model selection
	- Published: 2021Mar10
	- Summary: Machine learning algorithms, particularly extra-trees regression, are used to predict lamotrigine dose-adjusted concentrations for personalized dose optimization using clinical parameters.

- **[Multi-objective Molecular Optimization for Opioid Use Disorder Treatment Using Generative Network Complex.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37396606/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Unsupervised learning, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2023Jun13
	- Summary: Deep generative models using SDE-based diffusion and autoencoders generate multi-target opioid receptor molecules with optimized ADMET properties for OUD treatment discovery.

- **[Machine learning-based prediction model for teicoplanin plasma concentrations in adults with liver disease using real-world data.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41424785/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Deep learning
	- Published: 2025
	- Summary: Machine learning and deep learning techniques are used to construct a prediction model for teicoplanin plasma concentrations in liver disease patients using real-world data.

- **[Deep compartment models: A deep learning approach for the reliable prediction of time-series data in pharmacokinetic modeling.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38100092/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Time-series modeling
	- Published: 2022Jul
	- Summary: The paper proposes deep compartment models combining neural networks with ODEs for reliable pharmacokinetic modeling and drug concentration prediction.

- **[Supervised Machine-Learning Reveals That Old and Obese People Achieve Low Dapsone Concentrations.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/28575552/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2017Aug
	- Summary: Supervised ML using multivariate adaptive regression splines for covariate selection to identify complex weight/age effects on dapsone PK parameters for personalized dosing.

- **[Machine learning advances the integration of covariates in population pharmacokinetic models: Valproic acid as an example.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36324679/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2022
	- Summary: XGBoost model with SHAP interpretation integrates covariates from population pharmacokinetic models to predict valproic acid steady-state concentrations in epileptic patients.

- **[Machine learning approach for dosage individualization of azithromycin in children with community-acquired pneumonia.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40181615/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Aug
	- Summary: Machine learning is used to predict azithromycin AUC0-24 in children with pneumonia to enable individualized dosing regimens.

- **[Development of neuro-fuzzy model to explore gene-nutrient interactions modulating warfarin dose requirement.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/27462768/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2016Aug
	- Summary: The paper develops a neuro-fuzzy model to explore gene-nutrient interactions affecting warfarin dosing requirements, combining neural networks with fuzzy logic for personalized dose optimization.

- **[An Ensemble Model for Prediction of Vancomycin Trough Concentrations in Pediatric Patients.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33883878/)**
	- Methodology: Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2021
	- Summary: This study develops an ensemble machine learning model to predict vancomycin trough concentrations in pediatric patients for improved dosing optimization.

- **[Dose-time-concentration prediction method based on GRU-TCN with temporal-channel attention.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41201288/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Time-series modeling
	- Published: 2025Nov07
	- Summary: The paper develops a GRU-TCN deep learning model with attention mechanisms for predicting drug concentration-time profiles to support precision dosing decisions.
