	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2025Oct20
	- Summary: The paper combines population pharmacokinetic modeling with machine learning techniques to optimize mycophenolate dosing for renal transplant patients.

- **[Development and validation of a machine learning model for individualised meropenem dosing in CRRT patients.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41792368/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Explainable AI, Surrogate modeling
	- Published: 2026Mar06
	- Summary: Machine learning models were developed to predict optimal meropenem dosing in CRRT patients, using gradient boosting and SHAP analysis to outperform traditional pharmacokinetic models.

- **[Mapping the dose-effect relationship of orbofiban from sparse data with an artificial neural network.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/16200613/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2005Nov
	- Summary: Neural networks are used to model the dose-effect relationship of orbofiban on platelet aggregation inhibition from sparse clinical data without requiring drug concentrations.

- **[Artificial neural network-based pharmacogenomic algorithm for warfarin dose optimization.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/26666467/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2016
	- Summary: The paper develops an artificial neural network-based pharmacogenomic algorithm to optimize warfarin dosing for safer and more effective treatment.

- **[Valproic acid monitoring: Serum prediction using a machine learning framework from multicenter real-world data.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37992772/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024Feb15
	- Summary: Machine learning is used to predict serum valproic acid concentrations from multicenter real-world data for therapeutic drug monitoring applications.

- **[The development of a deep reinforcement learning network for dose-volume-constrained treatment planning in prostate cancer intensity modulated radiotherapy.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35523130/)**
	- Methodology: Reinforcement learning, Deep learning
	- Published: 2022Jun03
	- Summary: Deep reinforcement learning is used to develop a virtual treatment planner that automatically adjusts dose-volume constraints for prostate cancer radiotherapy treatment planning.

- **[Generative models for age, race/ethnicity, and disease state dependence of physiological determinants of drug dosing.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36565395/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2023Apr
	- Summary: GANs are used to generate synthetic physiological data that captures complex inter-relationships among patient factors affecting drug dosing across age, race, and disease states.

- **[TuNa-AI: A Hybrid Kernel Machine To Design Tunable Nanoparticles for Drug Delivery.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40934473/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2025Sep23
	- Summary: Develops a hybrid kernel machine combining molecular features and compositional data to predict nanoparticle formulation outcomes and optimize drug delivery systems.

- **[Combined Experimental and Computational Approaches for Ternary Solid Dispersions to Enhance the Oral Bioavailability of Penfluridol.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41471061/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models
	- Published: 2025Nov30
	- Summary: Random forest regression was used to optimize the formulation composition of ternary solid dispersions to enhance penfluridol's oral bioavailability.

- **[Optimizing warfarin dosing using deep reinforcement learning.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36494060/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Reinforcement learning
	- Published: 2023Jan
	- Summary: Deep reinforcement learning is used to optimize individualized warfarin dosing by training on simulated virtual patients from PK/PD models to overcome small sample sizes.

- **[Prediction of teicoplanin plasma concentration in critically ill patients: a combination of machine learning and population pharmacokinetics.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39207798/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2024Nov04
	- Summary: The paper combines machine learning with population pharmacokinetics to predict teicoplanin plasma concentrations for personalized dosing in critically ill patients.

- **[Example-based support vector machine for drug concentration analysis.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/22254273/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2011
	- Summary: The paper uses Support Vector Machines to predict drug concentrations for dose individualization, comparing SVM performance to traditional pharmacokinetic models.

- **[Mycophenolic Acid Exposure Prediction Using Machine Learning.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33624286/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models
	- Published: 2021Aug
	- Summary: This paper uses extreme gradient boosting (XGBoost) machine learning models to predict mycophenolic acid AUC exposure in organ transplant patients for therapeutic drug monitoring.

- **[Enhancing targeted tumor treatment: A novel fuzzy logic framework for precision drug delivery strategy selection.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39146841/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024Sep
	- Summary: The paper introduces a fuzzy logic framework for selecting optimal drug delivery strategies in tumor treatment, enhancing precision medicine approaches.

- **[Computational design of clinical trials using a combination of simulation and the genetic algorithm.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36793239/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2023Apr
	- Summary: This paper uses genetic algorithms to computationally optimize clinical trial designs, specifically blood sampling schedules for bioequivalence studies and dose group allocation.

- **[A Machine Learning-Based Identification of Genes Affecting the Pharmacokinetics of Tacrolimus Using the DMETTM Plus Platform.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32260456/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Feature selection
	- Published: 2020Apr04
	- Summary: Machine learning algorithms including decision trees, random forest, and LASSO regression were used to identify genetic variants affecting tacrolimus pharmacokinetics for individualized dosing.

- **[Variational autoencoders for generative modeling of drug dosing determinants in renal, hepatic, metabolic, and cardiac disease states.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38949489/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2024Jul
	- Summary: This study uses variational autoencoders to generate synthetic data for physiological determinants of drug dosing across multiple disease states for precision medicine applications.

- **[DOD-BART: machine learning-based dose optimization design incorporating patient-level prognostic factors via Bayesian additive regression trees.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39611660/)**
	- Methodology: Bayesian ML, Tree-based models
	- Published: 2024Nov29
	- Summary: The paper proposes DOD-BART, a seamless phase I/II clinical trial design using Bayesian Additive Regression Trees to optimize dose selection by incorporating patient-level prognostic factors.

- **[Beyond Algorithms: Machine Learning and Clinical Determinants of Voriconazole Plasma Levels in Therapeutic Drug Monitoring.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41288921/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2026Jan
	- Summary: Machine learning is used to predict subtherapeutic or supratherapeutic voriconazole plasma levels for therapeutic drug monitoring optimization.

- **[Machine-learning model to predict the tacrolimus concentration and suggest optimal dose in liver transplantation recipients: a multicenter retrospective cohort study.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39198694/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Tree-based models, Time-series modeling, Neural networks
	- Published: 2024Aug28
	- Summary: LSTM and GBRT models predict tacrolimus concentrations and suggest optimal dosing in liver transplant recipients to improve therapeutic outcomes.

- **[Development of an mPBPK machine learning framework for early target pharmacology assessment of biotherapeutics.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39905178/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2025Feb04
	- Summary: The paper develops a machine learning framework combining minimal PBPK modeling with ML to optimize antibody physicochemical properties for improved target pharmacology.

- **[Development and evaluation of uncertainty quantifying machine learning models to predict piperacillin plasma concentrations in critically ill patients.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36008808/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Bayesian ML
	- Published: 2022Aug25
	- Summary: Machine learning models with uncertainty quantification are developed to predict piperacillin plasma concentrations in critically ill patients for clinical dosing guidance.

- **[Harnessing adaptive novelty for automated generation of cancer treatments.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33217377/)**
	- Methodology: Reinforcement learning, AI Agents
	- Published: 2021Jan
	- Summary: The paper presents an AI agent-based model using adaptive novelty and machine learning to automatically generate and optimize nanoparticle cancer treatments for heterogeneous tumors.

- **[Continuous action deep reinforcement learning for propofol dosing during general anesthesia.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34998516/)**
	- Methodology: Reinforcement learning, Deep learning
	- Published: 2022Jan
	- Summary: The paper uses deep reinforcement learning to automate propofol dosing during general anesthesia for precise control of patient unconsciousness levels.

- **[Insulin dose optimization using an automated artificial intelligence-based decision support system in youths with type 1 diabetes.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32908282/)**
	- Methodology: AI Agents
	- Published: 2020Sep
	- Summary: An AI-based decision support system was tested for automated insulin dose optimization in youth with type 1 diabetes, showing non-inferiority to physician-guided adjustments.

- **[Artificial Neural Network Analysis of Determinants of Tacrolimus Pharmacokinetics in Liver Transplant Recipients.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37559252/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2024May
	- Summary: This paper uses artificial neural networks to analyze factors influencing tacrolimus pharmacokinetics in liver transplant recipients to optimize individualized dosing protocols.

- **[Targeting the glabellar frown lines with OnabotulinumtoxinA: In-silico evidence supporting concentrated, low-volume injection protocols.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40907830/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2025Nov
	- Summary: Uses machine learning for off-target risk prediction integrated with mechanistic PK/PD modeling to optimize BoNT-A dosing protocols for glabellar frown line treatment.

- **[AI-driven ANN and RSM-CCD integrated optimization of cinnarizine-domperidone bilayer tablet: In-vitro evaluation and in-silico PBPK modeling using GastroPlus®.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41546579/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Surrogate modeling
	- Published: 2026Feb
	- Summary: The paper uses artificial neural networks (ANN) integrated with response surface methodology to optimize cinnarizine-domperidone bilayer tablet formulation with PBPK modeling validation.

- **[Optimizing methadone dose adjustment in patients with opioid use disorder.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38260800/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2023
	- Summary: This study uses mixed modeling and machine learning to optimize methadone dose adjustments for patients with opioid use disorder.

- **[Application of Machine Learning Classification to Improve the Performance of Vancomycin Therapeutic Drug Monitoring.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35631610/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Model selection
	- Published: 2022May09
	- Summary: Machine learning classifier is developed to select optimal vancomycin PK models for therapeutic drug monitoring, improving Bayesian estimation performance in individual patients.

- **[A Comparison of AI and Population PK Models to Predict the Concentrations of Antiepileptic Drugs Using Therapeutic Drug Monitoring Records.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41127895/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Deep learning, Tree-based models, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2025Oct
	- Summary: This study compares AI models (including ensemble and deep learning methods) with population PK models to predict antiepileptic drug concentrations using TDM data.

- **[A machine learning model for predicting blood concentration of quetiapine in patients with schizophrenia and depression based on real-world data.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37005382/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2023Sep
	- Summary: Machine learning techniques are used to predict quetiapine blood concentrations in schizophrenia and depression patients using real-world data to assist clinical dosing decisions.

- **[Deep learning based linear energy transfer calculation for proton therapy.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38714191/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2024May30
	- Summary: Deep learning model using 3D Cascaded UNet to rapidly calculate linear energy transfer for proton therapy dose optimization, replacing slow Monte Carlo simulations.

- **[Applying machine learning to the pharmacokinetic modeling of cyclosporine in adult renal transplant recipients: a multi-method comparison.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36386138/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2022
	- Summary: This study compares seven machine learning algorithms with population pharmacokinetic models for predicting cyclosporine concentrations to guide precision dosing in renal transplant patients.

- **[Using machine learning to optimize antibiotic combinations: dosing strategies for meropenem and polymyxin B against carbapenem-resistant Acinetobacter baumannii.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32061797/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2020Sep
	- Summary: Uses genetic algorithm with mechanism-based mathematical models to optimize dosing strategies for meropenem and polymyxin B combinations against resistant bacteria.

- **[Optimal dynamic regimens with artificial intelligence: The case of temozolomide.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/29944669/)**
	- Methodology: Reinforcement learning
	- Published: 2018
	- Summary: Uses Monte-Carlo tree search algorithms to optimize temozolomide dosing regimens by minimizing tumor size while maintaining acceptable toxicity levels through PK/PD modeling.

- **[Tacrolimus Exposure Prediction Using Machine Learning.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33253425/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models
	- Published: 2021Aug
	- Summary: XGBoost machine learning models are developed to predict tacrolimus area-under-curve exposure in organ transplant patients for dose optimization.

- **[Machine Learning-Based Algorithm for Tacrolimus Dose Optimization in Hospitalized Kidney Transplant Patients.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41374329/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Deep learning, Neural networks, Ensemble learning, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2025Nov21
	- Summary: ML models (XGBoost, CatBoost, LightGBM, MLP) with ensemble approach predict tacrolimus concentrations for precision dosing optimization in kidney transplant patients.

- **[Potential of a machine-learning model for dose optimization in CT quality assurance.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30783785/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2019Jul
	- Summary: Machine learning model is used to detect chest CT examinations with dose optimization potential for quality assurance in medical imaging.

- **[Predicting Serum Levels of Lithium-Treated Patients: A Supervised Machine Learning Approach.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34829787/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2021Oct28
	- Summary: Machine learning models predict lithium serum concentrations using real-world clinical data to improve therapeutic drug monitoring and dosing decisions.

- **[Improvement of accumulated dose distribution in combined cervical cancer radiotherapy with deep learning-based dose prediction.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39040460/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2024
	- Summary: Deep learning is used to predict and improve accumulated dose distribution in combined cervical cancer radiotherapy treatment planning.

- **[Reinforcement Learning and PK-PD Models Integration to Personalize the Adaptive Dosing Protocol of Erdafitinib in Patients with Metastatic Urothelial Carcinoma.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38339803/)**
	- Methodology: Reinforcement learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2024Apr
	- Summary: The paper integrates PK-PD models with Q-learning reinforcement learning to personalize erdafitinib dosing protocols for metastatic urothelial carcinoma patients.

- **[Simulating clinical trials for model-informed precision dosing: using warfarin treatment as a use case.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37927586/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Reinforcement learning
	- Published: 2023
	- Summary: The paper develops a simulation framework to compare AI/ML methods (neural networks, deep RL) with PKPD modeling for model-informed precision dosing using warfarin as a use case.

- **[Prediction of vancomycin plasma concentration in elderly patients based on multi-algorithm mining combined with population pharmacokinetics.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39511378/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Feature selection, Ensemble learning, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2024Nov08
	- Summary: This study develops an ensemble ML model combining SVR, LightGBM, and CatBoost with popPK parameters to predict vancomycin plasma concentrations in elderly patients for personalized dosing.

- **[Use of Machine Learning for Dosage Individualization of Vancomycin in Neonates.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37300630/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2023Aug
	- Summary: Machine learning is used to predict vancomycin treatment targets (trough concentration and AUC) to calculate optimal individualized dosing regimens for neonates.

## Drug repurposing

- **[Attention Mechanism-Based Graph Neural Network Model for Effective Activity Prediction of SARS-CoV-2 Main Protease Inhibitors: Application to Drug Repurposing as Potential COVID-19 Therapy.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37960886/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2023Nov27
	- Summary: Graph neural networks with attention mechanisms are used to predict SARS-CoV-2 main protease inhibitor activity for COVID-19 drug repurposing from existing drug databases.

- **[Decoding GuaB: Machine Learning-Powered Discovery of Enzyme Inhibitors Against the Superbug Acinetobacter baumannii.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41471330/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Dec02
	- Summary: Machine learning models based on chemical fingerprints were used for virtual screening to discover enzyme inhibitors against multidrug-resistant Acinetobacter baumannii.

- **[Computational determination of toxicity risks associated with a selection of approved drugs having demonstrated activity against COVID-19.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34674775/)**
	- Published: 2021Oct21
	- Summary: Computational methods are used to determine toxicity risks of approved drugs with COVID-19 activity for drug repurposing purposes.

- **[Multi-objective Molecular Optimization for Opioid Use Disorder Treatment Using Generative Network Complex.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37396606/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Unsupervised learning, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2023Jun13
	- Summary: Deep generative models using SDE-based diffusion and autoencoders generate multi-target opioid receptor molecules with optimized ADMET properties for OUD treatment discovery.

- **[Ai derivation and exploration of antibiotic class spaces.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41546122/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2026Jan16
	- Summary: AI methods are used to systematically explore and generate compounds within antibiotic chemical spaces for automated discovery of new antibiotic candidates.

- **[In silico identification of novel heterocyclic compounds combats Alzheimer's disease through inhibition of butyrylcholinesterase enzymatic activity.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37723904/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Bayesian ML, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2024
	- Summary: The paper uses artificial neural networks, Bayesian model averaging, and multiple regression models in QSAR studies to identify heterocyclic compounds for Alzheimer's disease treatment.

- **[Integrated AI and machine learning pipeline identifies novel WEE1 kinase inhibitors for targeted cancer therapy.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40106128/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2025Aug
	- Summary: AI-driven pipeline using MORLD platform, UMAP dimensionality reduction, and K-means clustering to identify novel WEE1 kinase inhibitors for targeted cancer therapy.

- **[AI-driven de novo design of BRAF inhibitors with enhanced binding affinity and optimized drug-likeness.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41497266/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2026
	- Summary: AI-driven de novo design uses deep learning to create novel BRAF inhibitors with improved binding affinity and drug-likeness properties for cancer treatment.

- **[Multi-omics pan-cancer profiling of CDK2 and in silico identification of plant-derived inhibitors using machine learning approaches.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41058670/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Oct02
	- Summary: Machine learning approaches are used to screen and identify plant-derived CDK2 inhibitors for cancer treatment through cheminformatics and predictive modeling.

- **[Identification of promising inhibitory heterocyclic compounds against acetylcholinesterase using QSAR, ADMET, biological activity, and molecular docking.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37119698/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Neural networks, Bayesian ML
	- Published: 2023Jun
	- Summary: The paper uses multiple AI/ML methods including artificial neural networks and Bayesian modeling to develop QSAR models for predicting acetylcholinesterase inhibition activity.

- **[Next-generation AI-assisted drug design against cancer: large language models meet conventional in silico methods.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41767851/)**
	- Methodology: LLM, Deep learning
	- Published: 2026
	- Summary: This study uses Large Language Models integrated with molecular docking and dynamics to design novel AXL inhibitors for cancer treatment through computational drug discovery.

- **[Fragment-based drug design coupled with AI/ML prediction enables identification of novel PARP-1 inhibitors against triple-negative breast cancer.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41724073/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2026May
	- Summary: AI/ML methods are used to predict PARP-1 inhibitory potential and anti-cancer activity for novel compounds identified through fragment-based drug design against triple-negative breast cancer.

- **[Anti-inflammatory drugs and prediction of new structures by comparative analysis.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/22946893/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2012
	- Summary: Uses hierarchical and K-means clustering analysis to classify NSAIDs and predict new molecular structures based on statistical analysis of molecular properties.

- **[Exploring the biologically relevant chemical space for drug discovery.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/24125686/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2013Nov25
	- Summary: The paper uses support vector machine (SVM) to develop prediction models for 43 biological activity categories using a novel biologically relevant spectrum descriptor for drug discovery.

- **[Ginkgetin Alleviates Inflammation and Senescence by Targeting STING.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39558862/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2025Jan
	- Summary: Uses graph convolutional networks to predict drug targets for Ginkgetin's anti-aging mechanism, identifying STING pathway as therapeutic target.

- **[Machine learning-guided identification and simulation-based validation of potent JAK3 inhibitors for cancer therapy.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41385500/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2025
	- Summary: Machine learning classifiers, particularly Random Forest, are used to virtually screen compounds and identify novel JAK3 inhibitors for cancer therapy through computational drug discovery.

- **[Selective Inhibitor Design for Kinase Homologs Using Multiobjective Monte Carlo Tree Search.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36334094/)**
	- Methodology: Reinforcement learning
	- Published: 2022Nov28
	- Summary: The paper develops a reinforcement learning-based molecular structure generator using Monte Carlo Tree Search to design selective kinase inhibitors by optimizing multiple objectives.

- **[Computational Systems Pharmacology-Target Mapping for Fentanyl-Laced Cocaine Overdose.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31257858/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2019Aug21
	- Summary: Machine learning methods are applied to map protein targets and analyze drug interactions for fentanyl-laced cocaine overdose using chemogenomics and PBPK modeling approaches.

- **[Exploring the mechanism of Stephania tetrandra S. Moore in the treatment of cisplatin resistance against ovarian cancer through integration of network pharmacology and molecular docking.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41239601/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2025Nov14
	- Summary: Machine learning algorithms were integrated to identify core targets for Stephania tetrandra treatment of cisplatin-resistant ovarian cancer through network pharmacology analysis.

- **[Identification of Novel High-Affinity Substrates of OCT1 Using Machine Learning-Guided Virtual Screening and Experimental Validation.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33606526/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2021Mar11
	- Summary: Machine learning algorithms using molecular descriptors and fingerprints predict OCT1 substrates through virtual screening, validated experimentally with 85% accuracy.

- **[Targeting the anti-apoptotic Bcl-2 family proteins: machine learning virtual screening and biological evaluation of new small molecules.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35265218/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2022
	- Summary: Machine learning virtual screening was used to identify new small molecule inhibitors targeting anti-apoptotic Bcl-2 family proteins for cancer treatment.

- **[Virtual and In Vitro Screening of Natural Products Identifies Indole and Benzene Derivatives as Inhibitors of SARS-CoV-2 Main Protease (Mpro).](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37106720/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning

- **[AI-powered literature mining reveals the therapeutic significance of GLP-1 receptor: Simulation of natural agonist candidates based on molecular dynamics.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41389576/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2026Apr
	- Summary: AI-integrated pipeline using BioBERT text mining and ML-guided ADMET profiling to identify natural GLP-1 receptor agonist candidates through virtual screening and molecular dynamics.

- **[Prospection of Peptide Inhibitors of Thrombin from Diverse Origins Using a Machine Learning Pipeline.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38002424/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning

	- Summary: A two-stage machine learning pipeline using supervised learning to identify and rank peptide sequences for their thrombin inhibitory potential from diverse peptide sources.

- **[Repurposing drugs for the human dopamine transporter through WHALES descriptors-based virtual screening and bioactivity evaluation.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40893439/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Aug
	- Summary: Machine learning methods are used for virtual screening with WHALES descriptors to identify novel dopamine transporter inhibitors for drug repurposing applications.

- **[Experimental and computational approaches for evaluating molecule interactions with equilibrative nucleoside transporters 1 and 2.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40779912/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2025Sep
	- Summary: Random forest models were built to predict ENT1/ENT2 transporter inhibition, leading to identification of FDA-approved drugs as potential repurposed inhibitors.

- **[Phytochemical drug discovery for COVID-19 using high-resolution computational docking and machine learning assisted binder prediction.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35993534/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2023Aug-Sep
	- Summary: The paper uses supervised learning for ligand-based virtual screening to predict phytochemical compounds that can bind to SARS-CoV-2 proteins for COVID-19 drug discovery.

- **[Computational Evaluation of Potential c-Abl Kinase Inhibitors for Parkinson's Disease: QSAR, Docking, Bioisosteric Replacement, ADMET, and MD Simulations.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41148549/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Dec
	- Summary: Machine learning-based QSAR modeling is used to identify and optimize potential c-Abl kinase inhibitors for Parkinson's disease treatment through computational screening and evaluation.

- **[Synergistic integration of deep learning with protein docking in cardiovascular disease treatment strategies.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38748776/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2024Sep
	- Summary: Deep learning protein language models ESM1-b and ProtT5 are used to predict interaction sites between proteins and tocopherol for cardiovascular disease treatment.

- **[DrugCentral 2021 supports drug discovery and repositioning.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33151287/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2021Jan08
	- Summary: DrugCentral 2021 incorporates REDIAL-2020, a machine learning platform for estimating anti-SARS-CoV-2 activities to support drug discovery and repositioning efforts.

- **[Identification of Antituberculars with Favorable Potency and Pharmacokinetics through Structure-Based and Ligand-Based Modeling.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39974961/)**
	- Methodology: Bayesian ML, Ensemble learning, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Feb04
	- Summary: Uses Bayesian optimization-guided docking and ensemble ML models to predict antibacterial efficacy and pharmacokinetics for tuberculosis drug discovery.

- **[Support vector machine classification-guided identification of novel monoamine oxidase-B inhibitors via structure-based modeling to treat neurodegenerative diseases.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40825424/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Deep learning, Tree-based models, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2025Sep
	- Summary: SVM classification models with molecular fingerprints were used to screen FDA-approved drugs for repurposing as novel MAO-B inhibitors to treat neurodegenerative diseases.

- **[A high-throughput structural dynamics approach for identification of potential agonists of FFAR4 for type 2 diabetes mellitus therapy.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37978906/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Jan
	- Summary: Machine learning is used alongside molecular docking and virtual screening to identify potential FFAR4 agonists for type 2 diabetes mellitus therapy from a large compound library.

- **[Probing the dark chemical matter against PDE4 for the management of psoriasis using in silico, in vitro and in vivo approach.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40095248/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Aug
	- Summary: Machine learning and AI were used for pharmacokinetics optimization in discovering novel PDE4D inhibitors from dark chemical matter for psoriasis treatment.

- **[Machine Learning-Based High-Throughput Screening, Molecular Modeling and Quantum Chemical Analysis to Investigate Mycobacterium tuberculosis MetRS Inhibitors.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39996268/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2025Jul
	- Summary: Machine learning algorithms including Random Forest, Extra Trees, and Nu-Support Vector are used in a voting classifier to screen 10 million molecules for Mtb MetRS inhibitors.

- **[Computational Screening and Cytotoxic Analysis of Beta vulgaris L. phytoconstituents as potent Dengue virus-NS5 Polymerase inhibitors.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40645353/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Unsupervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2025Oct
	- Summary: Machine learning is used for data preprocessing, gene expression analysis, and pathway enrichment to identify Beta vulgaris phytoconstituents as potential dengue virus NS5 inhibitors.

- **[Generative AI Uncovers Novel Chrebp/Txnip Axis Inhibitors with Potential Anti-inflammatory Activity.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41746845/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2026Feb26
	- Summary: Uses conditional recurrent neural networks for generative design of novel small-molecule inhibitors targeting the Chrebp/Txnip axis for anti-inflammatory therapy.

- **[Identification of Drug-Disease Associations Using a Random Walk with Restart Method and Supervised Learning.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36262874/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2022
	- Summary: The paper combines random walk with restart and supervised learning methods to identify drug-disease associations for drug repositioning using integrated networks.

- **[Biomarker discovery and drug repurposing in hepatocellular carcinoma through transcriptomics, machine learning, network pharmacology, and molecular dynamics.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41671946/)**

	- Published: 2026Jun
	- Summary: Uses machine learning classifiers (XGBoost, Random Forest, SVM, Logistic Regression) with SHAP explainability to identify HCC biomarkers and explore drug repurposing opportunities.

- **[Molecular Design in Synthetically Accessible Chemical Space via Deep Reinforcement Learning.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33403260/)**
	- Methodology: Reinforcement learning, Deep learning

	- Summary: Uses deep reinforcement learning to optimize molecular design in synthetically accessible chemical space for drug discovery with improved pharmacological properties.

- **[Machine learning identifies candidates for drug repurposing in Alzheimer's disease.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33589615/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2021Feb15
	- Summary: Machine learning framework DRIAD quantifies associations between Alzheimer's disease pathology and molecular mechanisms to identify FDA-approved drugs for repurposing.

- **[Artificial Intelligence Driven Virtual Screening and Molecular Docking Approaches Identified LIFR, BTG2, EPHX2, and PAK3 as Targets and BI-2536, AP-24534, and AZ-628 as Repurposed Drugs for PDAC.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41605175/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Deep learning, Tree-based models, Ensemble learning

- **[Elucidation of Artemisinin as a Potent GSK3β Inhibitor for Neurodegenerative Disorders via Machine Learning-Driven QSAR and Virtual Screening of Natural Compounds.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40573222/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Feature selection, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2025May31
	- Summary: Machine learning models (SVM, RF, KNN) with feature selection were used for virtual screening to identify artemisinin as a potent GSK3β inhibitor for neurodegenerative disorders.

- **[QSAR, molecular docking, MD simulations, and ADMET screening identify potential Heliotropium indicum leads against key targets in benign prostatic hyperplasia.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39569034/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024
	- Summary: Machine learning models predict pIC50 values for pyrrolizidine alkaloids against steroid 5α-reductase and androgen receptor targets for benign prostatic hyperplasia treatment.

- **[Computational identification of drug-like marine natural products as potential RNA polymerase inhibitors against Nipah virus.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36907056/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2023Jun
	- Summary: The paper uses GNINA, a deep learning-based docking software, to computationally screen marine natural products as potential RNA polymerase inhibitors against Nipah virus.

- **[Efficacy of Tilorone Dihydrochloride against Ebola Virus Infection.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/29133569/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2018Feb
	- Summary: A machine learning model previously identified tilorone as an anti-Ebola drug candidate, which this study validates through pharmacological and efficacy testing in mice.

- **[Performance of Force-Field- and Machine Learning-Based Scoring Functions in Ranking MAO-B Protein-Inhibitor Complexes in Relevance to Developing Parkinson's Therapeutics.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33081086/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2020Oct16
	- Summary: The paper compares force-field and machine learning methods for ranking MAO-B protein-inhibitor complexes, finding KNN superior for Parkinson's drug development.

- **[1-Sulfonyl-3-amino-1H-1,2,4-triazoles as Yellow Fever Virus Inhibitors: Synthesis and Structure-Activity Relationship.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38024733/)**

	- Published: 2023Nov14
	- Summary: Machine learning models were used to identify and select pyrazolesulfonamide derivatives as potential Yellow Fever Virus inhibitors for drug repurposing.

- **[A deep learning-driven discovery of berberine derivatives as novel antibacterial against multidrug-resistant Helicobacter pylori.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38972904/)**

	- Summary: A graph neural network-based deep learning model was used to predict and discover novel berberine derivatives as antibacterial agents against multidrug-resistant H. pylori.

- **[Cationic Compounds with SARS-CoV-2 Antiviral Activity and Their Interaction with Organic Cation Transporter/Multidrug and Toxin Extruder Secretory Transporters.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34253645/)**
	- Methodology: Bayesian ML
	- Published: 2021Oct
	- Summary: The paper uses Bayesian machine learning models to predict interactions between SARS-CoV-2 antiviral compounds and organic cation transporters for drug repurposing.

	- Summary: AI-assisted optimization and structure-based virtual screening were used to discover and optimize positive allosteric modulators for NMDA receptors as potential antidepressants.

	- Summary: Deep learning neural network trained on 220 compounds to identify Mangifera indica phytochemicals as potential TEM-1 β-lactamase inhibitors for antimicrobial resistance.

- **[Structure-guided discovery of food-derived GABA-T inhibitors as hunters for anti-anxiety compounds.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36382616/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2022Dec13
	- Summary: QSAR models using machine learning approaches were developed to predict food-derived compounds with anti-anxiety effects by targeting GABA-T enzyme inhibition.

- **[Neurotherapeutics across blood-brain barrier: screening of BBB-permeable and CNS-active molecules for neurodegenerative disease.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41079713/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025
	- Summary: Machine learning models are used to predict blood-brain barrier permeability and CNS activity for screening neurotherapeutic molecules in neurodegenerative disease drug discovery.

- **[Unveiling Berberine analogues as potential inhibitors of Escherichia coli FtsZ through machine learning molecular docking and molecular dynamics approach.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40287515/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Apr26
	- Summary: Machine learning is used to screen berberine analogues for drug-likeness and toxicity prediction, combined with molecular docking to identify potential FtsZ inhibitors.

- **[In-silico investigation integrated with machine learning to identify potential inhibitors targeting AKT2: Key driver of cancer cell progression and metastasis.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40305999/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning

	- Summary: Machine learning is integrated with in-silico methods to identify potential AKT2 inhibitors for cancer treatment, focusing on drug discovery and repurposing applications.

- **[DualPG-DTA: A Large Language Model-Powered Graph Neural Network Framework for Enhanced Drug-Target Affinity Prediction and Discovery of Novel CDK9 Inhibitors Exhibiting In Vivo Anti-Leukemia Activity.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41589601/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, LLM
	- Published: 2026Feb
	- Summary: DualPG-DTA uses large language models and graph neural networks to predict drug-target binding affinity, successfully identifying a novel CDK9 inhibitor for leukemia treatment.

- **[Integration of artificial intelligence and high-content screening enabled identification of drugs for long-term treatment of cerebral cavernous malformation disease.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41427327/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Dec11
	- Summary: AI integrated with high-content screening to identify existing drugs for repurposing as long-term treatments for cerebral cavernous malformation disease.

- **[A small-molecule TNIK inhibitor targets fibrosis in preclinical and clinical models.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38459338/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2025Jan
	- Summary: The paper uses AI-driven methodology including generative AI for drug discovery to identify TNIK as an anti-fibrotic target and generate a small-molecule inhibitor for IPF treatment.

- **[Breaking resistance with machine and deep learning: A computational intelligence hunt for AmvR (TetR) inhibitors in Acinetobacterbaumannii.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41442824/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Deep learning, Tree-based models, Ensemble learning

	- Summary: Machine learning models (RF, SVM, KNN, XGBoost) and CNN deep learning were used to screen natural compounds for AmvR inhibitors against multidrug-resistant Acinetobacter baumannii.

- **[Crystal engineering optimizes emodin-tetramethylpyrazine combination: From cocrystal design to in vivo anti-colitis efficacy assessment.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41323843/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning

	- Summary: Machine learning was used to assess cocrystallization propensity for rational design of an emodin-tetramethylpyrazine cocrystal to improve bioavailability for ulcerative colitis treatment.

	- Summary: Deep generative models combining SDE-based diffusion with autoencoders are used to generate and optimize druglike molecules targeting opioid receptors for OUD treatment.

- **[Circumventing glioblastoma resistance to temozolomide through optimal drug combinations designed by systems pharmacology and machine learning.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40229949/)**

	- Published: 2025Aug
	- Summary: Machine learning is used alongside systems pharmacology to design optimal drug combinations for overcoming temozolomide resistance in glioblastoma treatment.

- **[Generating 3D Binding Molecules Using Shape-Conditioned Diffusion Models with Guidance.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41255553/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2025May
	- Summary: DiffSMol uses diffusion models to generate 3D binding molecules based on ligand shapes and protein pockets for drug development applications.

- **[AI-driven peptide discovery for endometrial cancer: deep generative modeling and molecular simulation in the big data era.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41524971/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Reinforcement learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2026Jan12
	- Summary: AI-driven pipeline using deep reinforcement learning, GANs, and VAEs to generate and screen novel peptide-like molecules for endometrial cancer treatment.

- **[Determining similarities of COVID-19 - lung cancer drugs and affinity binding mode analysis by graph neural network-based GEFA method.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34877907/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning

	- Summary: Uses graph neural network-based GEFA method to identify COVID-19 drug repurposing candidates by analyzing molecular similarities and protein-drug binding affinities.

	- Summary: Uses unsupervised clustering methods (scVI) on single-nucleus RNA sequencing data to identify molecular drivers of Parkinson's disease and suggest repurposed therapeutic drugs.

- **[Discovery of novel SOS1 inhibitors using machine learning.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38665844/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models

	- Summary: Machine learning-based virtual screening using random forest to discover novel SOS1 inhibitors for RAS-driven cancers from compound libraries.

- **[Developing inhibitors of the guanosine triphosphate hydrolysis accelerating activity of Regulator of G protein Signaling-14.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40848973/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2025Oct
	- Summary: The paper uses structure-guided virtual screening, ligand docking, and deep learning-based scoring to develop inhibitors of RGS14 protein for drug discovery.

- **[Integrated machine learning and deep learning-based virtual screening framework identifies novel natural GSK-3β inhibitors for Alzheimer's disease.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40668407/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Deep learning, Tree-based models, Explainable AI

	- Summary: Integrates random forest and deep learning for virtual screening to identify natural GSK-3β inhibitors for Alzheimer's disease using SHAP for model interpretability.

- **[Predicting the effects of drugs and unveiling their mechanisms of action using an interpretable pharmacodynamic mechanism knowledge graph (IPM-KG).](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39556916/)**

	- Summary: The paper proposes an interpretable knowledge graph framework to predict drug effects and derive mechanistic pathways based on pharmacodynamic mechanisms of action.

	- Methodology: Deep learning

	- Summary: Deep generative models are used for de novo drug design to develop novel gastric proton pump inhibitors with improved binding affinity and potency.

	- Methodology: Neural networks

- **[Identification of novel class I and class IIb histone deacetylase inhibitor for Alzheimer's disease therapeutics.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32504755/)**

- **[Identification of novel Menin-MLL interaction inhibitors targeting leukemia using in-silico virtual screening and structure-based drug design approaches.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41386877/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Dec
	- Summary: The paper uses machine learning (PSICHIC model) for binding affinity prediction in virtual screening to identify novel Menin-MLL interaction inhibitors for leukemia treatment.

- **[Assessment of the Antibacterial Efficacy of Halicin against Pathogenic Bacteria.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34943692/)**
	- Published: 2021Dec02
	- Summary: This paper evaluates the antibacterial efficacy of halicin, an anti-diabetic drug repurposed as an antibiotic through AI screening, against various pathogenic bacteria.

- **[Discovery of SARS-CoV-2 papain-like protease (PLpro) inhibitors with efficacy in a murine infection model.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39213347/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning

	- Summary: Machine learning was used to discover potent SARS-CoV-2 papain-like protease inhibitors, leading to the identification of compound PF-07957472 with in vivo efficacy.

	- Summary: ML-QSAR approach using extra trees regressor and SHAP analysis to predict and identify novel ASAH1 inhibitors for glioblastoma therapy through virtual screening.

	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Aug
	- Summary: AI-driven virtual screening platform was used to discover and prioritize novel brain-penetrant Galectin-3 inhibitors for Alzheimer's disease therapy.

- **[Machine Learning, Molecular Docking, and Dynamics-Based Computational Identification of Potential Inhibitors against Lung Cancer.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38313551/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Unsupervised learning, Ensemble learning

	- Summary: Machine learning models including random forest, SVM, and XGBoost are used with molecular docking to identify potential phytomolecule inhibitors against lung cancer from natural product databases.

	- Methodology: Supervised learning

	- Summary: QSAR models were used to screen natural compounds for antibiotic activity against A. baumannii, identifying demethoxycurcumin as a promising therapeutic candidate.

- **[Computational screening of natural products as tryptophan 2,3-dioxygenase inhibitors: Insights from CNN-based QSAR, molecular docking, ADMET, and molecular dynamics simulations.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40233673/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2025Jun
	- Summary: CNN-based QSAR models and machine learning are used to computationally screen natural products as potential TDO inhibitors for Parkinson's disease treatment.

- **[Stacked ensemble learning and in-silico profiling reveal dual DPP-IV and SGLT2 inhibitors from Moringa oleifera metabolites.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41708792/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2026Feb18
	- Summary: Stacked ensemble machine learning models were developed to identify dual DPP-IV/SGLT2 inhibitors from Moringa oleifera metabolites for diabetes treatment.

- **[Discovery of novel NLRP3 inhibitors based on machine learning and physical methods.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39468648/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2024Oct28
	- Summary: Machine learning models (LightGBM, Random Forest, XGBoost) were used to screen millions of compounds for discovering novel NLRP3 inhibitors as potential therapeutics for alcohol-related liver disease.

- **[Deep Supramolecular Language Processing for Co-Crystal Prediction.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40358977/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2025Jul
	- Summary: DeepCocrystal uses deep learning to predict co-crystal formation for improving drug physicochemical properties, with explainable AI to understand decision-making processes.

- **[Repurposing Vandetanib plus Everolimus for the Treatment of ACVR1-Mutant Diffuse Intrinsic Pontine Glioma.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34551970/)**
	- Published: 2022Feb
	- Summary: AI platform was used to identify vandetanib plus everolimus combination for treating ACVR1-mutant DIPG, validated through experimental and clinical studies.

- **[Virtual Screening Based on Machine Learning Explores Mangrove Natural Products as KRASG12C Inhibitors.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35631410/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2022May08
	- Summary: Machine learning, specifically random forest, is used for virtual screening of mangrove natural products to identify potential KRASG12C inhibitors for cancer treatment.

- **[Hybrid neural network approaches to predict drug-target binding affinity for drug repurposing: screening for potential leads for Alzheimer's disease.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37441162/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2023
	- Summary: The paper develops HNNDTA, a hybrid neural network model to predict drug-target binding affinity for repurposing FDA-approved drugs as Alzheimer's disease treatments.

- **[Deep learning-guided discovery of selective JAK2-JH2 allosteric inhibitors: integration of MLP predictive modeling, BREED-based library design, and computational validation.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41404389/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2025
	- Summary: Deep learning MLP model trained on JAK2 compounds to predict and discover selective allosteric inhibitors through computational screening and molecular design.

- **[Cocrystal Prediction of Bexarotene by Graph Convolution Network and Bioavailability Improvement.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36297633/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2022Oct16
	- Summary: A graph convolutional network (GCN) deep learning model was developed to predict cocrystal formation for bexarotene to improve its bioavailability and solubility.

- **[Suggestion of active 3-chymotrypsin like protease (3CLPro) inhibitors as potential anti-SARS-CoV-2 agents using predictive QSAR model based on the combination of ALASSO with an ANN model.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34634208/)**
	- Methodology: Feature selection, Neural networks
	- Published: 2021Nov
	- Summary: Uses ALASSO feature selection combined with artificial neural networks to develop QSAR models for predicting new 3CLPro inhibitors as potential SARS-CoV-2 treatments.

- **[Multi-stage virtual screening of natural products against p38α mitogen-activated protein kinase: predictive modeling by machine learning, docking study and molecular dynamics simulation.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36105464/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2022Sep
	- Summary: Machine learning models (Random Forest and SVM) are used for virtual screening to identify natural product inhibitors of p38α MAPK, combined with molecular docking and dynamics.

## Drug toxicity prediction

- **[Attention Mechanism-Based Graph Neural Network Model for Effective Activity Prediction of SARS-CoV-2 Main Protease Inhibitors: Application to Drug Repurposing as Potential COVID-19 Therapy.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37960886/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2023Nov27
	- Summary: Graph neural networks with attention mechanisms are used to predict SARS-CoV-2 main protease inhibitor activity for COVID-19 drug repurposing from existing drug databases.

- **[A computer method for validating traditional Chinese medicine herbal prescriptions.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/15974487/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2005
	- Summary: Uses support vector machine (SVM) to classify whether multi-herb recipes are valid traditional Chinese medicine prescriptions based on herbal properties.

- **[Prediction of logP for Pt(II) and Pt(IV) complexes: Comparison of statistical and quantum-chemistry based approaches.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/26717258/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2016Mar
	- Summary: The paper develops statistical models to predict logP values for platinum complexes used in anticancer drug development, using consensus modeling approaches.

- **[Clozapine Toxicity Predictor: Deep neural network model predicting clozapine toxicity and its therapeutic dose range.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39522262/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2024Dec
	- Summary: Deep neural network model predicts clozapine toxicity and therapeutic dose range using clinical data to guide treatment when therapeutic drug monitoring is unavailable.

- **[Machine learning-based quantitative prediction of drug exposure in drug-drug interactions using drug label information.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35817846/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2022Jul11
	- Summary: Machine learning model predicts quantitative pharmacokinetic drug-drug interaction fold changes using FDA drug label data to enable DDI evaluation before clinical trials.

- **[Computational determination of toxicity risks associated with a selection of approved drugs having demonstrated activity against COVID-19.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34674775/)**
	- Published: 2021Oct21
	- Summary: Computational methods are used to determine toxicity risks of approved drugs with COVID-19 activity for drug repurposing purposes.

- **[Models for skin and brain penetration of major components from essential oils used in aromatherapy for dementia patients.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31204906/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2020May
	- Summary: Artificial neural networks are used to develop QSAR models predicting skin and blood-brain barrier penetration of essential oil terpenoids for aromatherapy applications.

- **[Multi-objective Molecular Optimization for Opioid Use Disorder Treatment Using Generative Network Complex.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37396606/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Unsupervised learning, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2023Jun13
	- Summary: Deep generative models using SDE-based diffusion and autoencoders generate multi-target opioid receptor molecules with optimized ADMET properties for OUD treatment discovery.

- **[Feature importance of machine learning prediction models shows structurally active part and important physicochemical features in drug design.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34089983/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Explainable AI, Feature selection
	- Published: 2021Aug
	- Summary: This paper develops machine learning models to predict pharmacokinetic properties and uses explainable AI to identify important physicochemical features and metabolic sites for drug design.

- **[In-silico identification of a Doxorubicin alternative with reduced cardiotoxicity informed by LLM-assisted modeling.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41223606/)**
	- Methodology: LLM, Tree-based models, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2026Jan
	- Summary: LLM-assisted pipeline with random forest and SHAP for predicting ADME/ADMET properties to identify safer doxorubicin alternatives with reduced cardiotoxicity.

- **[High-Throughput Physiologically Based Pharmacokinetic Model for Rodent Pharmacokinetics Prediction Using Machine Learning-Predicted Inputs and a Large In Vivo Pharmacokinetics Data Set.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41617209/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2026Mar02
	- Summary: The paper develops a high-throughput PBPK model (SwiftPK) that uses machine learning to predict in vitro parameters from chemical structures for rodent pharmacokinetic prediction.

- **[In silico analyses of toxicity of the major constituents of essential oils from two Ipomoea L. species.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33667485/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2021May
	- Summary: Machine learning algorithms are used to predict cytochrome P450 and P-glycoprotein inhibition activity for toxicity assessment of essential oil constituents from Ipomoea species.

- **[Prediction of Vancomycin-Associated Nephrotoxicity Based on the Area under the Concentration-Time Curve of Vancomycin: A Machine Learning Analysis.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39603615/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2024
	- Summary: Machine learning models including random forest and AdaBoost were developed to predict vancomycin-associated nephrotoxicity using AUC and patient factors, with SMOTE for imbalanced data.

- **[Machine Learning-Based Prediction of Escitalopram and Sertraline Side Effects With Pharmacokinetic Data in Children and Adolescents.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38297828/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024Apr
	- Summary: Machine learning models, specifically penalized logistic regression, were developed to predict SSRI side effects in pediatric patients using pharmacokinetic data and electronic health records.

- **[Bayesian algorithm implementation in a real time exposure assessment model on benzene with calculation of associated cancer risks.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/22399936/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Bayesian ML
	- Published: 2009
	- Summary: The paper develops ANNs with Bayesian regularization to predict benzene exposure and uses Bayesian algorithms in PBPK models to assess leukemia risk for filling station employees.

- **[Activity Cliff-Informed Contrastive Learning for Molecular Property Prediction.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39678335/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024Dec04
	- Summary: Introduces ACANet, a contrastive learning approach using graph neural networks to improve molecular property prediction by incorporating activity cliff awareness.

- **[Predicting Pharmacokinetics in Rats Using Machine Learning: A Comparative Study Between Empirical, Compartmental, and PBPK-Based Approaches.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40091606/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2025Mar
	- Summary: This study compares ML approaches integrated with empirical and mechanistic PK models to predict complete plasma concentration-time profiles in rats prior to synthesis.

- **[Prediction of polyreactive and nonspecific single-chain fragment variables through structural biochemical features and protein language-based descriptors.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36471239/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, LLM
	- Published: 2022Dec05
	- Summary: Uses protein language models and structural features to predict polyreactivity of single-chain antibody fragments for early drug developability assessment.

- **[Pushing the boundaries of few-shot learning for low-data drug discovery with a Bayesian meta-learning hypernetwork framework.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40814226/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Bayesian ML

	- Summary: Meta-Mol uses Bayesian meta-learning with hypernetworks for few-shot learning to predict molecular properties in drug discovery with limited data.

	- Summary: Machine learning algorithm developed to predict optimal starting doses of daptomycin based on PK/PD targets for efficacy and toxicity using Monte Carlo simulations.

	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2018Aug24
	- Summary: The paper develops an improved support vector machine system to predict drug clearance pathways among 9 routes, expanding from 5 pathways to include additional CYP and UGT pathways.

- **[Identification of mycobacterial Thymidylate kinase inhibitors: a comprehensive pharmacophore, machine learning, molecular docking, and molecular dynamics simulation studies.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39152354/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024Aug
	- Summary: Machine learning is used to predict binding affinity of potential TMK inhibitors for tuberculosis drug development through pharmacophore modeling and molecular screening.

- **[Machine Learning Enabled Structure-Based Drug Repurposing Approach to Identify Potential CYP1B1 Inhibitors.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36120033/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Neural networks, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2022Sep13
	- Summary: Machine learning models (SVM, RF, ANN) combined with structure-based virtual screening to identify potential CYP1B1 inhibitors for drug repurposing to overcome anticancer drug resistance.

- **[AI-assisted identification of innovative phytochemicals from Aizoon canariense aimed at brachyury protein in chordoma: a computational strategy.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41419749/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2025Dec19
	- Summary: AI-assisted drug discovery approach used to optimize phytochemicals from Aizoon canariense for targeting TBXT protein in chordoma treatment.

- **[Drug repurposing through Biophysical Insights: Focus on Indoleamine 2,3-Dioxygenase and Tryptophan 2,3-Dioxygenase Dual Inhibitors.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40133710/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Sep
	- Summary: This paper uses machine learning analysis alongside molecular docking to repurpose FDA-approved drugs as dual IDO1/TDO inhibitors for cancer treatment.

- **[AI fragment based optimization of saffron and chamomile phytochemicals as aryl hydrocarbon receptor inhibitors for dementia therapy an integrated computational approach.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40784071/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2026Feb
	- Summary: AI generative models are used to optimize phytochemicals from saffron and chamomile as aryl hydrocarbon receptor inhibitors for dementia therapy through fragment-based optimization.

- **[Predictive Multitask Deep Neural Network Models for ADME-Tox Properties: Learning from Large Data Sets.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30615828/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2019Mar25
	- Summary: Deep neural networks are used in a multitask learning approach to predict ADME-Tox properties from large chemical databases for drug discovery optimization.

- **[Prediction of plasma concentration-time profiles in mice using deep neural network integrated with pharmacokinetic models.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40250502/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Neural networks, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2025May15
	- Summary: Deep neural networks integrated with two-compartmental PK models to predict plasma concentration-time profiles in mice using chemical structure and ADME features.

- **[Classification Tree Analysis Based On Machine Learning for Predicting Linezolid-Induced Thrombocytopenia.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33609520/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models
	- Published: 2021May
	- Summary: CART analysis is used to predict linezolid-induced thrombocytopenia by identifying cutoff values for platelet reduction and drug concentration at 96 hours post-dose.

- **[BICEPP: an example-based statistical text mining method for predicting the binary characteristics of drugs.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/21510898/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2011Apr21
	- Summary: BICEPP uses statistical text mining with supervised learning to predict binary drug characteristics from text data to assist clinical screening.

- **[MolP-PC: a multi-view fusion and multi-task learning framework for drug ADMET property prediction.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41260779/)**

	- Summary: Deep learning framework using multi-view fusion and multi-task learning to predict drug ADMET properties from molecular representations.

- **[IDL-PPBopt: A Strategy for Prediction and Optimization of Human Plasma Protein Binding of Compounds via an Interpretable Deep Learning Method.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35607907/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2022Jun13
	- Summary: Uses interpretable deep learning to predict and optimize human plasma protein binding of compounds, identifying privileged substructures for drug optimization.

- **[MetaboGNN: predicting liver metabolic stability with graph neural networks and cross-species data.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40903787/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2025Sep03
	- Summary: MetaboGNN uses Graph Neural Networks with contrastive learning to predict liver metabolic stability across species, improving drug discovery predictions.

	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2018Mar20
	- Summary: QSAR models using six machine learning algorithms with consensus prediction to predict plasma protein binding of compounds for drug discovery applications.

- **[A Machine Learning-Empowered Quantitative Structure-Activity Relationship Model for Predicting the Plasma Half-life of Drugs in Dogs.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41254440/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2025Nov18
	- Summary: Deep neural networks and supervised ML algorithms are used to build QSAR models predicting drug plasma half-life in dogs from chemical structure descriptors.

- **[Convolutional neural network-assisted screening of natural product inhibitors against Naja naja venom: insights from molecular docking, molecular dynamics simulations and ADMET profiling.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41459409/)**

	- Summary: Uses convolutional neural networks (GraphDTA with GINConvNet) for virtual screening of natural compounds against snake venom proteins to identify potential antivenom candidates.

- **[A Predictive Tool for Electrophilic Aromatic Substitutions Using Machine Learning.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30336024/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning

	- Summary: Machine learning model predicts reactive sites for electrophilic aromatic substitutions in drug development using SMILES and quantum mechanics descriptors.

	- Methodology: Supervised learning

	- Summary: AI-based platform used to design selective BMX inhibitors and predict physicochemical/pharmacokinetic properties for cancer therapeutic development.

	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025
	- Summary: The paper develops an automated machine learning model to predict the risk of anti-tuberculosis drug-induced liver injury in pediatric patients.

- **[A Robust Machine Learning Framework Built Upon Molecular Representations Predicts CYP450 Inhibition: Toward Precision in Drug Repurposing.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37406257/)**

	- Summary: Develops a majority-voting ML framework using molecular representations and protein-ligand interaction fingerprints to predict CYP450 inhibition for drug repurposing.

	- Methodology: Supervised learning

	- Summary: Machine learning models predict rat pharmacokinetics from chemical structure, then allometrically scale to human PK for early-stage human dose prediction in drug discovery.

	- Summary: Deep learning models are used to predict pIC50 values and identify potential FGFR2 inhibitors from phytochemicals for cancer treatment applications.

	- Summary: The paper uses AlphaFold3 deep learning algorithm alongside computational docking to predict protein-protein interactions for engineered therapeutic targeting proteins.

- **[Improving Vancomycin Through Level Prediction with Machine Learning: A Comparative Study of Feature Selection and Model Performance.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41336096/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Feature selection, Explainable AI, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2025Jul
	- Summary: Machine learning models (Random Forest, LightGBM, XGBoost) are used to predict vancomycin trough levels for individualized dosing, outperforming traditional Bayesian methods.

- **[Identification of novel hit molecules targeting M. tuberculosis polyketide synthase 13 by combining generative AI and physics-based methods.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38723396/)**

- **[An AI-based Prediction Model for Drug-drug Interactions in Osteoporosis and Paget's Diseases from SMILES.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34989149/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Ensemble learning

	- Summary: An AI-based ensemble model using Random Forest and XGBoost predicts drug-drug interactions in osteoporosis and Paget's disease treatments from molecular SMILES data.

- **[Generation of new inhibitors of selected cytochrome P450 subtypes- In silico study.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36284709/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Deep learning
	- Published: 2022
	- Summary: The paper develops a graph neural network to predict activity changes and generate new cytochrome P450 inhibitors for improved metabolic stability in drug design.

	- Summary: Uses SVM classifier combined with PBPK modeling to predict hepatotoxic doses and dosing schedules for traditional Chinese medicine natural products.

	- Methodology: Surrogate modeling

	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks

	- Summary: Meta-GAT uses graph attention networks with meta learning to predict molecular properties for drug discovery in low-data scenarios through bilevel optimization.

	- Methodology: Neural networks, Bayesian ML

	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2023Nov14
	- Summary: Uses CNNs and feedforward networks to predict plasma protein binding of drugs from molecular structures, achieving high accuracy for drug development applications.

- **[Neural network emulation of the human ventricular cardiomyocyte action potential: a tool for more efficient computation in pharmacological studies.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38234850/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Surrogate modeling
	- Published: 2023Dec25
	- Summary: Neural networks are used to emulate human ventricular cardiomyocyte action potentials for faster computation in pharmacological studies and drug effect prediction.

	- Summary: The paper presents a hybrid modeling platform that integrates machine learning optimization with mechanistic models to predict tissue-plasma partition coefficients and dosing regimens.

	- Methodology: Tree-based models, Neural networks, Ensemble learning

	- Summary: The paper proposes a data-driven screening model using fusion regression (LGBM+BP-NN) and XGBoost to predict drug bioactivity and ADMET properties for pharmaceutical candidate selection.

- **[Predicting chemosensitivity using drug perturbed gene dynamics.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33413081/)**

	- Summary: Develops a novel regression model combining DeepSnap deep learning with conventional ML to predict rat clearance, improving QSAR prediction performance.

- **[Pushing the Boundaries of Molecular Representation for Drug Discovery with the Graph Attention Mechanism.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31408336/)**

	- Methodology: Supervised learning

	- Summary: Machine learning models are developed to predict ADME properties of small molecules and analyze their distributions across different ATC drug classes to guide drug discovery.
