	- Published: 2021Dec14
	- Summary: This paper compares deep neural network architectures (MLP, GCN, Mol2Vec) for predicting ADME properties and biological activity in drug discovery applications.

- **[Synthesis, Docking, and Machine Learning Studies of Some Novel Quinolinesulfonamides-Triazole Hybrids with Anticancer Activity.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38999109/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024Jul02
	- Summary: Machine learning methods are used alongside molecular docking to design quinolinesulfonamide-triazole hybrids and predict their anticancer activity and toxicity profiles.

- **[Improving the potency prediction for chemically modified siRNAs through insights from molecular modeling of individual sequence positions.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40171444/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Mar11
	- Summary: Machine learning is used to predict siRNA biological activity by correlating molecular modeling properties with experimental potency data for rational drug design.

- **[Updated in Silico Prediction Methods for Fractions Absorbed and Key Input Parameters of 355 Disparate Chemicals for Physiologically Based Pharmacokinetic Models for Time-Dependent Plasma Concentrations after Virtual Oral Doses in Humans.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36171106/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models
	- Published: 2022Dec01
	- Summary: Uses LightGBM machine learning to predict PBPK model input parameters from chemical descriptors for estimating human plasma concentrations after oral dosing.

- **[Predictive modeling of pediatric drug-induced liver injury: Dynamic classifier selection with clustering analysis.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40123880/)**
	- Methodology: Ensemble learning, Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2025Jan-Dec
	- Summary: The paper develops predictive models for pediatric drug-induced liver injury using dynamic classifier selection combined with clustering analysis to improve early identification.

- **[Identification of novel class I and class IIb histone deacetylase inhibitor for Alzheimer's disease therapeutics.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32504755/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2020Sep01
	- Summary: Machine learning is used to classify compounds as HDAC inhibitors/non-inhibitors for Alzheimer's disease drug discovery through virtual screening of ChEMBL database compounds.

- **[Prediction of Total Drug Clearance in Humans Using Animal Data: Proposal of a Multimodal Learning Method Based on Deep Learning.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33497658/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Supervised learning, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2021Apr
	- Summary: Deep learning multimodal approach combining chemical structure and rat clearance data to predict human drug clearance with explainable structure-activity insights.

- **[Actionable Predictions of Human Pharmacokinetics at the Drug Design Stage.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39132855/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Deep learning, Bayesian ML
	- Published: 2024Sep02
	- Summary: Machine learning models with self-supervised pretraining and epistemic uncertainty quantification are developed to predict human pharmacokinetic endpoints from chemical structures.

- **[Cluster-Based Toxicity Estimation of Osteoradionecrosis via Unsupervised Machine Learning: Moving Beyond Single Dose-Parameter Normal Tissue Complication Probability by Using Whole Dose-Volume Histograms for Cohort Risk Stratification.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37034700/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2023Mar29
	- Summary: Uses unsupervised machine learning to develop cluster-based models for predicting osteoradionecrosis toxicity risk by analyzing whole dose-volume histograms for patient stratification.

- **[vNN Web Server for ADMET Predictions.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/29255418/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2017
	- Summary: The paper presents vNN neural network models for predicting ADMET properties including cytotoxicity, mutagenicity, cardiotoxicity, and drug-induced liver injury in drug development.

- **[Guided Ensemble Stacking Method for Predicting Biological Activities of Compounds.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41457256/)**
	- Methodology: Ensemble learning, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Dec
	- Summary: The paper develops a guided ensemble stacking ML approach combining multiple algorithms to predict compound biological activities for improved drug discovery.

- **[Targeting the glabellar frown lines with OnabotulinumtoxinA: In-silico evidence supporting concentrated, low-volume injection protocols.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40907830/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2025Nov
	- Summary: Uses machine learning for off-target risk prediction integrated with mechanistic PK/PD modeling to optimize BoNT-A dosing protocols for glabellar frown line treatment.

- **[Towards Deep Neural Network Models for the Prediction of the Blood-Brain Barrier Permeability for Diverse Organic Compounds.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33322142/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2020Dec13
	- Summary: Deep neural networks are used to predict blood-brain barrier permeability for drug compounds using fragmental descriptors and artificial neural network approaches.

- **[QSAR analysis and experimental evaluation of new quinazoline-containing hydroxamic acids as histone deacetylase 6 inhibitors.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35786151/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2022Jul
	- Summary: Uses Random Forest and XGBoost to build QSAR models for predicting HDAC6 inhibitory activity and screens new quinazoline compounds for cancer treatment.

- **[SolPredictor: Predicting Solubility with Residual Gated Graph Neural Network.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38255790/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2024Jan05
	- Summary: The paper presents SolPredictor, a residual gated graph neural network model for predicting molecular solubility to enhance drug discovery and development processes.

- **[Integrating Pharmacokinetics and Quantitative Systems Pharmacology Approaches in Generative Drug Design.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40343729/)**
	- Methodology: Reinforcement learning, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025May26
	- Summary: The paper integrates AI/ML methods including reinforcement learning and QSPR models in a generative drug design framework to optimize both PK properties and receptor affinity.

- **[MODEL-molecular descriptor lab: a web-based server for computing structural and physicochemical features of compounds.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/17013940/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2007Jun01
	- Summary: Paper presents a web server for computing molecular descriptors used in QSAR and neural network models to predict pharmacological and toxicological properties of compounds.

- **[Fitness Landscape for Antibodies 2: Benchmarking Reveals That Protein AI Models Cannot Yet Consistently Predict Developability Properties.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41497662/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Dec27
	- Summary: This paper benchmarks 30 AI models including protein language models on predicting antibody developability properties using the largest public therapeutic antibody dataset (FLAb2).

- **[Machine Learning Models for Human In Vivo Pharmacokinetic Parameters with In-House Validation.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34758626/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2021Dec06
	- Summary: Machine learning models predict human pharmacokinetic parameters from chemical structure to accelerate drug discovery and reduce reliance on costly preclinical studies.

- **[An Integrated AI-PBPK Platform for Predicting Drug In Vivo Fate and Tissue Distribution in Human and Inter-Species Extrapolation.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40418625/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2025Oct
	- Summary: Develops an integrated AI-PBPK platform using machine learning to predict drug properties from molecular structures, which feeds into PBPK models for human PK prediction.

- **[An innovative machine learning-based QSAR approach for prediction and structural analysis of novel/repurposed acid ceramidase (ASAH1) inhibitors for glioblastoma therapy.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40682750/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Feature selection, Explainable AI, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2025Jul19
	- Summary: ML-QSAR approach using extra trees regressor and SHAP analysis to predict and identify novel ASAH1 inhibitors for glioblastoma therapy through virtual screening.

- **[DL-ADR: a novel deep learning model for classifying genomic variants into adverse drug reactions.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/27510822/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2016Aug10
	- Summary: A deep learning model using generative stochastic networks and hidden Markov chains to classify genomic variants (SNPs) in CYP genes for predicting adverse drug reactions.

- **[StreamChol: a web-based application for predicting cholestasis.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39838478/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2025Jan21
	- Summary: StreamChol is a web-based tool that integrates pharmacokinetic analyses with machine learning models to predict cholestasis toxicity in drug development.

- **[In silico evaluation of pharmacokinetic properties and molecular docking for the identification of potential anticancer compounds.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40803051/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2026Feb
	- Summary: The paper uses in silico methods including QSAR/SAR/QSPR for predicting ADME-Tox properties and identifying potential anticancer compounds through molecular docking.

- **[pH-dependent solubility prediction for optimized drug absorption and compound uptake by plants.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36797399/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Tree-based models, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2023Mar
	- Summary: Multi-task neural networks predict pH-dependent aqueous solubility for drug and agrochemical compounds to optimize absorption and bioavailability across different biological conditions.

- **[Contribution assessment of multiparameter optimization descriptors in CNS penetration.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30274532/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Gaussian processes, Feature selection
	- Published: 2018Oct
	- Summary: The paper applies multiple ML methods including logistic regression, random forest, SVM, and Gaussian processes to predict CNS penetration and assess physicochemical property contributions.

- **[Enhancing ADMET property predictions using cross-aligned multimodal attention mechanisms.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41764692/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2026Mar01
	- Summary: The paper presents a Cross-Aligned Multimodal Attention mechanism using deep learning to enhance ADMET property predictions in drug discovery through multimodal data integration.

- **[Computational screening of natural products as tryptophan 2,3-dioxygenase inhibitors: Insights from CNN-based QSAR, molecular docking, ADMET, and molecular dynamics simulations.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40233673/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2025Jun
	- Summary: CNN-based QSAR models and machine learning are used to computationally screen natural products as potential TDO inhibitors for Parkinson's disease treatment.

- **[Associations of novel genetic variations in the folate-related and ARID5B genes with the pharmacokinetics and toxicity of high-dose methotrexate in paediatric acute lymphoblastic leukaemia.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/24712521/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models, Feature selection
	- Published: 2014Aug
	- Summary: Random forest and regression trees were used for variable selection to identify genetic variations associated with methotrexate pharmacokinetics and toxicity in pediatric patients.

- **[In Silico Prediction of Human Clinical Pharmacokinetics with ANDROMEDA by Prosilico: Predictions for an Established Benchmarking Data Set, a Modern Small Drug Data Set, and a Comparison with Laboratory Methods.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36572567/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2023Jan
	- Summary: ANDROMEDA uses machine learning combined with physiologically-based pharmacokinetic modeling to predict human clinical pharmacokinetics in silico as an alternative to laboratory methods.

- **[Application of machine learning in combination with mechanistic modeling to predict plasma exposure of small molecules.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40809487/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Mechanism-informed machine learning
	- Published: 2023
	- Summary: The paper proposes a framework combining machine learning with mechanistic modeling to predict plasma exposure of small molecules from molecular structure and properties.

- **[Read-Across Structure-Property Relationship-Based Superior Prediction of Fraction Unbound in Plasma from Chemical Structure: Interpretable Models with Minimum Descriptors.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41692400/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2026Feb
	- Summary: Uses read-across strategy with machine learning methods including regression and support vector classifiers to predict fraction unbound in plasma from chemical structures.

- **[Improved Detection of Drug-Induced Liver Injury by Integrating Predicted in vivo and in vitro Data.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38895462/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2024Jun08
	- Summary: ML models predict drug-induced liver injury by integrating structural features with predicted in vitro/in vivo proxy-DILI labels, achieving improved detection performance.

- **[Predicting substrates of the human breast cancer resistance protein using a support vector machine method.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/23586520/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2013Apr15
	- Summary: The paper develops SVM models to predict whether drugs are substrates of BCRP transporter, which affects drug pharmacokinetics and multidrug resistance in cancer.

- **[Stacked ensemble learning and in-silico profiling reveal dual DPP-IV and SGLT2 inhibitors from Moringa oleifera metabolites.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41708792/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2026Feb18
	- Summary: Stacked ensemble machine learning models were developed to identify dual DPP-IV/SGLT2 inhibitors from Moringa oleifera metabolites for diabetes treatment.

- **[Discovery of novel NLRP3 inhibitors based on machine learning and physical methods.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39468648/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2024Oct28
	- Summary: Machine learning models (LightGBM, Random Forest, XGBoost) were used to screen millions of compounds for discovering novel NLRP3 inhibitors as potential therapeutics for alcohol-related liver disease.

- **[Evaluating the Immunogenicity Risk of Protein Therapeutics by Augmenting T Cell Epitope Prediction with Clinical Factors.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39849284/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2025Jan23
	- Summary: The paper combines clinical factors with in silico epitope prediction using supervised learning to improve prediction accuracy of protein therapeutic immunogenicity risk.

- **[PredPS: Attention-based graph neural network for predicting stability of compounds in human plasma.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37484492/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2023
	- Summary: Develops PredPS, an attention-based graph neural network to predict compound stability in human plasma for early-stage drug discovery screening.

- **[Quantitative Structure-Activity Relationships for Structurally Diverse Chemotypes Having Anti-Trypanosoma cruzi Activity.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31181717/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2019Jun08
	- Summary: This paper uses artificial neural networks and kernel-based partial least squares regression to develop QSAR models predicting anti-T. cruzi activity for drug design.

- **[Integration of in vitro and in silico approaches enables prediction of drug-induced liver injury.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41699306/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2026Feb17
	- Summary: The paper combines in vitro toxicity data with in silico pharmacokinetic modeling using physiologically based kinetic models to predict drug-induced liver injury.

- **[Virtual Screening Based on Machine Learning Explores Mangrove Natural Products as KRASG12C Inhibitors.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35631410/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2022May08
	- Summary: Machine learning, specifically random forest, is used for virtual screening of mangrove natural products to identify potential KRASG12C inhibitors for cancer treatment.

- **[Hybrid neural network approaches to predict drug-target binding affinity for drug repurposing: screening for potential leads for Alzheimer's disease.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37441162/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2023
	- Summary: The paper develops HNNDTA, a hybrid neural network model to predict drug-target binding affinity for repurposing FDA-approved drugs as Alzheimer's disease treatments.

- **[De novo drug design and biological evaluation of coumarin-pyrimidine co-drug derivatives as diabetic inhibitors: expanding a multi-algorithm approach with the integration of machine learning in pharmaceutical research.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40656568/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Jul10
	- Summary: Machine learning is integrated into de novo drug design for coumarin-pyrimidine derivatives as diabetic inhibitors, including toxicity prediction using web-based servers.

- **[Benchmarking of Small Molecule Feature Representations for hERG, Nav1.5, and Cav1.2 Cardiotoxicity Prediction.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37870574/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2024Apr08
	- Summary: This paper develops a deep learning framework to predict cardiotoxicity for three cardiac ion channels using molecular fingerprints, descriptors, and graph-based representations.

- **[Discovery of newer 1,3,4-Oxadiazole clubbed Isoindoline-1,3-dione derivatives as potential anticancer agents: Design, machine learning, synthesis, molecular docking, ADMET, DFT and MD simulation.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40306097/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Oct
	- Summary: Machine learning is used to predict IC50 values for anticancer drug candidates in the design and discovery of novel 1,3,4-Oxadiazole clubbed Isoindoline-1,3-dione derivatives.

- **[Predicting drug metabolism and pharmacokinetics features of in-house compounds by a hybrid machine-learning model.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33991751/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2021Aug
	- Summary: Machine learning models predict drug metabolism and pharmacokinetics with applicability domain assessment to improve reliability of predictions for in-house compounds.

- **[Enhancing the Predictive Power of Macrocyclic Drug Permeability by Knowledge Distillation from Analogous Pretraining Data.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41420604/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Supervised learning, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2026Jan08
	- Summary: Multi_DDPP uses deep learning with knowledge distillation to predict macrocyclic drug membrane permeability from 2D molecular structures, improving drug development efficiency.

- **[Discovery of Bergamotanes against Hepatic Ischemia-Reperfusion Injury Based on DeepSAT Technology from Paraconiothyrium sp. H-B.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41283635/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2025Dec10
	- Summary: The paper uses DeepSAT, a neural network-based tool for HSQC spectrum analysis, to guide discovery of bergamotane compounds with hepatoprotective effects against liver injury.

- **[iQSP: A Sequence-Based Tool for the Prediction and Analysis of Quorum Sensing Peptides via Chou's 5-Steps Rule and Informative Physicochemical Properties.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31861928/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Feature selection, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2019Dec20
	- Summary: The paper develops iQSP, a sequence-based predictor using SVM and random forest to identify quorum sensing peptides for combating bacterial infections through drug design.

- **[Benchmarking the Predictive Power of Ligand Efficiency Indices in QSAR.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/27399907/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2016Aug22
	- Summary: This paper benchmarks ligand efficiency indices as dependent variables in QSAR models using multiple ML algorithms to improve drug discovery predictive power.

- **[Using classification structure pharmacokinetic relationship (SCPR) method to predict drug bioavailability based on grid-search support vector machine.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/17920387/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2007Oct10
	- Summary: The paper uses grid-search support vector machine and linear discriminant analysis to predict drug bioavailability from molecular descriptors, comparing classification models.

- **[Multi-stage virtual screening of natural products against p38α mitogen-activated protein kinase: predictive modeling by machine learning, docking study and molecular dynamics simulation.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36105464/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2022Sep
	- Summary: Machine learning models (Random Forest and SVM) are used for virtual screening to identify natural product inhibitors of p38α MAPK, combined with molecular docking and dynamics.

- **[Good oral absorption prediction on non-nucleoside benzothiadiazine dioxide human cytomegalovirus inhibitors using combined chromatographic and neuronal network techniques.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/15780633/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2005Apr01
	- Summary: Neural networks (CODES) combined with chromatographic techniques are used to predict oral absorption and bioavailability of benzothiadiazine dioxide derivatives as HCMV inhibitors.

- **[Impact of gut permeability on estimation of oral bioavailability for chemicals in commerce and the environment.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39434630/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models
	- Published: 2025
	- Summary: Random forest QSPR model predicts gut permeability to improve pharmacokinetic modeling and bioavailability estimation for non-pharmaceutical chemicals in toxicity assessment.

## Endpoint / biomarker assessment

- **[Analyzing patient perspectives with large language models: a cross-sectional study of sentiment and thematic classification on exception from informed consent.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39979559/)**
	- Methodology: LLM
	- Published: 2025Feb20
	- Summary: This study evaluates large language models (LLMs) including GPT-4 for analyzing patient sentiment and thematic classification in exception from informed consent interviews.

- **[Independent component analysis applied to pharmacological magnetic resonance imaging (phMRI): new insights into the functional networks underlying panic attacks as induced by CCK-4.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/19075726/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2008
	- Summary: The paper applies independent component analysis (ICA) to pharmacological fMRI data to identify neural networks underlying CCK-4 induced panic attacks.

- **[Virtual patient analysis identifies strategies to improve the performance of predictive biomarkers for PD-1 blockade.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39467131/)**
	- Methodology: Feature selection, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2024Nov05
	- Summary: Uses ML-based feature selection with quantitative systems pharmacology modeling to identify and optimize predictive biomarkers for PD-1 blockade response in TNBC patients.

- **[Improving the Reliability of Pharmacokinetic Parameters at Dynamic Contrast-enhanced MRI in Astrocytomas: A Deep Learning Approach.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32749203/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2020Oct
	- Summary: Deep learning model translates arterial input functions from DCE MRI to improve reliability of pharmacokinetic parameters for astrocytoma grading.

- **[Automatic determination of arterial input function for dynamic contrast enhanced MRI in tumor assessment.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/18979795/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2008
	- Summary: The paper presents an automatic method using detection and segmentation algorithms to determine arterial input function for pharmacokinetic modeling in DCE-MRI tumor assessment.

- **[Leveraging AI for facioscapulohumeral muscular dystrophy prediction and omics biomarker identification.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41256551/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Feature selection
	- Published: 2025Oct03
	- Summary: This paper develops ML models using Random Forest and SVM-RFE to predict FSHD from multi-omics data and identify key biomarker features for disease classification.

- **[[A GA-BP neural network model based on spectrum-effect relationship for assessing spectrum-effect score and quality evaluation of Cassia seeds extract].](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41139439/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2025Oct20
	- Summary: The paper develops a GA-BP neural network model to assess spectrum-effect relationships for quality control of Cassia seeds extract using spectrum-effect scoring.

- **[The cortical neurophysiological signature of amyotrophic lateral sclerosis.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38779353/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models
	- Published: 2024
	- Summary: Uses random forest regression to predict disease severity from magnetoencephalography metrics in amyotrophic lateral sclerosis patients for biomarker development.

- **[Radiomic Analysis of Pharmacokinetic Heterogeneity Within Tumor Based on the Unsupervised Decomposition of Dynamic Contrast-Enhanced MRI for Predicting Histological Characteristics of Breast Cancer.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34773446/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2022Jun
	- Summary: Uses unsupervised decomposition of dynamic contrast-enhanced MRI to analyze pharmacokinetic heterogeneity within breast tumors for predicting histological characteristics.

- **[Fully-automated deep learning-powered system for DCE-MRI analysis of brain tumors.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31980106/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2020Jan
	- Summary: Deep learning system automates DCE-MRI analysis for brain tumor pharmacokinetic modeling and biomarker extraction, eliminating manual processes and human error.

- **[Validation of Psychophysiological Measures for Caffeine Oral Films Characterization by Machine Learning Approaches.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35324803/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2022Mar11
	- Summary: Machine learning algorithms are used to analyze psychophysiological signals (ECG, EDA, RF) to characterize caffeine oral film delivery systems and detect caffeine activity.

- **[Multi-cohort, cross-species urinary proteomics reveals signatures of LRRK2 dysfunction in Parkinson's disease.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41611872/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2026Jan29
	- Summary: Machine learning was used to develop a 30-protein urinary panel that classifies LRRK2 G2019S carriers in Parkinson's disease with high accuracy across independent cohorts.

- **[Metabolomics and lipidomics predictor of survival in hepatocellular carcinoma patients receiving tyrosine kinase inhibitor and immune checkpoint inhibitor combination therapy.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41265377/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Feature selection, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2025Dec
	- Summary: Machine learning framework combining random forest, LASSO regression, and Cox modeling to identify metabolomic and lipidomic biomarkers predicting survival in HCC patients.

- **[Machine Learning Establishes Single-Cell Calcium Dynamics as an Early Indicator of Antibiotic Response.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34063175/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2021May05
	- Summary: Machine learning is used to analyze single-cell calcium dynamics as an early biomarker for antibiotic response and resistance detection in bacterial cells.

- **[A Comprehensive Analysis of Metabolomics and Transcriptomics Reveals Novel Biomarkers and Mechanistic Insights on Lorlatinib Crosses the Blood-Brain Barrier.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34497521/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Deep learning
	- Published: 2021
	- Summary: Uses multilayer perceptron neural networks to predict lorlatinib brain concentration based on metabolomic biomarkers, achieving >85% accuracy for brain penetration prediction.

- **[Single-cell peripheral immunoprofiling of Alzheimer's and Parkinson's diseases.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33239300/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2020Nov
	- Summary: Machine learning models were developed to predict Alzheimer's and Parkinson's diseases from single-cell immune profiling data to identify disease-specific biomarkers.

- **[Neural network-based segmentation of dynamic MR mammographic images.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/12034335/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2002Feb
	- Summary: Neural networks are used to segment dynamic MR mammographic images by classifying signal-time curves into tissue classes for breast cancer detection.

- **[Vector-Based Comparison and Average Slope Can Refine Bioequivalence Claims: A Machine and Deep Learning Approach.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41684335/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2026Feb13
	- Summary: The paper applies machine learning and artificial neural networks to improve bioequivalence studies using Average Slope and Vector-Based Comparison methods.

- **[DCE-MRI data analysis for cancer area classification.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/19387513/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2009
	- Summary: The paper proposes a machine learning methodology to automatically segment DCE-MRI data for isolating tumor areas with different histological meanings in cancer imaging.

- **[Tacrolimus in the treatment of childhood nephrotic syndrome: Machine learning detects novel biomarkers and predicts efficacy.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36521865/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2023Jan
	- Summary: Machine learning algorithms are used to identify biomarkers and develop prediction models for tacrolimus efficacy in childhood nephrotic syndrome patients.

- **[Association between salivary microbiota and tacrolimus pharmacokinetic variability in kidney transplant.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39426572/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models
	- Published: 2024Nov
	- Summary: Random forest model used to classify kidney transplant recipients based on tacrolimus pharmacokinetic variability using salivary microbiota composition as predictive features.

- **[Identification of Candidate Therapeutic Genes for More Precise Treatment of Esophageal Squamous Cell Carcinoma and Adenocarcinoma.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35664298/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Feature selection, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2022
	- Summary: Uses ensemble feature selection and random forest classification to identify differentially expressed genes as therapeutic targets for personalized esophageal cancer treatment.

- **[High-performance grating-like SERS substrate based on machine learning for ultrasensitive detection of Zexie-Baizhu decoction.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38726156/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models
	- Published: 2024May15
	- Summary: Machine learning algorithms including SVM, decision tree, and Naive Bayes are used with SERS technology to classify and identify traditional Chinese medicine decoctions.

- **[Radiomic combination of spatial and temporal features extracted from DCE-MRI for prostate cancer detection.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34891910/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2021Nov
	- Summary: Machine learning classifiers using quantitative radiomic features from DCE-MRI achieve good diagnostic performance in distinguishing malignant from benign prostate tissue regions.

- **[Treatment response assessment of breast masses on dynamic contrast-enhanced magnetic resonance scans using fuzzy c-means clustering and level set segmentation.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/19994516/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2009Nov
	- Summary: The paper uses fuzzy c-means clustering and level set segmentation to automatically assess breast tumor volume changes in response to chemotherapy treatment on MRI scans.

- **[Dynamic Contrast-enhanced MRI for Evaluating Breast Cancer Chemotherapy Response Using Conditional Generative Adversarial Networks.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41347917/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2026Jan
	- Summary: A conditional GAN is developed to rapidly translate DCE-MRI data to pharmacokinetic permeability maps for evaluating breast cancer chemotherapy response.

- **[A Physiological-Informed Generative Model for Improving Breast Lesion Classification in Small DCE-MRI Datasets.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39141452/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2024Nov
	- Summary: The paper develops a physiologically-informed generative model combining PBPK modeling with deep autoencoders for data augmentation to improve breast lesion classification in DCE-MRI.

- **[Temporal Feature Extraction from DCE-MRI to Identify Poorly Perfused Subvolumes of Tumors Related to Outcomes of Radiation Therapy in Head and Neck Cancer.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/28111634/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2016Dec
	- Summary: Uses SVM classification with temporal feature extraction from DCE-MRI to identify poorly perfused tumor subvolumes for predicting radiation therapy outcomes in head and neck cancer.

- **[A statistical pattern recognition approach for determining cellular viability and lineage phenotype in cultured cells and murine bone marrow.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/17542025/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2007Aug
	- Summary: The paper uses artificial neural networks for objective cellular phenotype classification in flow cytometry to determine cell viability and lineage boundaries.

- **[A precision medicine framework using artificial intelligence for the identification and confirmation of genomic biomarkers of response to an Alzheimer's disease therapy: Analysis of the blarcamesine (ANAVEX2-73) Phase 2a clinical study.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32318621/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2020
	- Summary: AI is used for genome-wide identification of biomarkers associated with therapeutic response to blarcamesine in Alzheimer's disease patients for precision medicine applications.

- **[Nanoplasmonic Avidity-Based Detection and Quantification of IgG Aggregates.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36318700/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2022Nov15
	- Summary: Neural network-based curve fitting is used to simultaneously quantify monomers and aggregates in therapeutic antibody quality control biosensor analysis.

- **[Statistical Learning Algorithm for in situ and invasive breast carcinoma segmentation.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/23693000/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2013Jun
	- Summary: The paper develops a Statistical Learning Algorithm for Tumor Segmentation (SLATS) using Hidden Markov Models to automatically segment breast cancer tumors from DCE-MRI data.

- **[Decoding the gut microbiota metabolite-matrix metalloproteinase-3 axis in breast cancer: a multi-omics and network pharmacology study.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40946247/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2025Sep14
	- Summary: This study uses machine learning with SHAP explainability to identify gut microbiota metabolites as potential therapeutic targets for breast cancer treatment via MMP3 axis.

- **[Combined 100 keV Cryo-Electron Microscopy and Image Analysis Methods to Characterize the Wider Adeno-Associated Viral Products.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38570072/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2024Jul
	- Summary: The paper uses convolutional neural networks for image analysis of cryo-electron micrographs to determine full-to-empty particle ratios in AAV gene therapy products.

- **[Analysis of Crosstalk Between Pathogens and Immune System in Human Airway Mucus via Machine Learning-Enhanced DIA Mass Spectrometry.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41104598/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Nov
	- Summary: Machine learning is used to enhance mass spectrometry analysis by matching low-abundance spectra to predicted peptide spectra for proteomic analysis of pathogen-immune interactions.

- **[Machine learning-guided, big data-enabled, biomarker-based systems pharmacology: modeling the stochasticity of natural history and disease progression.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34611796/)**
	- Methodology: Bayesian ML, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Time-series modeling
	- Published: 2022Feb
	- Summary: Uses Bayesian networks and stochastic VAR models to create hybrid ML-mechanistic systems pharmacology models for predicting biomarker dynamics and disease progression during aging.

- **[Molecular models of multiple sclerosis severity identify heterogeneity of pathogenic mechanisms.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36509784/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2022Dec12
	- Summary: Machine learning models using CSF biomarkers predict disability accumulation speed in MS patients and uncover molecular heterogeneity of pathogenic mechanisms.

- **[Computerized analysis of prostate lesions in the peripheral zone using dynamic contrast enhanced MRI.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/18404925/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2008Mar
	- Summary: The paper develops an automated computerized scheme using support vector machine classifier to determine likelihood of malignancy in prostate cancer from DCE-MRI images.

- **[Dexmedetomidine-induced deep sedation mimics non-rapid eye movement stage 3 sleep: large-scale validation using machine learning.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32860500/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2021Feb12
	- Summary: Machine learning techniques are used to validate that dexmedetomidine-induced deep sedation EEG patterns mimic natural non-REM stage 3 sleep patterns using large-scale recordings.

- **[Quantitative image analysis of intracellular protein translocation in 3-dimensional tissues for pharmacodynamic studies of immunogenic cell death.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37981052/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024Jan
	- Summary: The paper uses machine learning (trainable WEKA segmentation) for quantitative image analysis to assess ecto-CRT biomarker in 3D tissues for pharmacodynamic studies.

- **[Efficient DCE-MRI Parameter and Uncertainty Estimation Using a Neural Network.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31794389/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Bayesian ML

	- Summary: Neural networks are used to estimate tracer-kinetic parameters and their uncertainties from DCE-MRI data for brain tumor pharmacokinetic assessment.

- **[Retrospective quantification of clinical abdominal DCE-MRI using pharmacokinetics-informed deep learning: a proof-of-concept study.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37731600/)**

	- Published: 2023
	- Summary: Uses pharmacokinetics-informed deep learning to retrospectively improve temporal resolution and quantification of clinical abdominal DCE-MRI for cancer assessment.

- **[Large Language Models for Clinical Trial Protocol Assessments.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41121745/)**
	- Methodology: LLM
	- Published: 2026Feb
	- Summary: This paper evaluates the utility of large language models (GPT-4o) for reviewing statistical analysis plans and PK-PD components of clinical trial protocols.

- **[Virtual patient analysis identifies strategies to improve the performance of predictive biomarkers for PD-1 blockade.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38826266/)**
	- Methodology: Feature selection, Supervised learning

	- Summary: Uses machine learning-based feature selection and cutoff-based algorithms to identify and improve predictive biomarkers for PD-1 blockade response in TNBC patients.

- **[Automated calibration for computerized analysis of prostate lesions using pharmacokinetic magnetic resonance images.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/20426189/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Unsupervised learning

- **[Exploring the potential of Physics-Informed Neural Networks to extract vascularization data from DCE-MRI in the presence of diffusion.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38365330/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Mechanism-informed machine learning

	- Summary: Physics-Informed Neural Networks are used to improve DCE-MRI analysis by incorporating contrast agent diffusion into pharmacokinetic models for more accurate vascularization assessment.

	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2025Oct21
	- Summary: Machine learning classifiers are applied to network-based TSPO PET imaging data to identify disease-specific neuroinflammatory patterns and classify different neurological conditions.

- **[AI-assisted analysis of early fluid dynamics following aflibercept 8 mg in treatment-naïve neovascular AMD.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41741701/)**

	- Methodology: Neural networks

- **[An integrated strategy of spectrum-effect relationship and near-infrared spectroscopy rapid evaluation based on back propagation neural network for quality control of Paeoniae Radix Alba.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37037970/)**

	- Summary: Deep learning-based super-resolution and denoising techniques are applied to enhance DCE-MRI image quality and diagnostic reliability for diffuse glioma grading.

	- Summary: Uses support vector machine classification and independent components analysis on DCE-MRI data to differentiate treatment-related changes from progressive disease in brain tumor patients.

	- Methodology: Deep learning, Unsupervised learning

	- Summary: Deep learning-based segmentation and unsupervised ML are used to automate quantitative analysis of MSOT imaging data for pharmacokinetic and biodistribution studies.

	- Summary: Machine learning algorithms identify informative features to distinguish healthy/sick states and demonstrate TDM's positive impact on sepsis patient recovery through data-driven analysis.

- **[Unsupervised Deep Learning for Blood-Brain Barrier Leakage Detection in Diffuse Glioma Using Dynamic Contrast-enhanced MRI.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40172325/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning, Deep learning
	- Published: 2025May
	- Summary: Develops an unsupervised deep learning autoencoder framework to detect blood-brain barrier leakage in glioma patients using dynamic contrast-enhanced MRI without pharmacokinetic models.

- **[Automatic segmentation of invasive breast carcinomas from dynamic contrast-enhanced MRI using time series analysis.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/24115175/)**
	- Methodology: Time-series modeling
	- Published: 2014Aug
	- Summary: The paper uses time series analysis with linear dynamic system modeling to automatically segment invasive breast carcinomas from dynamic contrast-enhanced MRI data.

	- Methodology: Deep learning, Supervised learning, Neural networks, Explainable AI

	- Summary: Deep neural network ensemble with autoencoders is used for spatio-temporal denoising of DCE-MRI sequences to improve pharmacokinetic parameter extraction for blood-brain barrier assessment.

	- Methodology: LLM

	- Summary: Uses IBM Watson's natural language processing to analyze literature and identify potential pharmacodynamic biomarkers through network analysis of target-disease relationships.

- **[A myocardial reorientation method based on feature point detection for quantitative analysis of PET myocardial perfusion imaging.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40367541/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks

	- Summary: This paper develops a CNN for automatic reorientation of cardiac PET images to enable quantitative analysis of myocardial perfusion imaging.

- **[Machine Learning Modeling for Codonopsis Radix Quality Assessment Integrating Efficacy, Chemical Composition, and Macroscopic Traits.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41750843/)**

## Enrichment design

- **[Computational design of clinical trials using a combination of simulation and the genetic algorithm.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36793239/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2023Apr
	- Summary: This paper uses genetic algorithms to computationally optimize clinical trial designs, specifically blood sampling schedules for bioequivalence studies and dose group allocation.

- **[AI-NLME: A New Artificial Intelligence-Driven Nonlinear Mixed Effect Modeling Approach for Analyzing Longitudinal Data in Randomized Placebo-Controlled Clinical Trials.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40898887/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2025Sep
	- Summary: Proposes AI-NLME approach using artificial neural networks to estimate probability of non-specific treatment response for controlling confounding effects in clinical trials.

## Exposure–response analysis

- **[[Study on chromatography-efficacy relationship of anti-inflammatory activity of qizhi weitong particle compound herbs with neural network and gray correlation method].](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/24010300/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2013Jun
	- Summary: Neural networks and gray correlation analysis are used to establish chromatography-efficacy relationships for anti-inflammatory activity of traditional Chinese medicine compounds.

- **[Using Machine Learning To Define the Impact of Beta-Lactam Early and Cumulative Target Attainment on Outcomes in Intensive Care Unit Patients with Hospital-Acquired and Ventilator-Associated Pneumonia.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35699444/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2022Jul19
	- Summary: Machine learning is used alongside regression analyses to evaluate the impact of beta-lactam PK/PD parameters on therapy outcomes in ICU pneumonia patients.

- **[Machine learning predicts the serum PFOA and PFOS levels in pregnant women: Enhancement of fatty acid status on model performance.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38909401/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Feature selection
	- Published: 2024Aug
	- Summary: Machine learning models, particularly random forest, predict serum PFOA/PFOS levels in pregnant women using dietary, demographic, and fatty acid data as features.

- **[Effect of Cumulative Exposure on the Efficacy of Paroxetine: A Population Pharmacokinetic-Pharmacodynamic and Machine Learning Analyses.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40358139/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2025Jun
	- Summary: Machine learning models predict paroxetine treatment remission using PK/PD data, with SHAP values explaining feature contributions for depression outcome prediction.

- **[Applying exposure-response analysis to enhance Mycophenolate Mofetil dosing precision in pediatric patients with immune-mediated renal diseases by machine learning models.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40447059/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Aug01
	- Summary: Machine learning models are applied to develop pharmacokinetic models for mycophenolic acid and optimize MMF dosing in pediatric patients with immune-mediated renal diseases.

- **[Machine-Learning Exploration of Exposure-Effect Relationships of Cisplatin in Head and Neck Cancer Patients.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36432700/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2022Nov18
	- Summary: Machine learning is used to explore exposure-effect relationships of cisplatin in head and neck cancer patients to enable personalized dosing and optimize toxicity-efficacy ratios.

- **[Time-dependent AI-Modeling of the anticancer efficacy of synthesized gallic acid analogues.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30818108/)**
	- Methodology: Time-series modeling
	- Published: 2019Apr
	- Summary: AI modeling is used to map anticancer efficacy of gallic acid analogues across concentrations and exposure times to understand drug action mechanisms and response heterogeneity.

- **[Drug Concentration Thresholds Predictive of Therapy Failure and Death in Children With Tuberculosis: Bread Crumb Trails in Random Forests.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/27742636/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models
	- Published: 2016Nov01
	- Summary: Uses random forests to identify drug concentration thresholds predictive of therapy failure and death in pediatric tuberculosis patients for dose optimization.

- **[Optimizing Dosing Strategies in Cell Therapy With Machine Learning and Exposure-Response Integration.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41369984/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models
	- Published: 2026Jan-Feb
	- Summary: The paper proposes a random forest-based seamless phase I/II design that integrates toxicity, efficacy, and cellular kinetics data for optimal dose selection in cell therapy.

- **[Application of machine learning based methods in exposure-response analysis.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35275315/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2022Aug
	- Summary: This simulation study evaluates ML methods for exposure-response analysis using propensity score estimation and neural networks to improve confounding adjustment and E-R relationship estimation.

- **[Artificial intelligence-derived 3-Way Concentration-dependent Antagonism of Gatifloxacin, Pyrazinamide, and Rifampicin During Treatment of Pulmonary Tuberculosis.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30496458/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2018Nov28
	- Summary: AI is used to model 3-way concentration-dependent drug antagonism between gatifloxacin, pyrazinamide, and rifampicin to understand PK variability's role in TB treatment outcomes.

- **[The shape of low-concentration dose-response functions for benzene: implications for human health risk assessment.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33853483/)**
	- Methodology: Explainable AI
	- Published: 2021Feb
	- Summary: The paper uses partial dependence plots to analyze benzene exposure-metabolite relationships and characterize dose-response function shapes for risk assessment.

## Model selection

- **[Artificial Neural Networks approach to pharmacokinetic model selection in DCE-MRI studies.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/27876537/)**

- **[Radiation dose optimization potential of deep learning-based reconstruction for multiphase hepatic CT: A clinical and phantom study.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35381567/)**
	- Published: 2022Jun
	- Summary: not_AI_ML

	- Published: 2012Mar

	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning

	- Methodology: Deep learning

	- Summary: This paper evaluates a deep learning image reconstruction algorithm for CT imaging to assess image quality improvements and potential dose reduction compared to hybrid iterative reconstruction.

	- Methodology: LLM

	- Summary: This study evaluates the accuracy of AI chatbots (LLMs) in performing pharmaceutical calculations for pharmacy education and practice.

	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2022Jul30
	- Summary: Deep learning models were trained to identify anatomical landmarks (lung apices and bases) in chest CT scans to classify optimal anatomic coverage and detect under/over-scanning.

- **[Treatment of reverse-osmosis concentrate of printing and dyeing wastewater by electro-oxidation process with controlled oxidation-reduction potential (ORP).](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/29547852/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks

	- Summary: The paper uses a back-propagation neural network model with ORP monitoring to optimize electro-oxidation process for wastewater treatment, reducing energy costs by 24-29%.

## Outcome prediction

- **[Deep learning prediction of patient response time course from early data via neural-pharmacokinetic/pharmacodynamic modelling.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40271424/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Neural networks, Time-series modeling, Mechanism-informed machine learning
	- Published: 2021Aug
	- Summary: Neural-PK/PD framework combines deep learning with pharmacological principles using neural ODEs to predict patient response time courses and simulate untested dosing regimens.

- **[Computer-aided diagnosis of prostate cancer in the peripheral zone using multiparametric MRI.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/22640958/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2012Jun21
	- Summary: The paper develops a computer-aided diagnosis system using supervised learning classifiers (SVM, LDA, k-NN, naive Bayes) with feature selection to predict prostate cancer presence in MRI.

- **[Toward a Combinatorial Approach for the Prediction of IgG Half-Life and Clearance.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30232177/)**
	- Methodology: Feature selection, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2018Dec
	- Summary: Uses LASSO regression for feature selection to identify optimal combinations of biophysical parameters that predict monoclonal antibody half-life and clearance.

- **[Machine Learning-Based Prediction of Escitalopram and Sertraline Side Effects With Pharmacokinetic Data in Children and Adolescents.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38297828/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024Apr
	- Summary: Machine learning models, specifically penalized logistic regression, were developed to predict SSRI side effects in pediatric patients using pharmacokinetic data and electronic health records.

- **[Virtual patient analysis identifies strategies to improve the performance of predictive biomarkers for PD-1 blockade.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39467131/)**
	- Methodology: Feature selection, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2024Nov05
	- Summary: Uses ML-based feature selection with quantitative systems pharmacology modeling to identify and optimize predictive biomarkers for PD-1 blockade response in TNBC patients.

- **[Prediction of polyreactive and nonspecific single-chain fragment variables through structural biochemical features and protein language-based descriptors.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36471239/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, LLM
	- Published: 2022Dec05
	- Summary: Uses protein language models and structural features to predict polyreactivity of single-chain antibody fragments for early drug developability assessment.

- **[Data-Driven Discovery of Mathematical and Physical Relations in Oncology Data Using Human-Understandable Machine Learning.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34901835/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2021
	- Summary: The paper introduces a neural network system that discovers human-understandable mathematical relations in oncology data to model cancer progression and tumor-host-drug interactions.

- **[A Systems-Based Analysis of Mono- and Combination Therapy for Carbapenem-Resistant Klebsiella pneumoniae Bloodstream Infections.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36125299/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models
	- Published: 2022Oct18
	- Summary: Random Forest was used to rank variables important for predicting 30-day mortality in carbapenem-resistant Klebsiella pneumoniae bloodstream infections treated with antimicrobial therapies.

- **[Hybrid Machine Learning Method and Standard Data Analysis Approaches for Predicting Treatment Outcomes of Cardiovascular Diseases: A Randomized Controlled Trial.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41773358/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2026Mar-Apr
	- Summary: This RCT compares hybrid ML methods (combining random forest, SVM, linear regression) vs standard approaches for predicting cardiovascular disease treatment outcomes.

- **[HINT: Hierarchical interaction network for clinical-trial-outcome predictions.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35465223/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2022Apr08
	- Summary: HINT uses deep learning with hierarchical interaction networks to predict clinical trial outcomes by encoding multi-modal data including drug molecules, diseases, and trial criteria.

- **[Estimation of biliary excretion of foreign compounds using properties of molecular structure.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/24202722/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2014Jan
	- Summary: The paper develops QSAR models using regression trees, random forest, and boosted trees to predict biliary excretion of compounds based on molecular structure properties.

- **[Explaining transmission rate variations and forecasting epidemic spread in multiple regions with a semiparametric mixed effects SIR model.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37431147/)**
	- Methodology: Gaussian processes, Bayesian ML, Time-series modeling
	- Published: 2023Dec
	- Summary: The paper uses Gaussian processes and Bayesian methods to model time-varying transmission rates and forecast epidemic spread across multiple regions.

- **[Leveraging AI for facioscapulohumeral muscular dystrophy prediction and omics biomarker identification.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41256551/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Feature selection
	- Published: 2025Oct03
	- Summary: This paper develops ML models using Random Forest and SVM-RFE to predict FSHD from multi-omics data and identify key biomarker features for disease classification.

- **[The cortical neurophysiological signature of amyotrophic lateral sclerosis.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38779353/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models
	- Published: 2024
	- Summary: Uses random forest regression to predict disease severity from magnetoencephalography metrics in amyotrophic lateral sclerosis patients for biomarker development.

- **[Radiomic Analysis of Pharmacokinetic Heterogeneity Within Tumor Based on the Unsupervised Decomposition of Dynamic Contrast-Enhanced MRI for Predicting Histological Characteristics of Breast Cancer.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34773446/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2022Jun
	- Summary: Uses unsupervised decomposition of dynamic contrast-enhanced MRI to analyze pharmacokinetic heterogeneity within breast tumors for predicting histological characteristics.

- **[Machine Learning Application in a Phase I Clinical Trial Allows for the Identification of Clinical-Biomolecular Markers Significantly Associated With Toxicity.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34905217/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Model selection
	- Published: 2022Mar
	- Summary: Random Forest and other ML algorithms were trained to predict dose-limiting toxicity in phase I clinical trial using clinical and biomolecular baseline data.

- **[Optimizing vancomycin dosing in pediatrics: a machine learning approach to predict trough concentrations in children under four years of age.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38861047/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024Oct
	- Summary: Machine learning is used to predict vancomycin trough concentrations in pediatric patients under four years to optimize dosing regimens.

- **[Using Machine Learning To Define the Impact of Beta-Lactam Early and Cumulative Target Attainment on Outcomes in Intensive Care Unit Patients with Hospital-Acquired and Ventilator-Associated Pneumonia.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35699444/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2022Jul19
	- Summary: Machine learning is used alongside regression analyses to evaluate the impact of beta-lactam PK/PD parameters on therapy outcomes in ICU pneumonia patients.

- **[Prediction of Bispectral Index during Target-controlled Infusion of Propofol and Remifentanil: A Deep Learning Approach.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/28953500/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2018Mar
	- Summary: Deep learning approach is used to predict bispectral index during target-controlled infusion of propofol and remifentanil for improved anesthesia monitoring.

- **[Explorations into modeling human oral bioavailability.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/18603330/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2008Nov
	- Summary: The paper uses Kohonen's self-organizing neural networks for dataset partitioning and multiple linear regression to predict human oral bioavailability of drug compounds.

- **[Nephrocast-V: A Deep Learning Model for the Prediction of Vancomycin Trough Concentration Using Electronic Health Record Data.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41025800/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2026Jan
	- Summary: A deep learning model is developed to predict vancomycin trough concentrations using electronic health record data to optimize therapeutic dosing.

- **[Structure-Based Prediction of Anti-infective Drug Concentrations in the Human Lung Epithelial Lining Fluid.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/26626793/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2016Apr
	- Summary: The paper develops a predictive model using chemical structure descriptors to predict antibiotic concentration ratios in lung epithelial lining fluid versus plasma.

- **[Multi-cohort, cross-species urinary proteomics reveals signatures of LRRK2 dysfunction in Parkinson's disease.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41611872/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2026Jan29
	- Summary: Machine learning was used to develop a 30-protein urinary panel that classifies LRRK2 G2019S carriers in Parkinson's disease with high accuracy across independent cohorts.

- **[Global Deep Forecasting with Patient-Specific Pharmacokinetics.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37965077/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Time-series modeling
	- Published: 2025Feb12
	- Summary: A hybrid global-local deep learning architecture with PK encoder forecasts blood glucose levels by incorporating patient-specific pharmacokinetic properties for improved accuracy.

- **[Supporting Machine Learning Model in the Treatment of Chronic Pain.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37509416/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2023Jun21
	- Summary: ML model predicts chronic pain treatment outcomes using cannabis based on patient genomic polymorphisms to enable personalized therapy and avoid ineffective treatments.

- **[Metabolomics and lipidomics predictor of survival in hepatocellular carcinoma patients receiving tyrosine kinase inhibitor and immune checkpoint inhibitor combination therapy.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41265377/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Feature selection, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2025Dec
	- Summary: Machine learning framework combining random forest, LASSO regression, and Cox modeling to identify metabolomic and lipidomic biomarkers predicting survival in HCC patients.

- **[Radiomics Based on Multimodal MRI for the Differential Diagnosis of Benign and Malignant Breast Lesions.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32061014/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2020Aug
	- Summary: The paper uses radiomics-based machine learning on multimodal MRI data to predict and differentiate between benign and malignant breast lesions.

- **[Estimation of passive gastrointestinal absorption of new dual DNA gyrase and topoisomerase IV inhibitors using PAMPA and biopartitioning micellar chromatography and quantitative structure-retention relationship analysis.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38776787/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024Jun01
	- Summary: The paper uses multiple linear regression, partial least squares, and support vector machines to predict passive gastrointestinal absorption of DNA gyrase inhibitors.

- **[Establishing a Pharmacoinformatics Repository of Approved Medicines: A Database to Support Drug Product Development.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39705554/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Unsupervised learning

	- Summary: The paper uses association rule learning and binary prediction models to analyze drug formulations and predict oral fraction absorbed from regulatory data.

- **[A translational multimodal machine-learning prototype predicting valproate response in epilepsy treatment.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40909818/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2025Aug27
	- Summary: Develops a multimodal machine learning classifier integrating genetic and in-vitro data to predict valproate treatment response in epilepsy patients for personalized treatment decisions.

- **[Leveraging Predictive Pharmacometrics-Based Algorithms to Enhance Perinatal Care-Application to Neonatal Jaundice.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36034866/)**
	- Methodology: Bayesian ML
	- Published: 2022
	- Summary: Develops a pharmacometrics-based algorithm using Empirical Bayesian Estimation to predict bilirubin kinetics in neonates for personalized jaundice management.

- **[A Comprehensive Analysis of Metabolomics and Transcriptomics Reveals Novel Biomarkers and Mechanistic Insights on Lorlatinib Crosses the Blood-Brain Barrier.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34497521/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Deep learning
	- Published: 2021
	- Summary: Uses multilayer perceptron neural networks to predict lorlatinib brain concentration based on metabolomic biomarkers, achieving >85% accuracy for brain penetration prediction.

- **[Single-cell peripheral immunoprofiling of Alzheimer's and Parkinson's diseases.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33239300/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2020Nov
	- Summary: Machine learning models were developed to predict Alzheimer's and Parkinson's diseases from single-cell immune profiling data to identify disease-specific biomarkers.

	- Methodology: Bayesian ML, Ensemble learning, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Feb04
	- Summary: Uses Bayesian optimization-guided docking and ensemble ML models to predict antibacterial efficacy and pharmacokinetics for tuberculosis drug discovery.

	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2022Nov07
	- Summary: This study integrates machine learning predictions into physiologically based pharmacokinetic models to predict clinical PK parameters for highly lipophilic compounds.

- **[Machine learning predicts the serum PFOA and PFOS levels in pregnant women: Enhancement of fatty acid status on model performance.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38909401/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Feature selection

	- Summary: Machine learning models, particularly random forest, predict serum PFOA/PFOS levels in pregnant women using dietary, demographic, and fatty acid data as features.

- **[Nose-to-Brain Drug Delivery and Physico-Chemical Properties of Nanosystems: Analysis and Correlation Studies of Data from Scientific Literature.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38882536/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024
	- Summary: Machine learning is used to correlate physicochemical properties of nanosystems with drug targeting efficiency and direct transport percentages for nose-to-brain delivery.

	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2022
	- Summary: Machine learning models were developed to predict artemisinin combination therapy treatment outcomes based on antibody profiles to Plasmodium antigens.

- **[Explainable cluster-based learning for prediction of postprandial glycemic events and insulin dose optimization in type 1 diabetes.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40956852/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Unsupervised learning, Tree-based models, Explainable AI, Ensemble learning
