	- Summary: Uses cluster-based ensemble ML with explainable AI to predict postprandial glycemic events and optimize insulin dosing in type 1 diabetes patients.

- **[DCE-MRI pharmacokinetic parameter maps for cervical carcinoma prediction.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32174312/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Deep learning
	- Published: 2020Mar
	- Summary: Machine learning approaches including SVMs and a novel transfer learning method (APITL) are used to predict cervical carcinoma from DCE-MRI pharmacokinetic parameters.

- **[Primary infliximab failure in pediatric colonic inflammatory bowel disease: Development of a proteomics predictive model using a prospective Canadian cohort.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41671043/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2026Feb11
	- Summary: The paper develops a serum proteomics-based predictive model using supervised learning to predict primary nonresponse to infliximab in pediatric inflammatory bowel disease.

- **[Tacrolimus in the treatment of childhood nephrotic syndrome: Machine learning detects novel biomarkers and predicts efficacy.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36521865/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2023Jan
	- Summary: Machine learning algorithms are used to identify biomarkers and develop prediction models for tacrolimus efficacy in childhood nephrotic syndrome patients.

- **[Machine learning reveals that Mycobacterium tuberculosis genotypes and anatomic disease site impacts drug resistance and disease transmission among patients with proven extra-pulmonary tuberculosis.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32736602/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2020Jul31
	- Summary: Machine learning is used to analyze how Mycobacterium tuberculosis genotypes and anatomic disease sites impact drug resistance and transmission patterns in extrapulmonary TB patients.

- **[A novel multi-target regression framework for time-series prediction of drug efficacy.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/28098186/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Neural networks, Time-series modeling
	- Published: 2017Jan18
	- Summary: A multi-target regression framework using neural networks and support vector regression for time-series prediction of traditional Chinese medicine efficacy from small pharmacokinetic samples.

- **[Association between salivary microbiota and tacrolimus pharmacokinetic variability in kidney transplant.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39426572/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models
	- Published: 2024Nov
	- Summary: Random forest model used to classify kidney transplant recipients based on tacrolimus pharmacokinetic variability using salivary microbiota composition as predictive features.

- **[DCE-MRI prediction of survival time for patients with glioblastoma multiforme: using an adaptive neuro-fuzzy-based model and nested model selection technique.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/28543885/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Model selection
	- Published: 2017Sep
	- Summary: Uses Adaptive Neuro-Fuzzy Inference System (ANFIS) with pharmacokinetic parameters from DCE-MRI to predict survival time in glioblastoma patients.

- **[A Transformer-Based Prediction Method for Depth of Anesthesia During Target-Controlled Infusion of Propofol and Remifentanil.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37581963/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Time-series modeling
	- Published: 2023
	- Summary: A transformer-based deep learning method using LSTM and attention mechanisms to predict depth of anesthesia (BIS) during propofol and remifentanil infusion.

- **[Application of fuzzy logic to predicting erythropoietic response in hemodialysis patients.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/19115195/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2008Dec
	- Summary: The paper applies fuzzy logic to pharmacodynamic modeling for predicting erythropoietic response to erythropoietin in hemodialysis patients with uncertainty representation.

- **[A genetic risk score is significantly associated with statin therapy response in the elderly population.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/27943270/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models, Feature selection
	- Published: 2017Mar
	- Summary: Random forest models were used to develop a weighted genetic risk score for predicting statin therapy response and LDL-C reduction in elderly populations.

- **[A patient-informed approach to predict iodinated-contrast media enhancement in the liver.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36265222/)**
	- Methodology: Time-series modeling, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2022Nov
	- Summary: The paper develops a patient-informed time series model integrating clinical data and pharmacokinetics to predict liver contrast enhancement in CT scans.

- **[QSRR modeling of the chromatographic retention behavior of some quinolone and sulfonamide antibacterial agents using firefly algorithm coupled to support vector machine.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36329493/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2022Nov03
	- Summary: Uses firefly algorithm with support vector regression to predict chromatographic retention behavior of quinolone and sulfonamide antibiotics through QSRR modeling.

- **[Machine learning and individual variability in electric field characteristics predict tDCS treatment response.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33049412/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2020Nov-Dec
	- Summary: Machine learning models predict working memory improvements from tDCS treatment using MRI-derived electric field characteristics for precision cognitive intervention.

- **[Exploring the Mechanism of Sempervirine Inhibiting Glioblastoma Invasion Based on Network Pharmacology and Bioinformatics.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39458959/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024Oct02
	- Summary: Machine learning is used to construct a clinical prognostic model from genomic data to predict glioblastoma outcomes and explore sempervirine's anti-invasion mechanism.

- **[A data-driven approach to prostate cancer detection from dynamic contrast enhanced MRI.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/25060941/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Unsupervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2015Apr
	- Summary: Uses PCA and LASSO feature selection with SVM classification for data-driven prostate cancer detection from DCE-MRI without pharmacokinetic modeling.

- **[Dynamic Contrast-enhanced MRI for Evaluating Breast Cancer Chemotherapy Response Using Conditional Generative Adversarial Networks.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41347917/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2026Jan
	- Summary: A conditional GAN is developed to rapidly translate DCE-MRI data to pharmacokinetic permeability maps for evaluating breast cancer chemotherapy response.

- **[Proteolysis-targeting Chimera efficacy prediction using a deep-learning-QSP model.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41521293/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Neural networks
	- Published: 2026Jan12
	- Summary: Deep learning models are integrated with QSP modeling to predict PROTAC efficacy parameters (DC50 and Dmax) for targeted protein degradation therapeutics.

- **[Temporal Feature Extraction from DCE-MRI to Identify Poorly Perfused Subvolumes of Tumors Related to Outcomes of Radiation Therapy in Head and Neck Cancer.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/28111634/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2016Dec
	- Summary: Uses SVM classification with temporal feature extraction from DCE-MRI to identify poorly perfused tumor subvolumes for predicting radiation therapy outcomes in head and neck cancer.

- **[Comparison of time-series models for predicting physiological metrics under sedation.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39470955/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Time-series modeling
	- Published: 2025Jun
	- Summary: Compares statistical and deep learning time-series models (LSTM, GRU, TCN, Transformers) for predicting bispectral index to assess sedation depth during surgery.

- **[Classification of breast mass lesions using model-based analysis of the characteristic kinetic curve derived from fuzzy c-means clustering.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/22245697/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2012Apr
	- Summary: Uses fuzzy c-means clustering to extract representative kinetic curves from breast MRI for automated classification of benign vs malignant tumors in a CAD system.

- **[Prediction of Thiopurine Metabolite Levels Based on Haematological and Biochemical Parameters.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31568041/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2019Oct
	- Summary: The paper builds predictive models using supervised learning to predict low thiopurine metabolite levels and nonadherence in pediatric IBD patients based on clinical parameters.

- **[Harnessing machine learning and multi-omics to explore tumor evolutionary characteristics and the role of AMOTL1 in prostate cancer.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39643184/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2025Jan
	- Summary: Machine learning integrated with multi-omics data to develop TECPI for predicting prostate cancer progression and guiding personalized treatment decisions.

- **[Radiogenomics Analysis Linking Multiparametric MRI and Transcriptomics in Prostate Cancer.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37370685/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2023Jun06
	- Summary: Machine learning models are trained on imaging and transcriptomic features to classify prostate cancer aggressiveness and identify correlations for personalized treatment.

- **[Predicting pharmacodynamic effects through early drug discovery with artificial intelligence-physiologically based pharmacokinetic (AI-PBPK) modelling.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38434709/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024
	- Summary: The paper presents an AI-PBPK platform that uses machine learning to predict PK/PD parameters and outcomes for drug compounds in early discovery stages.

- **[An integrated computational methodology with data-driven machine learning, molecular modeling and PBPK modeling to accelerate solid dispersion formulation design.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33301864/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2021Jan
	- Summary: The paper integrates machine learning (random forest) with molecular dynamics and PBPK modeling to predict dissolution profiles and accelerate solid dispersion formulation design.

- **[Efficient estimation of pharmacokinetic parameters from breast dynamic contrast-enhanced MRI based on a convolutional neural network for predicting molecular subtypes.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37983902/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2023Dec04
	- Summary: A convolutional neural network is developed to efficiently estimate pharmacokinetic parameters from breast MRI for predicting molecular subtypes in cancer patients.

- **[Serum Predose Metabolic Profiling for Prediction of Rosuvastatin Pharmacokinetic Parameters in Healthy Volunteers.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34867363/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2021
	- Summary: Machine learning is used to identify endogenous metabolites in predose samples that can predict rosuvastatin pharmacokinetic parameters for personalized drug therapy optimization.

- **[A deep neural network: mechanistic hybrid model to predict pharmacokinetics in rat.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38294570/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Neural networks
	- Published: 2024Jan31
	- Summary: A hybrid deep neural network-mechanistic model is developed to predict pharmacokinetic parameters including oral and intravenous exposure in rats from chemical structure.

- **[Population Pharmacokinetic Modeling Combined With Machine Learning Approach Improved Tacrolimus Trough Concentration Prediction in Chinese Adult Liver Transplant Recipients.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36097320/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2023Mar
	- Summary: XGBoost machine learning is combined with population pharmacokinetic modeling to improve tacrolimus trough concentration prediction in liver transplant recipients.

- **[Molecular models of multiple sclerosis severity identify heterogeneity of pathogenic mechanisms.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36509784/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2022Dec12
	- Summary: Machine learning models using CSF biomarkers predict disability accumulation speed in MS patients and uncover molecular heterogeneity of pathogenic mechanisms.

- **[A Machine Learning Framework to Improve Rat Clearance Predictions and Inform Physiologically Based Pharmacokinetic Modeling.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37713584/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2023Oct02
	- Summary: Machine learning models are developed to predict rat intrinsic clearance for improved PBPK modeling, comparing ML approaches to traditional bottom-up methods.

- **[Machine Learning Prediction and Validation of Plasma Concentration-Time Profiles.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40345671/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Jun02
	- Summary: This study uses Random Forest models to predict plasma concentration-time profiles for remifentanil, demonstrating high accuracy on both virtual and real-world data sets.

- **[Numerical modeling and prediction of late estimated glomerular filtration rate in kidney transplant recipients based on machine learning models and the Monte Carlo simulation method.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41562636/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Surrogate modeling
	- Published: 2026Jan22
	- Summary: Machine learning models combined with Monte Carlo simulation are used to predict late estimated glomerular filtration rate in kidney transplant recipients on tacrolimus.

- **[Prediction of Intra-individual Variability in Bioequivalence Studies of 278 Formulations: Comprehensive Analysis Using Physicochemical and Pharmacokinetic Parameters.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40204499/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Unsupervised learning, Neural networks, Tree-based models, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2025
	- Summary: The paper uses neural networks, boosting trees, and K-means clustering to predict intra-individual variability in bioequivalence studies from physicochemical parameters.

- **[Predicting overall survival from tumor dynamics metrics using parametric statistical and machine learning models: application to patients with RET-altered solid tumors.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38919267/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024
	- Summary: Machine learning models are used to predict overall survival from tumor dynamics metrics in a pan-indication approach for RET-altered solid tumor patients.

- **[In silico prediction of volume of distribution of drugs in man using conformal prediction performs on par with animal data-based models.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34845977/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2021Dec
	- Summary: Machine learning with conformal prediction is used to predict human volume of distribution at steady state, comparing performance against animal-based scaling methods.

- **[Neural network-longitudinal assessment of the Electronic Anti-Retroviral THerapy (EARTH) cohort to follow response to HIV-treatment.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/16779050/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Time-series modeling
	- Published: 2005
	- Summary: Uses Jordan-Elman neural networks to longitudinally model drug-virus-host interactions and predict treatment success/failure in HIV patients over 20 years of follow-up data.

- **[Development and validation of a deep learning-based model to predict response and survival of T790M mutant non-small cell lung cancer patients in early clinical phase trials using electronic medical record and pharmacokinetic data.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38736496/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2024Apr29
	- Summary: Deep learning model using mixture-of-experts and Cox algorithm to predict treatment response and survival in T790M mutant NSCLC patients using EMR and PK data.

- **[Artificial Intelligence-Driven Prediction Revealed CFTR Associated with Therapy Outcome of Breast Cancer: A Feasibility Study.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39025053/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024
	- Summary: AI-driven predictors classify genomic variants to identify genes associated with breast cancer therapy outcomes for precision medicine applications.

- **[Model-based prediction of progression-free survival for combination therapies in oncology.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37300376/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2023Sep
	- Summary: Uses machine learning-guided analysis to reduce unexplained variability in joint models predicting progression-free survival for oncology combination therapies.

	- Summary: Machine learning models predict paroxetine treatment remission using PK/PD data, with SHAP values explaining feature contributions for depression outcome prediction.

- **[In Silico Models of Human PK Parameters. Prediction of Volume of Distribution Using an Extensive Data Set and a Reduced Number of Parameters.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32891631/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models, Feature selection
	- Published: 2021Jan
	- Summary: Random forest model with six descriptors predicts human volume of distribution at steady-state for drug development and compound design.

- **[Classification of dynamic contrast enhanced MR images of cervical cancers using texture analysis and support vector machines.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/24802069/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection

	- Methodology: Deep learning

	- Summary: The paper uses graph transformer deep learning to predict lymphatic transport potential of drugs based on limited historical in vivo data from 185 compounds.

	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning, Supervised learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2025Feb21
	- Summary: Uses multiomics clustering to identify patient subgroups, then applies ML with mechanistic modeling to predict anti-IFNα antibody treatment responses in virtual lupus patients.

	- Summary: Uses radiomics features from multiparametric MRI and multivariate logistic regression to predict endpoint biopsy positivity in prostate cancer patients after radiotherapy.

	- Summary: Meta-GAT uses graph attention networks with meta learning to predict molecular properties for drug discovery in low-data scenarios through bilevel optimization.

- **[Pan-cancer analysis of CDC7 in human tumors: Integrative multi-omics insights and discovery of novel marine-based inhibitors through machine learning and computational approaches.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40120182/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning

	- Summary: Machine learning is used to screen marine-derived compounds for CDC7 inhibitors and predict their toxicity, combined with pan-cancer analysis of CDC7 as a therapeutic target.

	- Summary: The paper uses random forest algorithm to establish QSAR models for predicting anti-gastric cancer activity of bisamide curcumin analogues.

	- Summary: Uses machine learning-based feature selection and cutoff-based algorithms to identify and improve predictive biomarkers for PD-1 blockade response in TNBC patients.

	- Methodology: Supervised learning

	- Methodology: Supervised learning

	- Summary: Machine learning model predicts dendrimer pharmacokinetic parameters from physicochemical properties using curated database to guide drug vector design.

	- Summary: Uses multiple linear regression, cluster analysis, and random forest regression to predict drug penetration across blood-brain and blood-milk barriers using chromatographic descriptors.

	- Summary: The paper uses linear discriminant analysis and supervised machine learning classifiers to predict neural inertia from EEG spectral features in mice under anesthesia.

	- Methodology: Explainable AI

	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Explainable AI

	- Summary: The paper uses deep learning to predict synthesizability of stereochemically diverse heterocycles based on high-throughput experimental data from 1152 reactions.

	- Methodology: Supervised learning

	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2025Sep
	- Summary: Deep learning is used for pharmacokinetic quantification of DCE MRI to predict pathologic complete response to neoadjuvant chemotherapy in breast cancer patients.

- **[Novel clinical phenotypes, drug categorization, and outcome prediction in drug-induced cholestasis: Analysis of a database of 432 patients developed by literature review and machine learning support.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38574623/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2024May
	- Summary: Machine learning is used to stratify drug-induced cholestasis patients, categorize drugs, and develop prognostic models to identify high-risk patients and concerning drugs.

- **[Big Data Toolsets to Pharmacometrics: Application of Machine Learning for Time-to-Event Analysis.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/29536640/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2018May
	- Summary: This paper compares machine learning methods to Cox regression for time-to-event analysis using simulated data, showing ML outperforms in high-dimensional scenarios.

- **[TuNa-AI: A Hybrid Kernel Machine To Design Tunable Nanoparticles for Drug Delivery.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40934473/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2025Sep23
	- Summary: Develops a hybrid kernel machine combining molecular features and compositional data to predict nanoparticle formulation outcomes and optimize drug delivery systems.

- **[Use of classification and regression tree (CART), to identify hemoglobin A1C (HbA1C) cut-off thresholds predictive of poor tuberculosis treatment outcomes and associated risk factors.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31720385/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models
	- Published: 2018May
	- Summary: Uses CART machine learning algorithm to identify HbA1C cut-off thresholds predictive of poor tuberculosis treatment outcomes in TB-diabetes co-affected patients.

- **[Machine learning driven bioequivalence risk assessment at an early stage of generic drug development.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39490606/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024Dec
	- Summary: Machine learning is used to predict bioequivalence risk in generic drug development using pharmacokinetic and physicochemical drug characteristics as predictors.

- **[Multimodal profiling of Pepcan-CB1 receptor structure-activity relationships: integrating molecular dynamics simulations, biological profiling, and the deep learning model MuMoPepcan.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41005111/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2025Oct
	- Summary: Deep learning model MuMoPepcan integrates molecular dynamics simulations with experimental data to predict pepcan peptide bioactivity for CB1 receptor drug discovery.

- **[Artificial neural network and bioavailability of the immunosuppression drug.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32452906/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Tree-based models
	- Published: 2020Aug
	- Summary: The paper uses artificial neural networks and CART to predict organ transplant success by analyzing bioavailability of immunosuppression drugs and multiple clinical variables.

- **[Effects of processing parameters on particle size of ultrasound prepared chitosan nanoparticles: an Artificial Neural Networks Study.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/22681416/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2012Sep-Oct
	- Summary: This study uses artificial neural networks to model and predict the particle size of chitosan nanoparticles based on processing parameters like concentration, pH, and sonication conditions.

- **[Classification of signal-time curves from dynamic MR mammography by neural networks.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/11295347/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2001Jan
	- Summary: Neural networks are used to classify signal-time curves from dynamic MR mammography to distinguish between malignant and benign breast lesions.

- **[A simulation-based framework for modeling and prediction of personalized blood pressure trajectories in hypertensive patients after antihypertensive treatment.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40209154/)**
	- Methodology: Gaussian processes
	- Published: 2025
	- Summary: The paper develops a simulation framework for blood pressure trajectories using PK/PD modeling and evaluates Gaussian processes for predicting steady-state blood pressure under treatment.

- **[Methods to Develop an in silico Clinical Trial: Computational Head-to-Head Comparison of Lisdexamfetamine and Methylphenidate.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34803764/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2021
	- Summary: The paper develops an in silico clinical trial comparing ADHD treatments using PBPK/QSP models combined with AI methods for data analysis and mechanistic predictions.

- **[Analytical and machine learning approaches identify a sea star steroid with promising activity for COVID-19 therapeutic development.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41057530/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Oct07
	- Summary: The paper develops a machine learning-based web application to predict IC50 values of SARS-CoV-2 inhibitors for drug discovery screening.

- **[Deep exploration of random forest model boosts the interpretability of machine learning studies of complicated immune responses and lung burden of nanoparticles.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34039604/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models, Feature selection, Explainable AI, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2021May
	- Summary: The paper develops TBRFA, a random forest-based framework with enhanced interpretability to predict nanoparticle immune responses and lung burden while revealing feature interactions.

- **[Fractal fingerprinting of chromatographic profiles based on wavelet analysis and its application to characterize the quality grade of medicinal herbs.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/14632446/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2003Nov-Dec
	- Summary: Uses artificial neural networks and PCA to predict quality grades of medicinal herbs based on fractal fingerprints extracted from chromatographic profiles via wavelet analysis.

- **[An innovative supervised longitudinal learning procedure of recurrent neural networks with temporal data augmentation: Insights from predicting fetal macrosomia and large-for-gestational age.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38820775/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Time-series modeling
	- Published: 2024Jul
	- Summary: The paper develops a supervised longitudinal learning procedure using recurrent neural networks with temporal data augmentation to predict fetal macrosomia and large-for-gestational age.

- **[A Long-term Co-perfused Disseminated Tuberculosis-3D Liver Hollow Fiber Model for Both Drug Efficacy and Hepatotoxicity in Babies.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/27211555/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2016Apr
	- Summary: AI algorithms were used to confirm that pyrazinamide was not predictive of good clinical outcomes in children ≤6 years with extrapulmonary tuberculosis.

- **[Improving the potency prediction for chemically modified siRNAs through insights from molecular modeling of individual sequence positions.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40171444/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Mar11
	- Summary: Machine learning is used to predict siRNA biological activity by correlating molecular modeling properties with experimental potency data for rational drug design.

- **[Prediction of Oral Pharmacokinetics Using a Combination of In Silico Descriptors and In Vitro ADME Properties.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33512165/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Gaussian processes, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2021Mar01
	- Summary: This study uses random forest regression and Gaussian processes to predict oral pharmacokinetics, combining in silico descriptors with in vitro ADME properties for improved AUC prediction.

- **[Evaluation of an in vitro in vivo correlation for nebulizer delivery using artificial neural networks.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/17630647/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2007Dec
	- Summary: This paper uses artificial neural networks to develop predictive models correlating in vitro nebulizer characteristics to in vivo pharmacokinetic outcomes for salbutamol.

- **[Machine-learning and mechanistic modeling of metastatic breast cancer after neoadjuvant treatment.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38701089/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024May
	- Summary: The paper combines mechanistic kinetics-pharmacodynamics modeling with machine learning to predict neoadjuvant treatment response and metastatic progression in breast cancer.

- **[Neuro-fuzzy models as an IVIVR tool and their applicability in generic drug development.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/24477942/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2014Mar
	- Summary: Neuro-fuzzy models are used to predict pharmacokinetic parameter ratios for bioequivalence studies as an alternative IVIVR tool in generic drug development.

- **[Machine learning-optimized non-invasive brain stimulation and treatment response classification for major depression.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39473014/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024Oct30
	- Summary: Machine learning is used to optimize personalized tDCS dosage for depression treatment and classify treatment response by accounting for individual anatomical differences.

- **[A Comprehensive Study on Nanoparticle Drug Delivery to the Brain: Application of Machine Learning Techniques.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38060692/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024Jan01
	- Summary: Machine learning techniques including linear mixed-effect models are used to predict nanoparticle drug delivery to the brain based on physicochemical properties.

- **[Fitness Landscape for Antibodies 2: Benchmarking Reveals That Protein AI Models Cannot Yet Consistently Predict Developability Properties.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41497662/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Dec27
	- Summary: This paper benchmarks 30 AI models including protein language models on predicting antibody developability properties using the largest public therapeutic antibody dataset (FLAb2).

- **[Effect of Genetic Polymorphisms on the Pharmacokinetics of Deferasirox in Healthy Chinese Subjects and an Artificial Neural Networks Model for Pharmacokinetic Prediction.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32930952/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2020Dec
	- Summary: The paper uses artificial neural networks to construct a pharmacokinetic prediction model for deferasirox based on physiologic factors and genetic polymorphism data.

- **[Random Forest Model Prediction of Compound Oral Exposure in the Mouse.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33615183/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2021Feb12
	- Summary: Random forest machine learning model is developed to predict oral exposure of compounds in mouse pharmacokinetic assays for drug discovery optimization.

- **[The Comparison of Machine Learning and Mechanistic In Vitro-In Vivo Extrapolation Models for the Prediction of Human Intrinsic Clearance.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37812508/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2023Nov06
	- Summary: This study compares machine learning models with mechanistic IVIVE models for predicting human intrinsic clearance using structural descriptors and in vitro measurements.

- **[Type 1 diabetes prevention clinical trial simulator: Case reports of model-informed drug development tool.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38961520/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models
	- Published: 2024Aug
	- Summary: The paper develops a clinical trial simulation tool for type 1 diabetes prevention using ctree machine learning algorithms to generate virtual populations for trial design optimization.

- **[Machine learning model for predicting physical activity related bleeding risk in Chinese boys with haemophilia A.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37931538/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2023Dec
	- Summary: Machine learning model developed to predict bleeding risk during physical activity in Chinese boys with haemophilia A for improved patient management.

- **[Simulation-based comparison of Biopharmaceutics Classification System and drug structure.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32295687/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2020Apr06
	- Summary: Uses support vector machines and information gain-based feature selection to predict BCS classification from molecular fingerprints and identify structural characteristics.

- **[Predicting Serum Levels of Lithium-Treated Patients: A Supervised Machine Learning Approach.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34829787/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2021Oct28
	- Summary: Machine learning models predict lithium serum concentrations using real-world clinical data to improve therapeutic drug monitoring and dosing decisions.

- **[Predictive Modeling Identifies Total Bleeds at 12-Weeks Postswitch to N8-GP Prophylaxis as a Predictor of Treatment Response.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34865209/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2022Jun
	- Summary: Uses supervised machine learning to predict long-term annualized bleeding rate during FVIII prophylaxis for severe hemophilia A patients.

- **[Integrating artificial intelligence and physiologically based pharmacokinetic modeling to predict in vitro and in vivo fate of amorphous solid dispersions.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40825400/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Oct10
	- Summary: This paper integrates AI with PBPK modeling using TabPFN to predict molecular dissolution and systemic absorption of amorphous solid dispersions for drug development.

- **[Computer-aided diagnosis of prostate cancer using multi-parametric MRI: comparison between PUN and Tofts models.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/29570456/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2018May01
	- Summary: The paper uses support vector machine classifiers to create voxel-wise malignancy likelihood maps for prostate cancer diagnosis from multi-parametric MRI data.

- **[Comparison of statistical methods for predicting penetration capacity of drugs into human breast milk using physicochemical, pharmacokinetic and chromatographic descriptors.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32627677/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models
	- Published: 2020Jun
	- Summary: The paper compares MLR, PLS, and random forest regression methods to predict drug penetration into human breast milk using physicochemical and pharmacokinetic descriptors.

- **[Using classification structure pharmacokinetic relationship (SCPR) method to predict drug bioavailability based on grid-search support vector machine.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/17920387/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2007Oct10
	- Summary: The paper uses grid-search support vector machine and linear discriminant analysis to predict drug bioavailability from molecular descriptors, comparing classification models.

- **[Machine learning-guided covariate selection for time-to-event models developed from a small sample of real-world patients receiving bevacizumab treatment.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35851999/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Feature selection, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2022Oct
	- Summary: Machine learning methods including LASSO, survival trees, and random forests are used to select important covariates for time-to-event models predicting survival outcomes in cancer patients.

- **[Good oral absorption prediction on non-nucleoside benzothiadiazine dioxide human cytomegalovirus inhibitors using combined chromatographic and neuronal network techniques.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/15780633/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2005Apr01
	- Summary: Neural networks (CODES) combined with chromatographic techniques are used to predict oral absorption and bioavailability of benzothiadiazine dioxide derivatives as HCMV inhibitors.

## Patient risk stratification / management

- **[Computer-aided diagnosis of prostate cancer in the peripheral zone using multiparametric MRI.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/22640958/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2012Jun21
	- Summary: The paper develops a computer-aided diagnosis system using supervised learning classifiers (SVM, LDA, k-NN, naive Bayes) with feature selection to predict prostate cancer presence in MRI.

- **[An A.I. classifier derived from 4D radiomics of dynamic contrast-enhanced breast MRI data: potential to avoid unnecessary breast biopsies.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33744990/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2021Aug
	- Summary: The paper develops an AI classifier using 4D radiomics from breast MRI to distinguish benign from malignant lesions and reduce unnecessary biopsies.

- **[Bayesian algorithm implementation in a real time exposure assessment model on benzene with calculation of associated cancer risks.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/22399936/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Bayesian ML
	- Published: 2009
	- Summary: The paper develops ANNs with Bayesian regularization to predict benzene exposure and uses Bayesian algorithms in PBPK models to assess leukemia risk for filling station employees.

- **[Hybrid Machine Learning Method and Standard Data Analysis Approaches for Predicting Treatment Outcomes of Cardiovascular Diseases: A Randomized Controlled Trial.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41773358/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2026Mar-Apr
	- Summary: This RCT compares hybrid ML methods (combining random forest, SVM, linear regression) vs standard approaches for predicting cardiovascular disease treatment outcomes.

- **[Development of an artificial intelligence-enhanced warfarin interaction checker platform.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40127068/)**
	- Methodology: LLM
	- Published: 2025Mar
	- Summary: Development of a bilingual AI web application using GPT-4 API to identify potential warfarin drug interactions through text, picture, or voice input for patient safety.

- **[Artificial intelligence modelling of tyrosine kinase inhibitors at risk of malabsorption and bioavailability-enhancing strategies.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40625205/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Nov
	- Summary: AI model developed to predict tyrosine kinase inhibitors at risk of malabsorption in patients with specific conditions and identify bioavailability enhancement strategies.

- **[A PopPBPK-RL approach for precision dosing of benazepril in renal impaired patients.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39663294/)**
	- Methodology: Reinforcement learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Feature selection
	- Published: 2024Dec11
	- Summary: The paper develops a hybrid PBPK-reinforcement learning system for precision dosing of benazepril in renal impaired patients using patient stratification and feature selection.

- **[Global Deep Forecasting with Patient-Specific Pharmacokinetics.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37965077/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Time-series modeling
	- Published: 2025Feb12
	- Summary: A hybrid global-local deep learning architecture with PK encoder forecasts blood glucose levels by incorporating patient-specific pharmacokinetic properties for improved accuracy.

- **[Metabolomics and lipidomics predictor of survival in hepatocellular carcinoma patients receiving tyrosine kinase inhibitor and immune checkpoint inhibitor combination therapy.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41265377/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Feature selection, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2025Dec
	- Summary: Machine learning framework combining random forest, LASSO regression, and Cox modeling to identify metabolomic and lipidomic biomarkers predicting survival in HCC patients.

- **[Explainable cluster-based learning for prediction of postprandial glycemic events and insulin dose optimization in type 1 diabetes.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40956852/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Unsupervised learning, Tree-based models, Explainable AI, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2025Sep
	- Summary: Uses cluster-based ensemble ML with explainable AI to predict postprandial glycemic events and optimize insulin dosing in type 1 diabetes patients.

- **[Association between salivary microbiota and tacrolimus pharmacokinetic variability in kidney transplant.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39426572/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models
	- Published: 2024Nov
	- Summary: Random forest model used to classify kidney transplant recipients based on tacrolimus pharmacokinetic variability using salivary microbiota composition as predictive features.

- **[Classification Tree Analysis Based On Machine Learning for Predicting Linezolid-Induced Thrombocytopenia.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33609520/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models
	- Published: 2021May
	- Summary: CART analysis is used to predict linezolid-induced thrombocytopenia by identifying cutoff values for platelet reduction and drug concentration at 96 hours post-dose.

- **[DCE-MRI prediction of survival time for patients with glioblastoma multiforme: using an adaptive neuro-fuzzy-based model and nested model selection technique.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/28543885/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Model selection
	- Published: 2017Sep
	- Summary: Uses Adaptive Neuro-Fuzzy Inference System (ANFIS) with pharmacokinetic parameters from DCE-MRI to predict survival time in glioblastoma patients.

- **[Infliximab monitoring in Crohn's disease: a neural network approach for evaluating disease activity and immunogenicity.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39664232/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2024
	- Summary: This paper uses neural networks to evaluate disease activity and immunogenicity in Crohn's disease patients receiving infliximab for therapeutic drug monitoring.

- **[Effect of dexamethasone pretreatment using deep learning on the surgical effect of patients with gastrointestinal tumors.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39018292/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2024
	- Summary: Deep learning-based anesthesia monitoring system is used to improve surgical outcomes and patient management in gastrointestinal tumor surgery compared to standard care.

- **[A genetic risk score is significantly associated with statin therapy response in the elderly population.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/27943270/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models, Feature selection
	- Published: 2017Mar
	- Summary: Random forest models were used to develop a weighted genetic risk score for predicting statin therapy response and LDL-C reduction in elderly populations.

- **[Risk Prediction of Liver Injury in Pediatric Tuberculosis Treatment: Development of an Automated Machine Learning Model.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39830784/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025
	- Summary: The paper develops an automated machine learning model to predict the risk of anti-tuberculosis drug-induced liver injury in pediatric patients.

- **[Contribution of machine learning to tumor growth inhibition modeling for hepatocellular carcinoma patients under Roblitinib (FGF401) drug treatment.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35728123/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2022Aug
	- Summary: Machine learning with elastic net modeling is combined with population PK/PD modeling to identify predictive factors and create a risk score for tumor growth inhibition in hepatocellular carcinoma patients.

- **[Advanced gastrointestinal stromal tumor: reliable classification of imatinib plasma trough concentration via machine learning.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38402382/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024Feb24
	- Summary: Machine learning is used to develop a classification model for predicting imatinib plasma trough concentrations in Chinese GIST patients to optimize treatment monitoring.

- **[Applying Machine Learning for Prescriptive Support: A Use Case with Unfractionated Heparin in Intensive Care Units.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39176549/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2024Aug22
	- Summary: Machine learning models (random forest and XGBoost) predict heparin over/under-dosing in ICU patients to support clinical decision-making for dose optimization.

- **[Temporal Feature Extraction from DCE-MRI to Identify Poorly Perfused Subvolumes of Tumors Related to Outcomes of Radiation Therapy in Head and Neck Cancer.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/28111634/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2016Dec
	- Summary: Uses SVM classification with temporal feature extraction from DCE-MRI to identify poorly perfused tumor subvolumes for predicting radiation therapy outcomes in head and neck cancer.

- **[Classification of breast mass lesions using model-based analysis of the characteristic kinetic curve derived from fuzzy c-means clustering.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/22245697/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2012Apr
	- Summary: Uses fuzzy c-means clustering to extract representative kinetic curves from breast MRI for automated classification of benign vs malignant tumors in a CAD system.

- **[Harnessing machine learning and multi-omics to explore tumor evolutionary characteristics and the role of AMOTL1 in prostate cancer.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39643184/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection

	- Summary: Machine learning integrated with multi-omics data to develop TECPI for predicting prostate cancer progression and guiding personalized treatment decisions.

- **[Radiogenomics Analysis Linking Multiparametric MRI and Transcriptomics in Prostate Cancer.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37370685/)**

	- Summary: Machine learning models are trained on imaging and transcriptomic features to classify prostate cancer aggressiveness and identify correlations for personalized treatment.

- **[Model-Informed Dosing Regimen of Ticagrelor in Chinese Patients With Acute Coronary Syndrome.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37702259/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2023Dec
	- Summary: Eight ML methods including Random Forest were used to construct bleeding risk prediction models for ticagrelor dosing optimization in Chinese patients with acute coronary syndrome.

	- Methodology: Bayesian ML
	- Published: 2026Feb
	- Summary: Bayesian machine learning model is used to guide personalized anticoagulant dosing decisions by incorporating patient preferences for stroke, bleeding, and death risks.

- **[Neural network-longitudinal assessment of the Electronic Anti-Retroviral THerapy (EARTH) cohort to follow response to HIV-treatment.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/16779050/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Time-series modeling
	- Published: 2005
	- Summary: Uses Jordan-Elman neural networks to longitudinally model drug-virus-host interactions and predict treatment success/failure in HIV patients over 20 years of follow-up data.

- **[Developing a Warning Model of Potentially Inappropriate Medications in Older Chinese Outpatients in Tertiary Hospitals: A Machine-Learning Study.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37048702/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2023Mar30
	- Summary: Machine learning models using multilabel classification algorithms were developed to predict potentially inappropriate medications in older Chinese outpatients.

	- Methodology: Deep learning, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2024Apr29
	- Summary: Deep learning model using mixture-of-experts and Cox algorithm to predict treatment response and survival in T790M mutant NSCLC patients using EMR and PK data.

- **[Integrative Machine Learning Model Leveraging DCE-MRI and PSA Values for Advanced Risk Stratification in Prostate Cancer.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41203263/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Nov07
	- Summary: Machine learning model integrates DCE-MRI pharmacokinetic parameters with PSA values to predict ISUP grade metastatic risk groups for prostate cancer risk stratification.

	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning, Supervised learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2025Feb21
	- Summary: Uses multiomics clustering to identify patient subgroups, then applies ML with mechanistic modeling to predict anti-IFNα antibody treatment responses in virtual lupus patients.

- **[Item Response Modeling and Artificial Neural Network for Differentiation of Parkinson's Patients and Subjects Without Evidence of Dopaminergic Deficit.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40045658/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025May
	- Summary: The paper uses artificial neural networks and item response theory to classify Parkinson's disease patients versus subjects without dopaminergic deficits using MDS-UPDRS scores.

- **[Clinical decision support for chemotherapy-induced neutropenia using a hybrid pharmacodynamic/machine learning model.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37503916/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Bayesian ML

	- Summary: A hybrid PKPD/ML approach enriches machine learning models with pharmacodynamic predictions to improve chemotherapy-induced neutropenia risk prediction for clinical decision support.

	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Feature selection, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2022
	- Summary: Machine learning methods including logistic regression, elastic net, random forest, and SVM were used to develop a risk scoring system for predicting vancomycin-associated acute kidney injury.

- **[Biomarker discovery and drug repurposing in hepatocellular carcinoma through transcriptomics, machine learning, network pharmacology, and molecular dynamics.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41671946/)**
