	- Summary: Uses machine learning classifiers (XGBoost, Random Forest, SVM, Logistic Regression) with SHAP explainability to identify HCC biomarkers and explore drug repurposing opportunities.

- **[Cluster-Based Toxicity Estimation of Osteoradionecrosis Via Unsupervised Machine Learning: Moving Beyond Single Dose-Parameter Normal Tissue Complication Probability by Using Whole Dose-Volume Histograms for Cohort Risk Stratification.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38462018/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2024Aug01
	- Summary: Uses unsupervised machine learning to develop a cluster-based model for predicting osteoradionecrosis risk by analyzing whole dose-volume histograms for patient stratification.

- **[Models for depression recognition and efficacy assessment based on clinical and sequencing data.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39130405/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Feature selection, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024Jul30
	- Summary: This paper uses deep learning and feature selection on clinical and sequencing data to build models for depression diagnosis and treatment efficacy prediction.

- **[Impact of Machine Learning With Multiparametric Magnetic Resonance Imaging of the Breast for Early Prediction of Response to Neoadjuvant Chemotherapy and Survival Outcomes in Breast Cancer Patients.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30358693/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2019Feb
	- Summary: Machine learning is applied to multiparametric MRI data to predict pathological complete response to neoadjuvant chemotherapy and survival outcomes in breast cancer patients.

- **[Machine Learning Models of Early Longitudinal Toxicity Trajectories Predict Cetuximab Concentration and Metastatic Colorectal Cancer Survival in the Canadian Cancer Trials Group/AGITG CO.17/20 Trials.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40215447/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Apr
	- Summary: Machine learning models analyze early toxicity trajectories to predict cetuximab concentration and survival outcomes in metastatic colorectal cancer patients.

- **[Gut microbiota and metabolites related intra-patient variability of tacrolimus pharmacokinetics predicted adverse one-year outcomes following kidney transplantation.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40913864/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Nov14
	- Summary: Random forest model used to classify kidney transplant patients into high/low tacrolimus variability groups based on gut microbiota and metabolites to predict adverse outcomes.

- **[Effects of motion correction, sampling rate and parametric modelling in dynamic contrast enhanced MRI of the temporomandibular joint in children affected with juvenile idiopathic arthritis.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33359424/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2021Apr
	- Summary: Uses k-means clustering and random forest classification to analyze DCE-MRI data for optimizing diagnosis of juvenile idiopathic arthritis in children's temporomandibular joints.

- **[Molecular features of human pathological tau distinguish tauopathy-associated dementias.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41616780/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2026Feb05
	- Summary: Uses unsupervised and supervised ML to identify distinct molecular features of pathological tau protein for disease stratification and biomarker discovery across tauopathies.

- **[Enhancing Severe Neutropenia Prediction: PKPD-Informed Labeling for Machine Learning Models Trained on Real-World Data.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41208554/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2026Feb
	- Summary: PKPD-informed labeling strategy enhances machine learning models (logistic regression, XGBoost, TabPFN) for predicting docetaxel-induced severe neutropenia using real-world data.

- **[Novel clinical phenotypes, drug categorization, and outcome prediction in drug-induced cholestasis: Analysis of a database of 432 patients developed by literature review and machine learning support.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38574623/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2024May
	- Summary: Machine learning is used to stratify drug-induced cholestasis patients, categorize drugs, and develop prognostic models to identify high-risk patients and concerning drugs.

- **[Use of classification and regression tree (CART), to identify hemoglobin A1C (HbA1C) cut-off thresholds predictive of poor tuberculosis treatment outcomes and associated risk factors.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31720385/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models
	- Published: 2018May
	- Summary: Uses CART machine learning algorithm to identify HbA1C cut-off thresholds predictive of poor tuberculosis treatment outcomes in TB-diabetes co-affected patients.

- **[Development of a predictive model for nephrotoxicity during tacrolimus treatment using machine learning methods.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37921554/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024Mar
	- Summary: The paper develops machine learning models to predict tacrolimus-induced nephrotoxicity for individualized patient risk assessment and therapeutic management.

- **[A classifier-combined method for grading breast cancer based on Dempster-Shafer evidence theory.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37179927/)**
	- Methodology: Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2023May01
	- Summary: The paper develops a machine learning classification method using Dempster-Shafer evidence theory to combine classifiers for histologic grading of breast cancer.

- **[Evaluation of Supervised Machine Learning Algorithms and Computational Structural Validation of Single Nucleotide Polymorphisms Related to Acute Liver Injury with Paracetamol.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37927072/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2023
	- Summary: The paper uses supervised machine learning algorithms to predict acute liver injury from paracetamol by identifying relevant SNPs in drug-metabolizing enzymes.

- **[Cluster-Based Toxicity Estimation of Osteoradionecrosis via Unsupervised Machine Learning: Moving Beyond Single Dose-Parameter Normal Tissue Complication Probability by Using Whole Dose-Volume Histograms for Cohort Risk Stratification.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37034700/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2023Mar29
	- Summary: Uses unsupervised machine learning to develop cluster-based models for predicting osteoradionecrosis toxicity risk by analyzing whole dose-volume histograms for patient stratification.

- **[DL-ADR: a novel deep learning model for classifying genomic variants into adverse drug reactions.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/27510822/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2016Aug10
	- Summary: A deep learning model using generative stochastic networks and hidden Markov chains to classify genomic variants (SNPs) in CYP genes for predicting adverse drug reactions.

- **[Differentiation between treatment-related changes and progressive disease in patients with high grade brain tumors using support vector machine classification based on DCE MRI.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/26754857/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2016May
	- Summary: Uses support vector machine classification and independent components analysis on DCE-MRI data to differentiate treatment-related changes from progressive disease in brain tumor patients.

- **[Unraveling the impact of therapeutic drug monitoring via machine learning for patients with sepsis.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39127039/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2024Aug20
	- Summary: Machine learning algorithms identify informative features to distinguish healthy/sick states and demonstrate TDM's positive impact on sepsis patient recovery through data-driven analysis.

- **[Evaluating the Immunogenicity Risk of Protein Therapeutics by Augmenting T Cell Epitope Prediction with Clinical Factors.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39849284/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2025Jan23
	- Summary: The paper combines clinical factors with in silico epitope prediction using supervised learning to improve prediction accuracy of protein therapeutic immunogenicity risk.

- **[Machine learning model for predicting physical activity related bleeding risk in Chinese boys with haemophilia A.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37931538/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2023Dec
	- Summary: Machine learning model developed to predict bleeding risk during physical activity in Chinese boys with haemophilia A for improved patient management.

- **[Computer-aided diagnosis of prostate cancer using multi-parametric MRI: comparison between PUN and Tofts models.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/29570456/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2018May01
	- Summary: The paper uses support vector machine classifiers to create voxel-wise malignancy likelihood maps for prostate cancer diagnosis from multi-parametric MRI data.

## Pharmacodynamic modeling

- **[Deep learning prediction of patient response time course from early data via neural-pharmacokinetic/pharmacodynamic modelling.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40271424/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Neural networks, Time-series modeling, Mechanism-informed machine learning
	- Published: 2021Aug
	- Summary: Neural-PK/PD framework combines deep learning with pharmacological principles using neural ODEs to predict patient response time courses and simulate untested dosing regimens.

- **[Independent component analysis applied to pharmacological magnetic resonance imaging (phMRI): new insights into the functional networks underlying panic attacks as induced by CCK-4.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/19075726/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2008
	- Summary: The paper applies independent component analysis (ICA) to pharmacological fMRI data to identify neural networks underlying CCK-4 induced panic attacks.

- **[Uncertainty quantified discovery of chemical reaction systems via Bayesian scientific machine learning.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40809132/)**
	- Methodology: Bayesian ML, Deep learning, Neural networks, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2024
	- Summary: Uses Bayesian neural ODEs with Chemical Reaction Neural Networks to probabilistically discover chemical reaction pathways with uncertainty quantification.

- **[Antagonistic Trends Between Binding Affinity and Drug-Likeness in SARS-CoV-2 Mpro Inhibitors Revealed by Machine Learning.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40733553/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Jun30
	- Summary: Machine learning models (SVM and Logistic Regression) classify SARS-CoV-2 Mpro inhibitors based on IC50 data to reveal antagonistic trends between binding affinity and drug-likeness.

- **[Neural network emulation of the human ventricular cardiomyocyte action potential for more efficient computations in pharmacological studies.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38598284/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Surrogate modeling
	- Published: 2024Apr10
	- Summary: Neural networks are used to emulate human ventricular cardiomyocyte action potentials for faster computational modeling in pharmacological studies of drug effects on cardiac electrophysiology.

- **[A Systems-Based Analysis of Mono- and Combination Therapy for Carbapenem-Resistant Klebsiella pneumoniae Bloodstream Infections.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36125299/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models
	- Published: 2022Oct18
	- Summary: Random Forest was used to rank variables important for predicting 30-day mortality in carbapenem-resistant Klebsiella pneumoniae bloodstream infections treated with antimicrobial therapies.

- **[A support vector machine based pharmacodynamic prediction model for searching active fraction and ingredients of herbal medicine: Naodesheng prescription as an example.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/21664786/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2011Sep10
	- Summary: Uses support vector machine to predict pharmacodynamic effects of herbal medicine fractions, reducing experimental costs by predicting bioactivities from limited assay data.

- **[Prediction of pharmacokinetic/pharmacodynamic properties of aldosterone synthase inhibitors at drug discovery stage using an artificial intelligence-physiologically based pharmacokinetic model.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40356995/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2025
	- Summary: An AI-PBPK model integrating machine learning with classical PBPK modeling is developed to predict PK/PD properties of aldosterone synthase inhibitors for drug discovery screening.

- **[Prediction of Bispectral Index during Target-controlled Infusion of Propofol and Remifentanil: A Deep Learning Approach.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/28953500/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2018Mar
	- Summary: Deep learning approach is used to predict bispectral index during target-controlled infusion of propofol and remifentanil for improved anesthesia monitoring.

- **[Compartment Model and Neural Network-Based Analysis of Combination Medication Ratios.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40006595/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2025Feb10
	- Summary: Neural networks combined with compartment models to optimize combination drug ratios by establishing dose-effect relationships and using feedback mechanisms to achieve desired efficacy.

- **[Using neural networks to determine the contribution of danshensu to its multiple cardiovascular activities in acute myocardial infarction rats.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/21924339/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2011Oct31
	- Summary: Neural networks are used to determine the relative contribution of danshensu to multiple cardiovascular activities in acute myocardial infarction rats.

- **[Abciximab pharmacodynamic model with neural networks used to integrate sources of patient variability.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/14749692/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2004Jan
	- Summary: Neural networks are used to develop a computational model predicting abciximab-induced platelet aggregation inhibition from dose and patient characteristics.

- **[Machine Learning for Pharmacokinetic/Pharmacodynamic Modeling.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36669560/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Time-series modeling
	- Published: 2023May
	- Summary: This paper evaluates various recurrent neural networks for modeling irregularly sampled PK/PD data and predicting pharmacodynamic responses under different dosing regimens.

- **[Building virtual patients using simulation-based inference.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40809131/)**
	- Methodology: Bayesian ML, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2024
	- Summary: Uses simulation-based inference (SBI) to generate virtual patients from individual patient data for QSP models, demonstrated on rheumatoid arthritis treatment response prediction.

- **[Quantifying Intratumoral Heterogeneity and Immunoarchitecture Generated In-Silico by a Spatial Quantitative Systems Pharmacology Model.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37345087/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2023May13
	- Summary: Uses hybrid QSP-ABM model with spatial metrics to quantify intratumoral heterogeneity and classify tumor microenvironment immunoarchitecture for anti-PD-1 therapy response.

- **[Shap-Cov: An Explainable Machine Learning Based Workflow for Rapid Covariate Identification in Population Modeling.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40601439/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2025Aug
	- Summary: The paper presents Shap-Cov, an explainable ML workflow using SHAP analysis for automated covariate identification in population PK/PD modeling with uncertainty quantification.

- **[Modeling the pharmacokinetics and pharmacodynamics of a unique oral hypoglycemic agent using neural networks.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/11837705/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2002Jan
	- Summary: The paper develops a population PK/PD model for repaglinide using artificial neural networks to predict pharmacokinetic and pharmacodynamic responses.

- **[A Transformer-Based Prediction Method for Depth of Anesthesia During Target-Controlled Infusion of Propofol and Remifentanil.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37581963/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Time-series modeling
	- Published: 2023
	- Summary: A transformer-based deep learning method using LSTM and attention mechanisms to predict depth of anesthesia (BIS) during propofol and remifentanil infusion.

- **[Application of fuzzy logic to predicting erythropoietic response in hemodialysis patients.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/19115195/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2008Dec
	- Summary: The paper applies fuzzy logic to pharmacodynamic modeling for predicting erythropoietic response to erythropoietin in hemodialysis patients with uncertainty representation.

- **[Representation Meets Optimization: Training PINNs and PIKANs for Gray-Box Discovery in Systems Pharmacology.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40297233/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2025Apr10
	- Summary: Compares Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs) and Physics-Informed Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (PIKANs) for gray-box system identification in pharmacology modeling.

- **[Fuzzy modeling, a novel approach to studying pharmacodynamics.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/21317764/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2014Jan-Feb
	- Summary: The paper applies fuzzy logic modeling to improve prediction of pharmacodynamic responses, demonstrating better fit than conventional methods for quantal dose-response data.

- **[Infliximab monitoring in Crohn's disease: a neural network approach for evaluating disease activity and immunogenicity.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39664232/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2024
	- Summary: This paper uses neural networks to evaluate disease activity and immunogenicity in Crohn's disease patients receiving infliximab for therapeutic drug monitoring.

- **[Nonlinear System Identification Based on Convolutional Neural Networks for Multiple Drug Interactions.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30440247/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2018Jul
	- Summary: CNN is used for nonlinear system identification to model complex pharmacodynamics of multiple drug interactions in heart failure patients for improved cardiac treatment.

- **[Contribution of machine learning to tumor growth inhibition modeling for hepatocellular carcinoma patients under Roblitinib (FGF401) drug treatment.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35728123/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2022Aug
	- Summary: Machine learning with elastic net modeling is combined with population PK/PD modeling to identify predictive factors and create a risk score for tumor growth inhibition in hepatocellular carcinoma patients.

- **[Long short-term memory recurrent neural network for pharmacokinetic-pharmacodynamic modeling.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33210994/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Time-series modeling
	- Published: 2021Feb
	- Summary: This paper presents the application of LSTM neural networks for analyzing simulated pharmacokinetic and pharmacodynamic time series data of a hypothetical drug.

- **[Pharmacodynamic population analysis in chronic renal failure using artificial neural networks--a comparative study.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/12850042/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2003Jun-Jul
	- Summary: This paper applies artificial neural networks to perform pharmacodynamic population modeling in chronic renal failure patients for drug dose-effect prediction and dosing optimization.

- **[Proteolysis-targeting Chimera efficacy prediction using a deep-learning-QSP model.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41521293/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Neural networks
	- Published: 2026Jan12
	- Summary: Deep learning models are integrated with QSP modeling to predict PROTAC efficacy parameters (DC50 and Dmax) for targeted protein degradation therapeutics.

- **[Tumor growth inhibition modeling in patients with second line biliary tract cancer and first line non-small cell lung cancer based on bintrafusp alfa trials.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38087967/)**
	- Methodology: Feature selection, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2024Jan
	- Summary: The paper uses elastic net and random forest ML approaches alongside NLME modeling for covariate evaluation in tumor dynamics modeling of cancer patients receiving immunotherapy.

- **[Comparison of time-series models for predicting physiological metrics under sedation.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39470955/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Time-series modeling
	- Published: 2025Jun
	- Summary: Compares statistical and deep learning time-series models (LSTM, GRU, TCN, Transformers) for predicting bispectral index to assess sedation depth during surgery.

- **[Predicting pharmacodynamic effects through early drug discovery with artificial intelligence-physiologically based pharmacokinetic (AI-PBPK) modelling.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38434709/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024
	- Summary: The paper presents an AI-PBPK platform that uses machine learning to predict PK/PD parameters and outcomes for drug compounds in early discovery stages.

- **[Machine learning aided multiscale modelling of the HIV-1 infection in the presence of NRTI therapy.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37020854/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2023
	- Summary: Machine learning is combined with multi-strain ODE models to predict HIV-1 dynamics and optimize NRTI therapy protocols based on genotype-phenotype relationships and drug resistance patterns.

- **[State-space models are accurate and efficient neural operators for dynamical systems.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41447828/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Mechanism-informed machine learning
	- Published: 2026May
	- Summary: The paper introduces Mamba state-space models for physics-informed machine learning of dynamical systems, with application to tumor growth modeling in quantitative systems pharmacology.

- **[Novel design of fractional cholesterol dynamics and drug concentrations model with analysis on machine predictive networks.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39579668/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Time-series modeling
	- Published: 2025Jan
	- Summary: NARX neural networks are used to predict temporal dynamics of a fractional order cholesterol-drug interaction model for pharmacokinetic/pharmacodynamic modeling.

- **[[Study on the compatibility optimization of categorized formulas of Chinese medicine based on support vector regression].](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/23469530/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2012Dec
	- Summary: Uses support vector regression to predict pharmacodynamic actions of Chinese medicine formulas and optimize their compatibility for improved therapeutic effects.

- **[Machine Learning-Led Optimization of Combination Therapy: Confronting the Public Health Threat of Extensively Drug Resistant Gram-Negative Bacteria.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38062797/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2024Apr
	- Summary: Uses genetic algorithm optimization with hybrid ML-PK/PD/TD models to optimize combination antibiotic therapy regimens against drug-resistant bacteria using human PK and in vitro PD data.

- **[Forecasting anesthetic depth using an auto-regressive transformer in propofol infusion during the induction phase.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40522504/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Time-series modeling
	- Published: 2025Oct
	- Summary: The paper uses auto-regressive transformers to forecast depth of anesthesia for optimizing propofol dosing during anesthesia induction phase.

- **[Representation meets optimization: Training PINNs and PIKANs for gray-box discovery in systems pharmacology.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41447950/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2026Jan15
	- Summary: Compares Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs) and Physics-Informed Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (PIKANs) for gray-box system identification in pharmacokinetic and pharmacodynamic modeling.

- **[Reduction of quantitative systems pharmacology models using artificial neural networks.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33651241/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Surrogate modeling
	- Published: 2021Aug
	- Summary: The paper uses artificial neural networks to create simplified surrogate models of complex quantitative systems pharmacology models, demonstrated on a blood coagulation model.

- **[Numerical modeling and prediction of late estimated glomerular filtration rate in kidney transplant recipients based on machine learning models and the Monte Carlo simulation method.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41562636/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Surrogate modeling
	- Published: 2026Jan22
	- Summary: Machine learning models combined with Monte Carlo simulation are used to predict late estimated glomerular filtration rate in kidney transplant recipients on tacrolimus.

- **[Dexmedetomidine-induced deep sedation mimics non-rapid eye movement stage 3 sleep: large-scale validation using machine learning.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32860500/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2021Feb12
	- Summary: Machine learning techniques are used to validate that dexmedetomidine-induced deep sedation EEG patterns mimic natural non-REM stage 3 sleep patterns using large-scale recordings.

- **[Effect of Cumulative Exposure on the Efficacy of Paroxetine: A Population Pharmacokinetic-Pharmacodynamic and Machine Learning Analyses.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40358139/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2025Jun
	- Summary: Machine learning models predict paroxetine treatment remission using PK/PD data, with SHAP values explaining feature contributions for depression outcome prediction.

- **[A multi-scale pipeline linking drug transcriptomics with pharmacokinetics predicts in vivo interactions of tuberculosis drugs.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33707554/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2021Mar11
	- Summary: Uses machine learning model INDIGO-MTB to predict drug interactions from transcriptomics data, integrated with mechanistic PK/PD modeling to optimize tuberculosis combination therapies.

- **[CMINNs: Compartment model informed neural networks - Unlocking drug dynamics.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39608036/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Mechanism-informed machine learning
	- Published: 2025Jan
	- Summary: The paper proposes CMINNs using Physics-Informed Neural Networks with fractional calculus to enhance PK/PD modeling by capturing complex drug dynamics in heterogeneous tissues.

- **[Functional ultrasound imaging combined with machine learning for whole-brain analysis of drug-induced hemodynamic changes.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40948602/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Supervised learning, Neural networks, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2025
	- Summary: The paper uses CNN, SVM, and ViT machine learning approaches with functional ultrasound imaging to analyze pharmacodynamics of dizocilpine (MK-801) for whole-brain hemodynamic analysis.

- **[A new approach to modeling covariate effects and individualization in population pharmacokinetics-pharmacodynamics.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/16402288/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Model selection
	- Published: 2006Feb
	- Summary: The paper develops a new estimation approach for nonlinear mixed effects models in population PK/PD that uses neural networks as basis functions for nonparametric covariate modeling.

- **[A preliminary evaluation of neural network analysis for pharmacodynamic modeling of the dosing of the hydroxymethylglutaryl coenzyme A-reductase inhibitors simvastatin and atorvastatin.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/12017409/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2002Apr
	- Summary: This paper evaluates neural networks for pharmacodynamic modeling and dosing optimization of statin medications simvastatin and atorvastatin.

- **[Neural Controlled Differential Equation and Its Application in Pharmacokinetics and Pharmacodynamics.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41241764/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2026Jan
	- Summary: This paper investigates neural controlled differential equations (NCDE) for data-driven PK/PD modeling, combining differential equation dynamics with ML flexibility.

- **[Model-Based Virtual PK/PD Exploration and Machine Learning Approach to Define PK Drivers in Early Drug Discovery.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38375820/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2024Mar14
	- Summary: Uses machine learning to identify PK drivers and structural features influencing drug response through model-based virtual PK/PD exploration in early drug discovery.

- **[Modeling tumor size dynamics based on real-world electronic health records and image data in advanced melanoma patients receiving immunotherapy.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37328961/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2023Aug
	- Summary: The paper combines machine learning with pharmacometric approaches for covariate selection using radiomics features to model tumor growth dynamics in melanoma patients receiving immunotherapy.

- **[Predicting the anti-hypertensive effect of nitrendipine from plasma concentration profiles using artificial neural networks.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/16310013/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2005Dec
	- Summary: This paper uses artificial neural networks to model the relationship between nitrendipine plasma concentrations and blood pressure effects for optimizing drug dosing regimens.

- **[A machine learning model (Sol_ME) assisted development of a unit-dose lipid formulation for apalutamide: formulation, pharmacokinetics, and pharmacodynamics evaluation.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40858184/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Nov10
	- Summary: A machine learning model (Sol_ME) is used to assist in developing a lipid-based formulation for apalutamide with PK/PD evaluation for prostate cancer treatment.

- **[Interdependency of estradiol-mediated ERα activation and subsequent PR and GREB1 induction to control cell cycle progression.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39583845/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2024Nov15
	- Summary: Deep neural networks are used for image segmentation to track cell nuclei and quantify cell cycle durations in a mathematical model of estrogen receptor signaling.

- **[DN-ODE: Data-driven neural-ODE modeling for breast cancer tumor dynamics and progression-free survivals.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39089112/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2024Sep
	- Summary: Uses Neural ODEs to model individual breast cancer tumor dynamics and progression-free survival from early clinical trial data to predict later phase outcomes.

- **[From data to QSP models: a pipeline for using Boolean networks for hypothesis inference and dynamic model building.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34988912/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2022Feb
	- Summary: The paper presents a pipeline using k-means clustering and Boolean networks to convert data into QSP models, combining ML with mechanistic modeling for drug response prediction.

- **[Time-dependent AI-Modeling of the anticancer efficacy of synthesized gallic acid analogues.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30818108/)**
	- Methodology: Time-series modeling
	- Published: 2019Apr
	- Summary: AI modeling is used to map anticancer efficacy of gallic acid analogues across concentrations and exposure times to understand drug action mechanisms and response heterogeneity.

- **[Chemometrics-based approach to feature selection of chromatographic profiles and its application to search active fraction of herbal medicine.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/23375004/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2013Jun
	- Summary: Uses SVM with chemometrics-based feature selection (mRMR and PSO) to predict active fractions of herbal medicines from chromatographic profiles for pharmacodynamic modeling.

- **[Neural network emulation of the human ventricular cardiomyocyte action potential: a tool for more efficient computation in pharmacological studies.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38234850/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Surrogate modeling
	- Published: 2023Dec25
	- Summary: Neural networks are used to emulate human ventricular cardiomyocyte action potentials for faster computation in pharmacological studies and drug effect prediction.

- **[Exploring Transformer Model in Longitudinal Pharmacokinetic/Pharmacodynamic Analyses and Comparing with Alternative Natural Language Processing Models.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38350557/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Time-series modeling
	- Published: 2024May
	- Summary: This paper explores transformer models and compares them with LSTM and neural-ODE for longitudinal PK/PD modeling using virtual data across different dosing regimens.

- **[Understanding virtual patients efficiently and rigorously by combining machine learning with dynamical modelling.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34985622/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2022Feb
	- Summary: The paper combines machine learning with bifurcation analysis to efficiently analyze virtual patient populations in quantitative systems pharmacology modeling.

- **[A pharmacokinetic-pharmacodynamic model based on the SSA-1DCNN-Attention network and the semicompartment method.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37018446/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2024Nov
	- Summary: The paper develops a PK-PD model using 1D CNN with attention mechanism and sparrow search algorithm optimization to predict drug concentrations and model concentration-effect relationships.

- **[Mapping the dose-effect relationship of orbofiban from sparse data with an artificial neural network.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/16200613/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2005Nov
	- Summary: Neural networks are used to model the dose-effect relationship of orbofiban on platelet aggregation inhibition from sparse clinical data without requiring drug concentrations.

- **[Pharmacokinetic-pharmacodynamic modeling for Moutan Cortex/Moutan Cortex charcoal and the contributions of the chemical component using support vector regression with particle swarm optimization.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35516193/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2020Jun24
	- Summary: Support vector regression with particle swarm optimization is used to model PK-PD relationships of Chinese medicinal materials and identify chemical component contributions.

- **[A simulation-based framework for modeling and prediction of personalized blood pressure trajectories in hypertensive patients after antihypertensive treatment.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40209154/)**
	- Methodology: Gaussian processes
	- Published: 2025
	- Summary: The paper develops a simulation framework for blood pressure trajectories using PK/PD modeling and evaluates Gaussian processes for predicting steady-state blood pressure under treatment.

- **[Methods to Develop an in silico Clinical Trial: Computational Head-to-Head Comparison of Lisdexamfetamine and Methylphenidate.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34803764/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2021
	- Summary: The paper develops an in silico clinical trial comparing ADHD treatments using PBPK/QSP models combined with AI methods for data analysis and mechanistic predictions.

- **[An Analysis of Proteochemometric and Conformal Prediction Machine Learning Protein-Ligand Binding Affinity Models.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32671093/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Deep learning, Tree-based models, Neural networks
	- Published: 2020
	- Summary: Compares random forest and neural network proteochemometric models with conformal prediction for protein-ligand binding affinity prediction in drug discovery.

- **[Predicting the effects of drugs and unveiling their mechanisms of action using an interpretable pharmacodynamic mechanism knowledge graph (IPM-KG).](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39556916/)**
	- Methodology: Explainable AI, Mechanism-informed machine learning
	- Published: 2025Jan
	- Summary: The paper proposes an interpretable knowledge graph framework to predict drug effects and derive mechanistic pathways based on pharmacodynamic mechanisms of action.

- **[Machine-learning and mechanistic modeling of metastatic breast cancer after neoadjuvant treatment.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38701089/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024May
	- Summary: The paper combines mechanistic kinetics-pharmacodynamics modeling with machine learning to predict neoadjuvant treatment response and metastatic progression in breast cancer.

- **[Impact of sex and pathophysiology on optimal drug choice in hypertensive rats: quantitative insights for precision medicine.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33870137/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2021Apr23
	- Summary: The paper integrates quantitative systems pharmacology models with machine learning to predict optimal antihypertensive drug selection based on sex and pathophysiology.

- **[AI-Aristotle: A physics-informed framework for systems biology gray-box identification.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38470870/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Mechanism-informed machine learning
	- Published: 2024Mar
	- Summary: AI-Aristotle combines physics-informed neural networks with symbolic regression for parameter estimation and missing physics identification in systems biology models.

- **[Targeting the glabellar frown lines with OnabotulinumtoxinA: In-silico evidence supporting concentrated, low-volume injection protocols.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40907830/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2025Nov
	- Summary: Uses machine learning for off-target risk prediction integrated with mechanistic PK/PD modeling to optimize BoNT-A dosing protocols for glabellar frown line treatment.

- **[Development of a genetic algorithm and NONMEM workbench for automating and improving population pharmacokinetic/pharmacodynamic model selection.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34604941/)**
	- Methodology: Model selection
	- Published: 2022Apr
	- Summary: The paper presents a genetic algorithm integrated with NONMEM workbench to automate population PK/PD model selection by finding globally optimal solutions for model components.

- **[Integrating Pharmacokinetics and Quantitative Systems Pharmacology Approaches in Generative Drug Design.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40343729/)**
	- Methodology: Reinforcement learning, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025May26
	- Summary: The paper integrates AI/ML methods including reinforcement learning and QSPR models in a generative drug design framework to optimize both PK properties and receptor affinity.

- **[MODEL-molecular descriptor lab: a web-based server for computing structural and physicochemical features of compounds.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/17013940/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2007Jun01
	- Summary: Paper presents a web server for computing molecular descriptors used in QSAR and neural network models to predict pharmacological and toxicological properties of compounds.

- **[Using machine learning to optimize antibiotic combinations: dosing strategies for meropenem and polymyxin B against carbapenem-resistant Acinetobacter baumannii.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32061797/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2020Sep
	- Summary: Uses genetic algorithm with mechanism-based mathematical models to optimize dosing strategies for meropenem and polymyxin B combinations against resistant bacteria.

- **[Functional Ultrasound Imaging Combined with Machine Learning for Whole-Brain Analysis of Drug-Induced Hemodynamic Changes.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40895074/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Supervised learning, Neural networks, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2025Aug19
	- Summary: This paper compares CNN, SVM, and ViT machine learning approaches combined with functional ultrasound imaging to analyze pharmacodynamics of MK-801 drug effects on whole-brain hemodynamics.

- **[Learning Chemotherapy Drug Action via Universal Physics-Informed Neural Networks.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40244511/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Mechanism-informed machine learning
	- Published: 2025Apr
	- Summary: The paper applies Universal Physics-Informed Neural Networks to learn unknown components of differential equations modeling chemotherapy pharmacodynamics in QSP.

- **[Generative models for synthetic data generation: application to pharmacokinetic/pharmacodynamic data.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39192091/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2024Dec
	- Summary: This paper benchmarks deep learning generative models (GANs, TimeGAN, PAR) for generating synthetic pharmacokinetic/pharmacodynamic patient data to enable data access and augmentation.

- **[Population pharmacokinetic and pharmacodynamic models of remifentanil in healthy volunteers using artificial neural network analysis.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/17324247/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2007Jul
	- Summary: This study uses artificial neural networks to develop population PK/PD models for remifentanil and predict EEG approximate entropy overshoot during recovery in healthy volunteers.

- **[A novel approach for the analysis of time-course gene expression data based on computing with words.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34271172/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning, Time-series modeling
	- Published: 2021Aug
	- Summary: Novel fuzzy logic approach for time-course gene expression analysis to investigate pharmacodynamic effects of anticancer drugs on human colon cancer cells.

## Pharmacokinetic modeling

- **[Development of LC-MS determination method and back-propagation ANN pharmacokinetic model of corynoxeine in rat.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/24732215/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2014May15
	- Summary: The paper develops a back-propagation artificial neural network model to predict corynoxeine pharmacokinetics in rats using LC-MS concentration data.

- **[Ensemble Machine Learning Model for Real-Time Valproic Acid Prediction in Epilepsy Treatment.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40456293/)**
	- Methodology: Ensemble learning, Tree-based models, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2025Jun02
	- Summary: Develops ensemble ML model using gradient boosting algorithms with SHAP interpretation to predict valproic acid concentrations for optimized epilepsy treatment dosing.

- **[LSTM-Based Prediction of Human PK Profiles and Parameters for Intravenous Small Molecule Drugs Using ADME and Physicochemical Properties.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41147737/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Time-series modeling
	- Published: 2025Dec
	- Summary: LSTM neural networks are used to predict human pharmacokinetic concentration-time profiles and parameters for IV drugs using ADME and physicochemical properties.

- **[Deep learning prediction of patient response time course from early data via neural-pharmacokinetic/pharmacodynamic modelling.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40271424/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Neural networks, Time-series modeling, Mechanism-informed machine learning
	- Published: 2021Aug
	- Summary: Neural-PK/PD framework combines deep learning with pharmacological principles using neural ODEs to predict patient response time courses and simulate untested dosing regimens.

- **[Improving measurement of blood-brain barrier permeability with reduced scan time using deep-learning-derived capillary input function.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37507078/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2023Sep
	- Summary: Deep learning is used to estimate capillary input function for improved blood-brain barrier permeability measurement in DCE-MRI with reduced scan time.

- **[Prediction of human clearance based on animal data and molecular properties.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/25845625/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2015Nov
	- Summary: Uses genetic algorithm for feature selection and support vector machines to predict human drug clearance from animal data and molecular descriptors, improving upon traditional allometric scaling.

- **[Clozapine Toxicity Predictor: Deep neural network model predicting clozapine toxicity and its therapeutic dose range.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39522262/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2024Dec
	- Summary: Deep neural network model predicts clozapine toxicity and therapeutic dose range using clinical data to guide treatment when therapeutic drug monitoring is unavailable.

- **[Machine learning-based quantitative prediction of drug exposure in drug-drug interactions using drug label information.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35817846/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2022Jul11
	- Summary: Machine learning model predicts quantitative pharmacokinetic drug-drug interaction fold changes using FDA drug label data to enable DDI evaluation before clinical trials.

- **[Prediction of infliximab and anti-drug antibody concentrations in patients with inflammatory bowel disease using machine learning models with real-world data from a prospective cohort study.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41685197/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2026
	- Summary: Machine learning models were developed to predict infliximab and anti-drug antibody concentrations in IBD patients as an alternative to complex population PK models.

- **[Toward noninvasive quantification of brain radioligand binding by combining electronic health records and dynamic PET imaging data.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/25823051/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2015Jul
	- Summary: Machine learning is applied to electronic health records to enable noninvasive estimation of arterial input functions for quantitative PET brain imaging analysis.

- **[Drug Clearance in Neonates: A Combination of Population Pharmacokinetic Modelling and Machine Learning Approaches to Improve Individual Prediction.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34041714/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2021Nov
	- Summary: The paper combines population pharmacokinetic modeling with machine learning approaches to improve individual drug clearance prediction in neonates.

- **[Models for skin and brain penetration of major components from essential oils used in aromatherapy for dementia patients.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31204906/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2020May
	- Summary: Artificial neural networks are used to develop QSAR models predicting skin and blood-brain barrier penetration of essential oil terpenoids for aromatherapy applications.

- **[Utility of the 13 C-pantoprazole breath test as a CYP2C19 phenotyping probe for children.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35099109/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models, Feature selection
	- Published: 2022May
	- Summary: Random forest models with feature engineering were used to predict CYP2C19 metabolizer phenotypes from breath test data to guide personalized PPI dosing in children.

- **[Feature importance of machine learning prediction models shows structurally active part and important physicochemical features in drug design.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34089983/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Explainable AI, Feature selection
	- Published: 2021Aug
	- Summary: This paper develops machine learning models to predict pharmacokinetic properties and uses explainable AI to identify important physicochemical features and metabolic sites for drug design.

- **[Machine learning-based prediction model for teicoplanin plasma concentrations in adults with liver disease using real-world data.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41424785/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Deep learning
	- Published: 2025
	- Summary: Machine learning and deep learning techniques are used to construct a prediction model for teicoplanin plasma concentrations in liver disease patients using real-world data.

- **[Deep compartment models: A deep learning approach for the reliable prediction of time-series data in pharmacokinetic modeling.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38100092/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Time-series modeling
	- Published: 2022Jul
	- Summary: The paper proposes deep compartment models combining neural networks with ODEs for reliable pharmacokinetic modeling and drug concentration prediction.

- **[In-silico identification of a Doxorubicin alternative with reduced cardiotoxicity informed by LLM-assisted modeling.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41223606/)**
	- Methodology: LLM, Tree-based models, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2026Jan
	- Summary: LLM-assisted pipeline with random forest and SHAP for predicting ADME/ADMET properties to identify safer doxorubicin alternatives with reduced cardiotoxicity.

- **[High-Throughput Physiologically Based Pharmacokinetic Model for Rodent Pharmacokinetics Prediction Using Machine Learning-Predicted Inputs and a Large In Vivo Pharmacokinetics Data Set.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41617209/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2026Mar02
	- Summary: The paper develops a high-throughput PBPK model (SwiftPK) that uses machine learning to predict in vitro parameters from chemical structures for rodent pharmacokinetic prediction.

- **[Supervised Machine-Learning Reveals That Old and Obese People Achieve Low Dapsone Concentrations.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/28575552/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2017Aug
	- Summary: Supervised ML using multivariate adaptive regression splines for covariate selection to identify complex weight/age effects on dapsone PK parameters for personalized dosing.

- **[Machine learning advances the integration of covariates in population pharmacokinetic models: Valproic acid as an example.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36324679/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2022
	- Summary: XGBoost model with SHAP interpretation integrates covariates from population pharmacokinetic models to predict valproic acid steady-state concentrations in epileptic patients.

- **[Bayesian algorithm implementation in a real time exposure assessment model on benzene with calculation of associated cancer risks.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/22399936/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Bayesian ML
	- Published: 2009
	- Summary: The paper develops ANNs with Bayesian regularization to predict benzene exposure and uses Bayesian algorithms in PBPK models to assess leukemia risk for filling station employees.

- **[Machine learning approach for dosage individualization of azithromycin in children with community-acquired pneumonia.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40181615/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Aug
	- Summary: Machine learning is used to predict azithromycin AUC0-24 in children with pneumonia to enable individualized dosing regimens.

- **[Activity Cliff-Informed Contrastive Learning for Molecular Property Prediction.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39678335/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024Dec04
	- Summary: Introduces ACANet, a contrastive learning approach using graph neural networks to improve molecular property prediction by incorporating activity cliff awareness.

- **[Predicting Pharmacokinetics in Rats Using Machine Learning: A Comparative Study Between Empirical, Compartmental, and PBPK-Based Approaches.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40091606/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2025Mar
	- Summary: This study compares ML approaches integrated with empirical and mechanistic PK models to predict complete plasma concentration-time profiles in rats prior to synthesis.

- **[Microdose Cocktail Study Reveals the Activity and Key Influencing Factors of OATP1B, P-Gp, BCRP, and CYP3A in End-Stage Renal Disease Patients.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39789999/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025May
	- Summary: Machine learning models were used alongside population pharmacokinetic modeling to identify key factors influencing drug transporter/enzyme activities in ESRD patients.

- **[Named entity recognition of pharmacokinetic parameters in the scientific literature.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39379460/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2024Oct08
	- Summary: The paper develops BioBERT-based named entity recognition models to automatically extract pharmacokinetic parameters from scientific literature using deep learning approaches.

- **[Improving the Reliability of Pharmacokinetic Parameters at Dynamic Contrast-enhanced MRI in Astrocytomas: A Deep Learning Approach.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32749203/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2020Oct
	- Summary: Deep learning model translates arterial input functions from DCE MRI to improve reliability of pharmacokinetic parameters for astrocytoma grading.

- **[An Ensemble Model for Prediction of Vancomycin Trough Concentrations in Pediatric Patients.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33883878/)**
	- Methodology: Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2021
	- Summary: This study develops an ensemble machine learning model to predict vancomycin trough concentrations in pediatric patients for improved dosing optimization.

- **[Predicting Elimination of Small-Molecule Drug Half-Life in Pharmacokinetics Using Ensemble and Consensus Machine Learning Methods.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38563433/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Ensemble learning, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2024Apr22
	- Summary: Uses ensemble ML methods (XGBoost, RF, GBM, SVM) with SHAP interpretation to predict drug half-life from molecular structure via QSAR modeling for pharmacokinetic parameter estimation.

- **[Dose-time-concentration prediction method based on GRU-TCN with temporal-channel attention.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41201288/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Time-series modeling
	- Published: 2025Nov07
	- Summary: The paper develops a GRU-TCN deep learning model with attention mechanisms for predicting drug concentration-time profiles to support precision dosing decisions.

- **[Deep learning quantification of vascular pharmacokinetic parameters in mouse brain tumor models.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35345331/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2022Mar16
	- Summary: Deep learning is used to automate generation of pharmacokinetic parameter maps from DCE MRI as a surrogate to conventional PK modeling in mouse brain tumor models.

- **[Estimation of drug exposure by machine learning based on simulations from published pharmacokinetic models: The example of tacrolimus.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33775863/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models, Surrogate modeling
	- Published: 2021May
	- Summary: XGBoost models are developed to estimate tacrolimus AUC from limited concentration samples using simulated data from published PK models, compared against Bayesian estimation.

- **[Modelling of intrinsic membrane permeability of drug molecules by explainable ML-based q-RASPR approach towards better pharmacokinetics and toxicokinetics properties.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40190164/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2025Feb
	- Summary: Uses explainable ML with Support Vector Regression to predict intrinsic membrane permeability of drugs for better pharmacokinetic and toxicokinetic assessment.

- **[A fuzzy physiologically based pharmacokinetic modeling framework to predict drug disposition in humans.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/17959432/)**
	- Methodology: Surrogate modeling
	- Published: 2006
	- Summary: The paper presents a fuzzy set-based PBPK modeling framework using sparse grid methods as surrogate models to efficiently handle parameter uncertainty in drug disposition prediction.

- **[Machine learning algorithms to estimate everolimus exposure trained on simulated and patient pharmacokinetic profiles.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35599364/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models
	- Published: 2022Aug
	- Summary: XGBoost machine learning algorithm was trained on simulated and patient PK profiles to estimate everolimus AUC exposure using limited blood samples for therapeutic drug monitoring.

- **[Estimation of biliary excretion of foreign compounds using properties of molecular structure.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/24202722/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2014Jan
	- Summary: The paper develops QSAR models using regression trees, random forest, and boosted trees to predict biliary excretion of compounds based on molecular structure properties.

- **[An integrated scheme for feature selection and parameter setting in the support vector machine modeling and its application to the prediction of pharmacokinetic properties of drugs.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/18701266/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2009Jun
	- Summary: The paper proposes an integrated scheme for simultaneous feature selection and parameter optimization in SVM modeling to predict ADMET properties of drugs.

- **[A multi-compartment physiologically based pharmacokinetic (PBPK) model coupled with Fourier-ARIMA (auto regressive integrated moving average) for estimation and prediction of polychlorinated biphenyls (PCB) concentration in tuna.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40577972/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Time-series modeling
	- Published: 2025Sep
	- Summary: The paper develops a hybrid PBPK-Fourier-ARIMA model to predict PCB concentrations in tuna tissues, combining mechanistic modeling with time-series analysis for contamination forecasting.

- **[Normalizing Flow-Based Distribution Estimation of Pharmacokinetic Parameters in Dynamic Contrast-Enhanced Magnetic Resonance Imaging.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37738180/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Bayesian ML
	- Published: 2024Mar
	- Summary: The paper develops normalizing flow-based deep learning methods to estimate posterior distributions of pharmacokinetic parameters from DCE-MRI data with uncertainty quantification.

- **[DeepTDM: Deep Learning-Based Prediction of Sequential Therapeutic Drug Monitoring Levels of Vancomycin.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41220794/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2025
	- Summary: Deep learning is used to predict sequential vancomycin therapeutic drug monitoring levels in critically ill patients to optimize dosing and improve treatment outcomes.

- **[Estimation of the capillary level input function for dynamic contrast-enhanced MRI of the breast using a deep learning approach.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35001423/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2022May
	- Summary: Deep learning is used to estimate capillary-level input functions for pharmacokinetic modeling of dynamic contrast-enhanced MRI in breast imaging.

- **[A proof of concept reinforcement learning based tool for non parametric population pharmacokinetics workflow optimization.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36478350/)**
	- Methodology: Reinforcement learning
	- Published: 2023Feb
	- Summary: This paper presents a proof of concept using SARSA reinforcement learning to automate the iterative process of building population pharmacokinetic models.

- **[Machine learning estimation of FVIII pharmacokinetic parameters in Chinese children with severe Hemophilia A.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41760637/)**
	- Methodology: LLM
	- Published: 2026Feb27
	- Summary: Machine learning framework incorporating language models predicts FVIII pharmacokinetic parameters in pediatric hemophilia A patients for personalized prophylactic treatment.

- **[Estimation of pharmacokinetic parameters from DCE-MRI by extracting long and short time-dependent features using an LSTM network.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32379942/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Time-series modeling
	- Published: 2020Aug
	- Summary: The paper proposes using LSTM neural networks to estimate pharmacokinetic parameters from DCE-MRI by extracting temporal features as an alternative to least squares fitting.

- **[In Silico Predictions of the Gastrointestinal Uptake of Macrocycles in Man Using Conformal Prediction Methodology.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35605685/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2022Sep
	- Summary: Machine learning with conformal prediction is used to predict gastrointestinal uptake parameters of macrocyclic compounds in humans for drug development.

- **[Pharmacokinetics and tissue distribution model of cabozantinib in rat determined by UPLC-MS/MS.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/25638029/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2015Mar01
	- Summary: The paper develops a BP-ANN (backpropagation artificial neural network) model to predict tissue distribution concentrations of cabozantinib based on pharmacokinetic data.

- **[A Deep Learning-Based Approach for Prediction of Vancomycin Treatment Monitoring: Retrospective Study Among Patients With Critical Illness.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38152042/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2024Mar08
	- Summary: Deep learning approach developed to predict vancomycin therapeutic drug monitoring levels in ICU patients to assist clinicians with dosing decisions.

- **[PK-RNN-V E: A deep learning model approach to vancomycin therapeutic drug monitoring using electronic health record data.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35985620/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Time-series modeling
	- Published: 2022Sep
	- Summary: Deep learning RNN model (PK-RNN-VE) for vancomycin therapeutic drug monitoring using EHR data, outperforming traditional Bayesian approaches for pharmacokinetic predictions.

- **[Hybrid Population Pharmacokinetic-Machine Learning Modeling to Predict Infliximab Pharmacokinetics in Pediatric and Young Adult Patients with Crohn's Disease.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40885855/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2025Nov
	- Summary: The paper develops a hybrid approach combining machine learning with population PK-based Bayesian methods to improve infliximab concentration predictions in pediatric Crohn's disease patients.

- **[Prediction of pharmacokinetic/pharmacodynamic properties of aldosterone synthase inhibitors at drug discovery stage using an artificial intelligence-physiologically based pharmacokinetic model.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40356995/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2025
	- Summary: An AI-PBPK model integrating machine learning with classical PBPK modeling is developed to predict PK/PD properties of aldosterone synthase inhibitors for drug discovery screening.

- **[Development of machine learning-based quantitative structure-activity relationship models for predicting plasma half-lives of drugs in six common food animal species.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39302735/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Deep learning, Neural networks, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2025Jan01
	- Summary: Develops QSAR models using multiple ML algorithms including deep neural networks to predict plasma half-lives of drugs in food animals for withdrawal interval estimation.

- **[Fully-automated deep learning-powered system for DCE-MRI analysis of brain tumors.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31980106/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2020Jan
	- Summary: Deep learning system automates DCE-MRI analysis for brain tumor pharmacokinetic modeling and biomarker extraction, eliminating manual processes and human error.

- **[Uncertainty estimates in pharmacokinetic modelling of DCE-MRI.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41371063/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Ensemble learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2026Mar
	- Summary: The paper uses ensemble neural networks with uncertainty estimation to improve pharmacokinetic parameter quantification in DCE-MRI compared to conventional methods.

- **[Optimal Transport Based Graph Kernels for Drug Property Prediction.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39906265/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025
	- Summary: The paper develops optimal transport-based graph kernels for predicting drug ADMET properties, outperforming state-of-the-art graph neural networks on multiple datasets.

- **[Quantitative Structure-Pharmacokinetic Relationships for drug distribution properties by using general regression neural network.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/15761939/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2005Jan
	- Summary: The paper uses general regression neural networks (GRNN) to develop QSPR models for predicting drug distribution properties including blood-brain barrier penetration and protein binding.

- **[Improving Absorption, Distribution, Metabolism, and Excretion Property Predictions by Integrating Public and Proprietary Data.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41290196/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2026Jan
	- Summary: This study uses supervised learning and multitask models to predict ADME properties by integrating public and proprietary datasets for improved drug discovery.

- **[In Silico Prediction of Intestinal Permeability by Hierarchical Support Vector Regression.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32438630/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2020May19
	- Summary: The paper develops a hierarchical support vector regression model to predict intestinal permeability for drug absorption assessment in drug discovery and development.

- **[Application of ALOGPS to predict 1-octanol/water distribution coefficients, logP, and logD, of AstraZeneca in-house database.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/15514985/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2004Dec
	- Summary: The paper applies Associative Neural Networks via ALOGPS 2.1 to predict octanol/water partition coefficients (logP) and distribution coefficients (logD) for drug development.

- **[Artificial intelligence modelling of tyrosine kinase inhibitors at risk of malabsorption and bioavailability-enhancing strategies.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40625205/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Nov
	- Summary: AI model developed to predict tyrosine kinase inhibitors at risk of malabsorption in patients with specific conditions and identify bioavailability enhancement strategies.

- **[Developing Robust Human Liver Microsomal Stability Prediction Models: Leveraging Inter-Species Correlation with Rat Data.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39458588/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2024Sep27
	- Summary: The paper develops AI/ML models using classical machine learning and neural networks to predict human liver microsomal stability for drug discovery applications.

- **[MELLODDY: Cross-pharma Federated Learning at Unprecedented Scale Unlocks Benefits in QSAR without Compromising Proprietary Information.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37642660/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Deep learning
	- Published: 2024Apr08
	- Summary: The paper presents federated learning across 10 pharma companies to improve QSAR models for drug activity prediction using 2.6+ billion confidential data points.

- **[Effect of molecular descriptor feature selection in support vector machine classification of pharmacokinetic and toxicological properties of chemical agents.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/15446820/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2004Sep-Oct
	- Summary: Uses support vector machines with recursive feature elimination to predict pharmacokinetic and toxicological properties of chemical agents from molecular descriptors.

- **[Segmentation of rodent whole-body dynamic PET images: an unsupervised method based on voxel dynamics.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/18334430/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2008Mar
	- Summary: An unsupervised ML method for segmenting rodent whole-body PET images to improve pharmacokinetic analysis by estimating organ dynamics while accounting for spillover and movement.

- **[Advancing exposure science through artificial intelligence: Neural ordinary differential equations for predicting blood concentrations of volatile organic compounds.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39978105/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2025Mar01
	- Summary: Neural ODEs are used to predict blood concentrations of volatile organic compounds, outperforming traditional PBPK models for toxicological forecasting and risk assessment.

- **[Artificial intelligence-driven prediction and validation of blood-brain barrier permeability and absorption, distribution, metabolism, excretion profiles in natural product research laboratory compounds.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39777113/)**
	- Methodology: LLM, Tree-based models, Deep learning
	- Published: 2024
	- Summary: Uses transformer-based LLM (MegaMolBART) with XGBoost to predict blood-brain barrier permeability and ADME profiles of natural product compounds.

- **[Development of Machine Learning and Chemical Language Model-Based QSAR Models for Predicting Drug Residue Depletion Half-Lives in Plasma and Tissues of Cattle Across Various Administration Routes.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41442152/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Deep learning, Neural networks, LLM, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2025Dec24
	- Summary: Machine learning and chemical language models (ImprovedChemBERTa) are used to predict drug depletion half-lives in cattle plasma and tissues for food safety applications.

- **[Compartment Model and Neural Network-Based Analysis of Combination Medication Ratios.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40006595/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2025Feb10
	- Summary: Neural networks combined with compartment models to optimize combination drug ratios by establishing dose-effect relationships and using feedback mechanisms to achieve desired efficacy.

- **[A Hybrid Algorithm Combining Population Pharmacokinetic and Machine Learning for Isavuconazole Exposure Prediction.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36991227/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2023Apr
	- Summary: A hybrid algorithm combining population pharmacokinetic modeling with machine learning to improve prediction accuracy of isavuconazole clearance for individualized dosing.

- **[Harnessing large language models' zero-shot and few-shot learning capabilities for regulatory research.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39177261/)**
	- Methodology: LLM
	- Published: 2024Jul25
	- Summary: The paper evaluates open-source LLMs for extracting clinical pharmacology information from regulatory drug labels using zero-shot and few-shot learning approaches.

- **[A model based on machine learning for the prediction of cyclosporin A trough concentration in Chinese allo-HSCT patients.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36373407/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2023Jan
	- Summary: Machine learning model developed to predict cyclosporin A trough concentrations in Chinese allo-HSCT patients for improved therapeutic drug monitoring.

- **[Structure-Based Prediction of Anti-infective Drug Concentrations in the Human Lung Epithelial Lining Fluid.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/26626793/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2016Apr
	- Summary: The paper develops a predictive model using chemical structure descriptors to predict antibiotic concentration ratios in lung epithelial lining fluid versus plasma.

- **[Computational Systems Pharmacology-Target Mapping for Fentanyl-Laced Cocaine Overdose.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31257858/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2019Aug21
	- Summary: Machine learning methods are applied to map protein targets and analyze drug interactions for fentanyl-laced cocaine overdose using chemogenomics and PBPK modeling approaches.

- **[Learning pharmacometric covariate model structures with symbolic regression networks.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37864654/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Feature selection, Model selection
	- Published: 2024Apr
	- Summary: The paper uses symbolic regression networks to automatically identify covariate model structures in pharmacokinetic modeling, demonstrated on propofol clinical data.

- **[Rational Design of Safer Inorganic Nanoparticles via Mechanistic Modeling-informed Machine Learning.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40034433/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2025Feb18
	- Summary: ML framework predicts nanoparticle toxicity by integrating PBPK modeling with binary classification models to enable rational design of safer inorganic nanoparticles.

- **[Global Deep Forecasting with Patient-Specific Pharmacokinetics.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37965077/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Time-series modeling
	- Published: 2025Feb12
	- Summary: A hybrid global-local deep learning architecture with PK encoder forecasts blood glucose levels by incorporating patient-specific pharmacokinetic properties for improved accuracy.

- **[A physics-informed neural network approach for estimating population-level pharmacokinetic parameters from aggregated concentration data.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41699348/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2026Feb16
	- Summary: The paper introduces distributional physics-informed neural networks (D-PINNs) to estimate population-level pharmacokinetic parameter distributions from aggregated concentration data.

- **[Prediction of drug distribution within blood.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/19152835/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2009Mar02
	- Summary: The paper uses artificial neural networks and PLS regression to predict drug blood-to-plasma concentration ratios from molecular descriptors for pharmacokinetic modeling.

- **[Predicting blood-to-plasma concentration ratios of drugs from chemical structures and volumes of distribution in humans.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33569705/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Tree-based models, Feature selection

	- Summary: This study uses XGBoost for feature selection and artificial neural networks to predict blood-to-plasma concentration ratios from chemical structures and PK parameters.

	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Deep learning, Tree-based models, Neural networks, Ensemble learning

	- Summary: Machine learning and deep learning algorithms predict mycophenolic acid exposure in pediatric patients using limited sampling strategy to optimize therapeutic drug monitoring.

	- Published: 2025Dec28
	- Summary: The paper uses ensemble machine learning methods including k-NN and time series classification to predict blood-brain barrier permeability from experimental data.

- **[Machine Learning for Pharmacokinetic/Pharmacodynamic Modeling.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36669560/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Time-series modeling
	- Published: 2023May
	- Summary: This paper evaluates various recurrent neural networks for modeling irregularly sampled PK/PD data and predicting pharmacodynamic responses under different dosing regimens.

- **[Joint use of population pharmacokinetics and machine learning for prediction of valproic acid plasma concentration in elderly epileptic patients.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39128815/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Supervised learning

	- Summary: The paper combines population pharmacokinetics with machine learning to predict valproic acid plasma concentrations in elderly epileptic patients.

	- Summary: Develops a pharmacometrics-based algorithm using Empirical Bayesian Estimation to predict bilirubin kinetics in neonates for personalized jaundice management.

	- Methodology: Supervised learning

	- Summary: The paper presents an AI-driven drug design platform that uses evolutionary algorithms to optimize molecular properties by integrating PBPK simulations and ADMET predictions.

- **[Employing artificial intelligence for optimising antibiotic dosages in sepsis on intensive care unit: a study protocol for a prospective observational study (KI.SEP).](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39672586/)**

	- Summary: This study develops ML-based models to predict antibiotic serum concentrations for optimizing dosing in sepsis patients using clinical and laboratory data.

- **[Predicting Absorption-Distribution Properties of Neuroprotective Phosphine-Borane Compounds Using In Silico Modeling and Machine Learning.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33923006/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2021Apr25
	- Summary: Neural networks are used to predict absorption-distribution properties of novel phosphine-borane neuroprotective compounds for drug development.
