	- Methodology: Neural networks, Deep learning
	- Published: 2021
	- Summary: Uses multilayer perceptron neural networks to predict lorlatinib brain concentration based on metabolomic biomarkers, achieving >85% accuracy for brain penetration prediction.

- **[Go beyond the limits of genetic algorithm in daily covariate selection practice.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37493851/)**
	- Methodology: Feature selection
	- Published: 2024Apr
	- Summary: The paper proposes an improved genetic algorithm for covariate selection in population pharmacokinetic modeling to overcome limitations of stepwise covariate models.

	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2012Aug30

	- Summary: Uses SHAP values with random forest and XGBoost models to infer optimal functional forms of covariates in population pharmacokinetic modeling for hemophilia A patients.

	- Summary: The paper uses hierarchical clustering (unsupervised ML) to enhance read-across approaches for selecting chemical analogs in physiologically based kinetic modeling of propiconazole.

	- Summary: A deep learning model is developed to automatically extract arterial input functions from DCE-MRI images for quantitative pharmacokinetic modeling in multi-site cohorts.

- **[Machine learning-based prediction of sertraline concentration in patients with depression through therapeutic drug monitoring.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38510645/)**

	- Summary: The paper combines machine learning with PBPK modeling to predict pharmacokinetic profiles of small molecules without requiring experimental data inputs.

	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2021Mar16
	- Summary: The paper develops automated machine learning models to predict fraction unbound of drugs in human plasma using chemical fingerprints for pharmacokinetic modeling.

- **[A fuzzy physiologically based pharmacokinetic modeling framework to predict drug disposition in humans.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/17947127/)**
	- Methodology: Surrogate modeling
	- Published: 2006
	- Summary: The paper presents a fuzzy set-based PBPK modeling framework using sparse grid methods as surrogate models to efficiently handle parameter uncertainty in drug disposition prediction.

- **[Human Brain Penetration Prediction Using Scaling Approach from Animal Machine Learning Models.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37667061/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2023Sep05
	- Summary: ML models predict human brain penetration (Kpuu,brain) by scaling from animal models, comparing performance against mechanistic NeuroPK models for drug development applications.

- **[Contribution of machine learning to tumor growth inhibition modeling for hepatocellular carcinoma patients under Roblitinib (FGF401) drug treatment.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35728123/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2022Aug
	- Summary: Machine learning with elastic net modeling is combined with population PK/PD modeling to identify predictive factors and create a risk score for tumor growth inhibition in hepatocellular carcinoma patients.

- **[Predicting Volume of Distribution in Humans: Performance of In Silico Methods for a Large Set of Structurally Diverse Clinical Compounds.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33239335/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2021Feb
	- Summary: Machine learning models are developed to predict human volume of distribution at steady state directly from chemical structures, outperforming mechanistic and empirical methods.

- **[In silico estimation of basic activity-relevant parameters for a set of drug absorption promoters.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/28573881/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning, Feature selection, Neural networks
	- Published: 2017Jun
	- Summary: The paper uses PCA, self-organizing neural networks, and IVE-PLS for predicting lipophilicity and ADMET properties of cholic acid derivatives as drug absorption promoters.

- **[Enhancing Chemical Prediction in Plasma Protein Binding: A Deep Learning Model Combining Molecular Descriptors and Fingerprint Representation With Attention Mechanisms.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40811414/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2025Mar-Apr
	- Summary: A deep learning model combining molecular descriptors, fingerprints, and graph features with attention mechanisms to predict plasma protein binding properties of drug molecules.

- **[Long short-term memory recurrent neural network for pharmacokinetic-pharmacodynamic modeling.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33210994/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Time-series modeling
	- Published: 2021Feb
	- Summary: This paper presents the application of LSTM neural networks for analyzing simulated pharmacokinetic and pharmacodynamic time series data of a hypothetical drug.

- **[Application of machine-learning models to predict the ganciclovir and valganciclovir exposure in children using a limited sampling strategy.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39194260/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models
	- Published: 2024Oct08
	- Summary: Machine learning models, particularly XGBoost, are used to predict ganciclovir AUC in children using limited sampling strategies as an alternative to Bayesian estimators.

- **[pyDarwin machine learning algorithms application and comparison in nonlinear mixed-effect model selection and optimization.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38941056/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models, Gaussian processes, Model selection, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2024Dec
	- Summary: This paper compares five ML algorithms (GA, GP, RF, GBRT, PSO) for automated population pharmacokinetic model selection as alternatives to traditional FABE methods.

- **[QSAR models for the prediction of plasma protein binding.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/23678466/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2013
	- Summary: QSAR models use supervised learning to predict plasma protein binding, a key pharmacokinetic parameter affecting drug distribution and clearance.

- **[Machine Learning Predicts Drug Metabolism and Bioaccumulation by Intestinal Microbiota.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34959282/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Unsupervised learning, Tree-based models
	- Published: 2021Nov25
	- Summary: Machine learning models predict drug susceptibility to metabolism and bioaccumulation by gut microbiota using supervised and unsupervised learning approaches.

- **[Fast screening of covariates in population models empowered by machine learning.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34019213/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Neural networks, Feature selection
	- Published: 2021Aug
	- Summary: ML methods including random forest, neural networks, and SVR are used to accelerate covariate selection in population pharmacokinetic modeling, outperforming traditional approaches.

- **[Highly Sensitive and Interference-Free Detection of Multiple Drug Molecules in Serum Using Dual-Modified SERS Substrates Combined with AI Algorithm Analysis.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39901357/)**
	- Published: 2025Feb18
	- Summary: AI algorithm is integrated to automatically recognize and analyze Raman spectral features for detecting multiple drug molecules in serum using SERS technology.

- **[Dynamic Contrast-enhanced MRI for Evaluating Breast Cancer Chemotherapy Response Using Conditional Generative Adversarial Networks.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41347917/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2026Jan
	- Summary: A conditional GAN is developed to rapidly translate DCE-MRI data to pharmacokinetic permeability maps for evaluating breast cancer chemotherapy response.

- **[Rational formulation design through retrospective machine learning methodology: Case study ibuprofen.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41687834/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2026Feb11
	- Summary: Machine learning is used to identify patterns in ibuprofen formulation data to understand how excipients and drug variants influence pharmacokinetic profiles.

- **[A hybrid machine learning/pharmacokinetic approach outperforms maximum a posteriori Bayesian estimation by selectively flattening model priors.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34270885/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2021Oct
	- Summary: A hybrid ML/PK approach uses machine learning to identify when flattened priors outperform MAP Bayesian estimation for precision dosing, improving vancomycin PK predictions.

- **[Systematic Evaluation of Local and Global Machine Learning Models for the Prediction of ADME Properties.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36745394/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2023Mar06
	- Summary: This paper systematically compares local vs global machine learning models for predicting ADME properties, finding global models consistently outperform local approaches.

- **[Human intestinal transporter database: QSAR modeling and virtual profiling of drug uptake, efflux and interactions.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/23269503/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2013Apr
	- Summary: QSAR modeling is used to predict drug-transporter interactions for assessing pharmacokinetic profiles and selecting viable drug candidates.

- **[Simulating realistic patient profiles from pharmacokinetic models by a machine learning postprocessing correction of residual variability.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38877660/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2024Sep
	- Summary: Machine learning methods, particularly Random Forest, are used to model residual unexplained variability in population pharmacokinetic models to generate realistic virtual patient profiles.

- **[Toward Dose Prediction at Point of Design.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39666573/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024Dec26
	- Summary: Machine learning models predict rat pharmacokinetics from chemical structure, then allometrically scale to human PK for early-stage human dose prediction in drug discovery.

- **[A Generative and Causal Pharmacokinetic Model for Factor VIII in Hemophilia A: A Machine Learning Framework for Continuous Model Refinement.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38372445/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Bayesian ML
	- Published: 2024Apr
	- Summary: Develops a hybrid ML-PK model using causal inference to correct for assay differences and generative modeling to simulate virtual patients and impute missing data in hemophilia A.

- **[Deep Learning Methods Applied to Drug Concentration Prediction of Olanzapine.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37111625/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Time-series modeling, Bayesian ML
	- Published: 2023Apr04
	- Summary: Deep learning LSTM-ANN model developed to predict olanzapine drug concentrations, optimized with Bayesian methods and compared against traditional NONMEM population PK modeling.

- **[Advances in Predictions of Oral Bioavailability of Candidate Drugs in Man with New Machine Learning Methodology.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33925103/)**
	- Methodology: Ensemble learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2021Apr28
	- Summary: The paper develops an integrated methodology combining 9 machine learning models with physiologically-based pharmacokinetic models to predict oral bioavailability from chemical structure.

- **[Prediction of Human Clearance Using In Silico Models with Reduced Bias.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38285644/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models, Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2024Mar04
	- Summary: Random forest models are developed to predict human drug clearance from in silico parameters, with bias reduction through removal of structurally similar compounds from training data.

- **[Machine Learning and Pharmacometrics for Prediction of Pharmacokinetic Data: Differences, Similarities and Challenges Illustrated with Rifampicin.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35893785/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2022Jul22
	- Summary: This paper compares ML algorithms (LASSO, Gradient Boosting, XGBoost, Random Forest) with pharmacometrics for predicting rifampicin pharmacokinetic profiles and AUC values.

- **[Qualifying drug dosing regimens in pediatrics using Gaussian processes.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33586203/)**
	- Methodology: Gaussian processes
	- Published: 2021May10
	- Summary: The paper uses Gaussian processes to detect deviations from adult-to-pediatric PK model extrapolation assumptions and qualify pediatric drug dosing regimens.

- **[Predicting mycophenolic acid exposure in pediatric lupus nephritis: a real-world study across three clinical scenarios.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41701178/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2026Feb17
	- Summary: Machine learning models were developed to predict mycophenolic acid exposure (AUC) in pediatric lupus nephritis patients for therapeutic monitoring and dose optimization.

- **[Mixed effect estimation in deep compartment models: Variational methods outperform first-order approximations.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38965175/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Bayesian ML, Neural networks
	- Published: 2024Dec
	- Summary: The paper extends deep compartment models with mixed-effects using variational inference to enable personalized drug predictions and treatment optimization.

	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2021
	- Summary: Machine learning approach using BioBERT embeddings to automatically identify and classify scientific publications reporting pharmacokinetic parameters from in vivo data.

	- Methodology: Neural networks, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2012Sep
	- Summary: This study develops an artificial neural network to predict blood tacrolimus concentrations in liver transplant recipients for therapeutic drug monitoring.

	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Neural networks, Feature selection

	- Summary: The paper uses artificial neural networks and support vector machines with feature selection to predict human volume of distribution values for drugs from molecular descriptors.

	- Summary: Machine learning models (Random Forest and Artificial Neural Networks) predict PK parameters for insulin analogs using novel molecular descriptors.

	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Ensemble learning

	- Summary: The paper develops ensemble graph neural networks (SolNet-GCN and SolNet-GAT) using geometric deep learning to predict molecular aqueous solubility for drug development.

	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Neural networks, Tree-based models, Feature selection

	- Summary: Machine learning algorithms including KNN, Random Forest, SVM, and Neural Networks are used to predict milk/plasma drug concentration ratios for breastfeeding safety assessment.

	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Mechanism-informed machine learning

	- Summary: DeepMetab uses graph neural networks with mechanistic knowledge to predict CYP450-mediated drug metabolism through integrated substrate profiling, site identification, and metabolite generation.

	- Methodology: Deep learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models

	- Summary: DrugAppy combines deep learning with computational chemistry methods for end-to-end drug discovery, predicting pharmacokinetics and activity against cancer targets.

- **[Gaussian process inference for estimating pharmacokinetic parameters of dynamic contrast-enhanced MR images.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/23286178/)**
	- Methodology: Gaussian processes
	- Published: 2012

- **[Quantitative structure-retention-pharmacokinetic relationship studies.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/19356082/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks

	- Summary: The paper uses artificial neural networks to predict pharmacokinetic parameters (half-life and volume of distribution) from molecular descriptors and chromatographic retention data.

- **[Utilization of Machine Learning Approaches for Drug Clearance Prediction and Population Pharmacokinetic Covariate Analysis.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40968579/)**

	- Summary: Machine learning models using molecular fingerprints predict pharmacokinetic properties (clearance, half-life, volume of distribution) for EGFR-targeting PROTAC molecules.

	- Methodology: Tree-based models

	- Summary: The paper uses gradient boosting machine to predict elimination half-lives of organic chemicals in humans as a pharmacokinetic modeling application.

	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Neural networks, Mechanism-informed machine learning

	- Summary: The paper demonstrates using scientific machine learning to integrate neural networks with compartmental PK models for learning unknown absorption while preserving interpretability.

	- Summary: The paper develops ML models using XGBoost and ChemBERTa to predict human clearance and volume of distribution by incorporating SMILES molecular representations.

	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2018
	- Summary: CNNs are used to directly estimate pharmacokinetic parameters from DCE-MRI image-time series for stroke patients, replacing conventional model fitting approaches.

- **[A Machine Learning Approach to Estimate the Glomerular Filtration Rate in Intensive Care Unit Patients Based on Plasma Iohexol Concentrations and Covariates.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32794122/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2021Feb
	- Summary: Machine learning is used to estimate glomerular filtration rate in ICU patients using sparse iohexol pharmacokinetic data and covariates.

- **[Predicting the blood concentration of levetiracetam in people with epilepsy using machine learning and real-world data.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41767611/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2026
	- Summary: This paper applies machine learning methods to predict levetiracetam blood concentrations in epilepsy patients using real-world data for therapeutic drug monitoring optimization.

- **[A Real-Time Plasma Concentration Prediction Model for Voriconazole in Elderly Patients via Machine Learning Combined with Population Pharmacokinetics.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40401163/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025
	- Summary: Machine learning combined with population pharmacokinetics to develop a real-time plasma concentration prediction model for voriconazole in elderly patients.

- **[Rational Design of Safer Inorganic Nanoparticles via Mechanistic Modeling-Informed Machine Learning.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40460056/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2025Jun17
	- Summary: ML framework predicts nanoparticle toxicity by integrating PBPK modeling with binary classification models to enable rational design of safer inorganic nanoparticles.

- **[Application of machine learning combined with population pharmacokinetics to improve individual prediction of vancomycin clearance in simulated adult patients.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38562463/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024
	- Summary: Machine learning is combined with population pharmacokinetics to improve individual prediction of vancomycin clearance for personalized dosing in adult patients.

- **[Effect of Cumulative Exposure on the Efficacy of Paroxetine: A Population Pharmacokinetic-Pharmacodynamic and Machine Learning Analyses.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40358139/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2025Jun
	- Summary: Machine learning models predict paroxetine treatment remission using PK/PD data, with SHAP values explaining feature contributions for depression outcome prediction.

- **[Integrating machine learning and molecular simulations for the design of potent HDAC2 inhibitors in diffuse large B-cell lymphoma.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41722288/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Feature selection

	- Summary: Random Forest QSAR modeling combined with molecular simulations to identify and optimize HDAC2 inhibitors for diffuse large B-cell lymphoma treatment.

- **[Learning pharmacokinetic models for in vivo glucocorticoid activation.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30048717/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning, Bayesian ML, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2018Oct14
	- Summary: A latent variable mixture model combines ML clustering with pharmacokinetic modeling to discover subpopulations with different glucocorticoid conversion patterns.

- **[Modeled Rat Hepatic and Plasma Concentrations of Chemicals after Virtual Administrations Using Two Sets of in Silico Liver-to-Plasma Partition Coefficients.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37380443/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2023Sep01
	- Summary: The paper uses machine learning systems to estimate pharmacokinetic parameters for modeling liver and plasma concentrations in rats using in silico partition coefficients.

- **[Multi-task convolutional neural networks for predicting in vitro clearance endpoints from molecular images.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35618861/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2022Jun
	- Summary: Multi-task convolutional neural networks predict multiple in vitro clearance endpoints from molecular images to optimize compound metabolic stability in drug development.

- **[Multi-Task ADME/PK prediction at industrial scale: leveraging large and diverse experimental datasets.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38973777/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2024Oct
	- Summary: Multi-task graph-based neural networks are used to predict ADME and animal PK endpoints using industrial-scale pharmaceutical datasets.

- **[In Silico Models of Human PK Parameters. Prediction of Volume of Distribution Using an Extensive Data Set and a Reduced Number of Parameters.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32891631/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models, Feature selection
	- Published: 2021Jan
	- Summary: Random forest model with six descriptors predicts human volume of distribution at steady-state for drug development and compound design.

- **[A multi-scale pipeline linking drug transcriptomics with pharmacokinetics predicts in vivo interactions of tuberculosis drugs.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33707554/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2021Mar11
	- Summary: Uses machine learning model INDIGO-MTB to predict drug interactions from transcriptomics data, integrated with mechanistic PK/PD modeling to optimize tuberculosis combination therapies.

- **[Prediction of human clearance from animal data and molecular structural parameters using multivariate regression analysis.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/12434392/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2002Dec
	- Summary: The paper uses multiple linear regression, partial least squares, and artificial neural networks to predict human drug clearance from animal data and molecular parameters.

- **[Prediction of drug distribution in rat and humans using an artificial neural networks ensemble and a PBPK model.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/24867425/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Ensemble learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2014Dec
	- Summary: The paper develops an artificial neural networks ensemble combined with PBPK modeling to predict drug tissue distribution coefficients in rats and humans.

	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Mechanism-informed machine learning

	- Summary: The paper proposes CMINNs using Physics-Informed Neural Networks with fractional calculus to enhance PK/PD modeling by capturing complex drug dynamics in heterogeneous tissues.

- **[Comparing Scientific Machine Learning With Population Pharmacokinetic and Classical Machine Learning Approaches for Prediction of Drug Concentrations.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39921335/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Supervised learning

	- Summary: The paper compares a scientific machine learning framework (MMPK-SciML) with classical ML and population PK models for predicting drug plasma concentrations using fluorouracil and sunitinib datasets.

- **[Non-invasive quantification of brain [¹⁸F]-FDG uptake by combining medical health records and dynamic PET imaging data.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/26736738/)**

	- Summary: The paper develops a non-invasive method combining machine learning with pharmacokinetic modeling to estimate brain glucose metabolism from PET imaging without arterial blood sampling.

	- Summary: Machine learning model developed to predict plasma nicotine levels from real-time physiological parameters and mouth level exposure during e-cigarette use.

	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2003Jan-Mar

- **[Integration of artificial neural network and physiologically based biopharmaceutic models in the development of sustained-release formulations.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37649136/)**

- **[Improving Vancomycin Through Level Prediction with Machine Learning: A Comparative Study of Feature Selection and Model Performance.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41336096/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Feature selection, Explainable AI, Ensemble learning

	- Summary: Machine learning models (Random Forest, LightGBM, XGBoost) are used to predict vancomycin trough levels for individualized dosing, outperforming traditional Bayesian methods.

- **[Machine learning and population pharmacokinetics: a hybrid approach for optimizing vancomycin therapy in sepsis patients.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40162774/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2025Mar31
	- Summary: This study compares PPK, Bayesian, ML, and hybrid PPK-ML models for predicting vancomycin AUC24 to optimize dosing in sepsis patients using machine learning approaches.

	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2014Nov
	- Summary: Uses cluster analysis and QSAR machine learning models to predict antiviral drug penetration into female genital tract based on molecular descriptors and pharmacokinetic parameters.

	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning, Time-series modeling

	- Summary: This paper applies unsupervised clustering methods to group similar plasma concentration-time curves for pharmacogenomic analysis and patient subgroup discovery.

- **[A Deep Learning-Based Framework for Highly Accelerated Prostate MR Dispersion Imaging.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39272841/)**

	- Published: 2024Aug27
	- Summary: Deep neural networks are used to accelerate pharmacokinetic parameter estimation in MR dispersion imaging for prostate cancer, replacing time-consuming nonlinear curve fitting.

- **[Retrospective quantification of clinical abdominal DCE-MRI using pharmacokinetics-informed deep learning: a proof-of-concept study.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37731600/)**

	- Methodology: Neural networks, Model selection

	- Summary: The paper develops a new estimation approach for nonlinear mixed effects models in population PK/PD that uses neural networks as basis functions for nonparametric covariate modeling.

	- Summary: The paper proposes a 1DCNN-Attention model optimized by sparrow search algorithm for predicting drug concentrations in pharmacokinetic modeling of phenobarbital.

	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Model selection, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2025Oct
	- Summary: ML-based XGBoost model selects and averages population PK models for individualized vancomycin dosing, outperforming single-model approaches in precision dosing applications.

- **[In Silico Prediction of Human Intravenous Pharmacokinetic Parameters with Improved Accuracy.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31403793/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Feature selection, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2019Sep23
	- Summary: Machine learning models (SVM, RF, GBM, XGBoost) with Boruta feature selection predict human IV pharmacokinetic parameters from molecular structure data.

- **[Construction and Interpretation of Prediction Model of Teicoplanin Trough Concentration via Machine Learning.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35360734/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2022
	- Summary: Machine learning models are developed to predict teicoplanin trough concentrations with SHAP method used to explain feature importance for dose optimization.

- **[Prediction of Human Liver Microsome Clearance with Chirality-Focused Graph Neural Networks.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38976447/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2024Jul22
	- Summary: The paper develops chirality-focused graph neural networks to predict human liver microsome clearance, with TetraDMPNN achieving best performance for drug clearance prediction.

- **[Plasma protein binding prediction focusing on residue-level features and circularity of cyclic peptides by deep learning.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34849593/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2022Jan27
	- Summary: Deep learning is used to predict plasma protein binding rates of cyclic peptides by incorporating residue-level features and structural circularity information.

- **[New Machine Learning Models for Predicting the Organic Cation Transporters OCT1, OCT2, and OCT3 Uptake.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41048715/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2025Sep30
	- Summary: Machine learning models using decision tree ensemble algorithms predict organic cation transporter uptake to forecast pharmacokinetic liabilities in drug development.

- **[Selective Fusion of Heterogeneous Classifiers for Predicting Substrates of Membrane Transporters.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/28228010/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2017Mar27
	- Summary: The paper develops QSAR and PCM models using machine learning classifiers and ensemble methods to predict whether small molecules are substrates of membrane transporters.

- **[Coverage bias in small molecule machine learning.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39788952/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Jan09
	- Summary: This paper investigates coverage bias in small molecule ML datasets using a novel distance measure based on Maximum Common Edge Subgraph to assess dataset representativeness.

- **[Multiple pharmacokinetic parameter prediction for a series of cephalosporins.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/12587116/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2003Mar
	- Summary: Uses artificial neural networks to simultaneously predict multiple pharmacokinetic parameters for cephalosporins from molecular structure descriptors.

	- Summary: Neural networks and Rough Sets are used to predict human methylmercury elimination half-life from animal pharmacokinetic data for toxicity assessment.

- **[Tacrolimus pharmacokinetics in pediatric nephrotic syndrome: A combination of population pharmacokinetic modelling and machine learning approaches to improve individual prediction.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36457704/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models, Ensemble learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models

	- Summary: Combines population pharmacokinetic modeling with machine learning algorithms (XGBoost, Random Forest, etc.) to improve individual tacrolimus dose prediction in pediatric nephrotic syndrome.

	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks

	- Summary: Uses Transformer-based deep learning to predict ADMET properties and optimize lead compounds while preserving core scaffolds and biological activity.

- **[Machine Learning Prediction of the Three Main Input Parameters of a Simplified Physiologically Based Pharmacokinetic Model Subsequently Used to Generate Time-Dependent Plasma Concentration Data in Humans after Oral Doses of 212 Disparate Chemicals.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34732590/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning

	- Summary: Machine learning algorithms predict PBPK model parameters to estimate plasma concentrations and chemical exposures for 212 compounds after oral administration.

	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2023Jul-Dec
	- Summary: Machine learning techniques are used to predict busulfan area under the curve at steady state in hematopoietic stem cell transplant patients.

- **[Deconvolution-Based Pharmacokinetic Analysis to Improve the Prediction of Pathological Information of Breast Cancer.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38343210/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Feature selection

	- Summary: Uses random forest classifiers with radiomic features from deconvolution-based PK parameter maps to predict breast cancer molecular subtypes and pathological characteristics.

- **[FormulationLAI: A physiology-based machine learning framework for accelerated development of long-acting injectable formulations.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41260272/)**

	- Summary: The paper presents a computational framework integrating ML with PBPK modeling and MD simulations to accelerate long-acting injectable formulation development and optimization.

- **[Evaluation of ADMET Predictor in Early Discovery Drug Metabolism and Pharmacokinetics Project Work.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34750195/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks

	- Summary: The paper evaluates ADMET Predictor software for predicting drug properties and builds local artificial neural network models for improved ADME predictions.

- **[Evaluation of the Success of High-Throughput Physiologically Based Pharmacokinetic (HT-PBPK) Modeling Predictions to Inform Early Drug Discovery.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35476457/)**

	- Summary: The paper evaluates a hybrid approach combining machine learning predictions with PBPK modeling to predict rat pharmacokinetic parameters for drug discovery applications.

	- Methodology: Neural networks, Deep learning, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2021Jul
	- Summary: Develops an artificial neural network model for pharmacokinetic prediction with SHAP explanations to interpret model predictions and identify influential factors.

	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Feature selection, Explainable AI, Ensemble learning

	- Summary: Machine learning models including Random Forest, LightGBM, and XGBoost with SHAP explainability are used to optimize anti-breast cancer drug candidates for biological activity and ADMET properties.

- **[Target and Tissue Selectivity Prediction by Integrated Mechanistic Pharmacokinetic-Target Binding and Quantitative Structure Activity Modeling.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/29204742/)**

	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks

	- Summary: Deep-PK uses graph neural networks to predict ADMET properties and toxicity for small molecules across 73 endpoints, providing a platform for drug optimization.

- **[A Machine Learning Approach to Predict Interdose Vancomycin Exposure.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35411504/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models
	- Published: 2022Apr
	- Summary: XGBoost algorithms are trained to predict vancomycin AUC from early concentrations and patient features as an alternative to population pharmacokinetic approaches.

- **[VTDCE-Net: A time invariant deep neural network for direct estimation of pharmacokinetic parameters from undersampled DCE MRI data.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36354289/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks

	- Summary: A deep neural network is developed to directly estimate pharmacokinetic parameters from undersampled DCE MRI data in a time and space invariant manner.

- **[Pharmacokinetic parameter prediction from drug structure using artificial neural networks.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/14726136/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks

	- Summary: This paper uses artificial neural networks to predict human pharmacokinetic parameters (clearance, protein binding, volume of distribution) from drug molecular structures.

	- Summary: Deep-NCA uses deep learning to improve noncompartmental analysis of sparse pharmacokinetic data, demonstrating superior performance over traditional NCA methods.

	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Transfer learning

	- Summary: Deep neural networks are used as a prescreening tool to automatically classify and assign individualized absorption models in population pharmacokinetic analyses.

	- Published: 2025Aug20
	- Summary: Machine learning was employed to identify factors influencing individual clearance in a PBPK population model for end-stage renal disease patients to enable precision dosing.

- **[Dynamic Contrast Enhanced MRI Mapping of Vascular Permeability for Evaluation of Breast Cancer Neoadjuvant Chemotherapy Response Using Image-to-Image Conditional Generative Adversarial Networks.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39281733/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2024Sep05
	- Summary: Uses conditional GANs to rapidly translate DCE MRI images to pharmacokinetic vascular permeability parameter maps for breast cancer treatment response assessment.

- **[Predicting drug metabolism and pharmacokinetics features of in-house compounds by a hybrid machine-learning model.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33991751/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2021Aug
	- Summary: Machine learning models predict drug metabolism and pharmacokinetics with applicability domain assessment to improve reliability of predictions for in-house compounds.

- **[Ensemble of expert deep neural networks for spatio-temporal denoising of contrast-enhanced MRI sequences.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/28802145/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2017Dec
	- Summary: Deep neural network ensemble with autoencoders is used for spatio-temporal denoising of DCE-MRI sequences to improve pharmacokinetic parameter extraction for blood-brain barrier assessment.

- **[Enhancing the Predictive Power of Macrocyclic Drug Permeability by Knowledge Distillation from Analogous Pretraining Data.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41420604/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Supervised learning, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2026Jan08
	- Summary: Multi_DDPP uses deep learning with knowledge distillation to predict macrocyclic drug membrane permeability from 2D molecular structures, improving drug development efficiency.

- **[Generative models for synthetic data generation: application to pharmacokinetic/pharmacodynamic data.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39192091/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2024Dec
	- Summary: This paper benchmarks deep learning generative models (GANs, TimeGAN, PAR) for generating synthetic pharmacokinetic/pharmacodynamic patient data to enable data access and augmentation.

- **[Population pharmacokinetic and pharmacodynamic models of remifentanil in healthy volunteers using artificial neural network analysis.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/17324247/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2007Jul
	- Summary: This study uses artificial neural networks to develop population PK/PD models for remifentanil and predict EEG approximate entropy overshoot during recovery in healthy volunteers.

- **[Artificial Neural Networks approach to pharmacokinetic model selection in DCE-MRI studies.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/27876537/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2016Dec
	- Summary: The paper proposes using Artificial Neural Networks for pharmacokinetic model selection in Dynamic Contrast Enhanced MRI studies to address computation time and fitting issues.

- **[A machine learning approach to population pharmacokinetic modelling automation.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40745042/)**
	- Methodology: Model selection
	- Published: 2025Jul31
	- Summary: This paper presents an automated machine learning approach using optimization algorithms (pyDarwin) to streamline population pharmacokinetic model development for extravascular drugs.

- **[Predicting Total Drug Clearance and Volumes of Distribution Using the Machine Learning-Mediated Multimodal Method through the Imputation of Various Nonclinical Data.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35993595/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2022Sep12
	- Summary: Machine learning methods with missing-value imputation and feature selection are used to predict human drug clearance and volume of distribution from nonclinical data.

- **[Integrating artificial intelligence and physiologically based pharmacokinetic modeling to predict in vitro and in vivo fate of amorphous solid dispersions.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40825400/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Oct10
	- Summary: This paper integrates AI with PBPK modeling using TabPFN to predict molecular dissolution and systemic absorption of amorphous solid dispersions for drug development.

- **[Application of Machine Learning and Mechanistic Modeling to Predict Intravenous Pharmacokinetic Profiles in Humans.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40146185/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2025Apr10
	- Summary: This paper develops machine learning models combined with physiologically based pharmacokinetic modeling to predict human IV pharmacokinetic profiles from compound properties.

- **[Bioavailability prediction based on molecular structure for a diverse series of drugs.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/14984260/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2004Jan
	- Summary: Radial basis function artificial neural networks are used with molecular descriptors to predict drug bioavailability through quantitative structure-pharmacokinetic relationships.

- **[Prediction of vancomycin plasma concentration in elderly patients based on multi-algorithm mining combined with population pharmacokinetics.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39511378/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Feature selection, Ensemble learning, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2024Nov08
	- Summary: This study develops an ensemble ML model combining SVR, LightGBM, and CatBoost with popPK parameters to predict vancomycin plasma concentrations in elderly patients for personalized dosing.

- **[Impact of gut permeability on estimation of oral bioavailability for chemicals in commerce and the environment.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39434630/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models
	- Published: 2025
	- Summary: Random forest QSPR model predicts gut permeability to improve pharmacokinetic modeling and bioavailability estimation for non-pharmaceutical chemicals in toxicity assessment.

- **[Exploration of sexual dimorphism and inter-individual variability in multivariate parameter spaces for a pharmacokinetic compartment model.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30557560/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2019Feb
	- Summary: Uses support vector machine and principal component analysis with global sensitivity analysis to explore sexual dimorphism and variability in pharmacokinetic model parameters.

- **[Toward Generalizable Data-Driven Pharmacokinetics with Interpretable Neural ODEs.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41738401/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Neural networks
	- Published: 2026Mar09
	- Summary: Proposes Uni-PK, a neural ODE framework combining molecular representations with mechanistic PK structure for generalizable drug concentration-time modeling.

## Postmarketing surveillance

- **[PharmBERT: a domain-specific BERT model for drug labels.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37317617/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, LLM
	- Published: 2023Jul20
	- Summary: The paper develops PharmBERT, a domain-specific BERT model pretrained on drug labels for improved NLP tasks like adverse reaction extraction and drug interaction detection.

- **[Harnessing large language models' zero-shot and few-shot learning capabilities for regulatory research.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39177261/)**
	- Methodology: LLM
	- Published: 2024Jul25
	- Summary: The paper evaluates open-source LLMs for extracting clinical pharmacology information from regulatory drug labels using zero-shot and few-shot learning approaches.

- **[Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) for Evaluating Regulatory Compliance of Drug Information and Clinical Trial Protocols.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41709726/)**
	- Methodology: LLM
	- Published: 2026Mar
	- Summary: The paper evaluates RAG and LLM systems for assessing regulatory compliance of drug information and clinical trial protocols against FDA guidance documents.

- **[Discovering Severe Adverse Reactions From Pharmacokinetic Drug-Drug Interactions Through Literature Analysis and Electronic Health Record Verification.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39585167/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Deep learning
	- Published: 2025Apr
	- Summary: Uses NLP techniques including Named Entity Recognition and Relation Extraction to discover severe adverse drug reactions from drug-drug interactions through literature mining and EHR validation.

- **[Adverse event profile of tigecycline: data mining of the public version of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration adverse event reporting system.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/22687540/)**
	- Methodology: Bayesian ML
	- Published: 2012
	- Summary: Uses Bayesian confidence propagation neural networks and other pharmacovigilance tools for signal detection to identify tigecycline-associated adverse events from FDA reporting data.

- **[Application of surface-enhanced Raman scattering combined with artificial intelligence for multiplex detection of chemotherapeutic agents used in solid tumors.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41324706/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2025Dec01
	- Summary: The paper uses convolutional neural networks to analyze SERS spectra for automated detection and quantification of multiple chemotherapeutic drugs in serum.

- **[Maternal and Pediatric Precision in Therapeutic Knowledge Portal (MPRINT-KP): Landscape Analysis of Pharmacology Research in Maternal and Pediatric Patient Populations.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41730795/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2026Mar
	- Summary: BioBERT models were used to classify and curate pharmacology research papers for maternal and pediatric populations in the MPRINT-KP knowledge portal.

- **[Natural language processing-based assessment of consistency in summaries of product characteristics of generic antimicrobials.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30455958/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2018Dec
	- Summary: Natural language processing is used to automatically assess consistency between original and generic antimicrobial drug labels for regulatory surveillance purposes.

## Precision medicine / optimized treatment regimen

- **[Ensemble Machine Learning Model for Real-Time Valproic Acid Prediction in Epilepsy Treatment.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40456293/)**
	- Methodology: Ensemble learning, Tree-based models, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2025Jun02
	- Summary: Develops ensemble ML model using gradient boosting algorithms with SHAP interpretation to predict valproic acid concentrations for optimized epilepsy treatment dosing.

- **[Deep learning prediction of patient response time course from early data via neural-pharmacokinetic/pharmacodynamic modelling.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40271424/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Neural networks, Time-series modeling, Mechanism-informed machine learning
	- Published: 2021Aug
	- Summary: Neural-PK/PD framework combines deep learning with pharmacological principles using neural ODEs to predict patient response time courses and simulate untested dosing regimens.

- **[Artificial intelligence-guided precision treatment of chronic kidney disease-mineral bone disorder.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35920131/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Reinforcement learning
	- Published: 2022Oct
	- Summary: The paper uses reinforcement learning combined with quantitative systems pharmacology modeling to optimize dosing of multiple medications for chronic kidney disease-mineral bone disorder treatment.

- **[Prediction of infliximab and anti-drug antibody concentrations in patients with inflammatory bowel disease using machine learning models with real-world data from a prospective cohort study.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41685197/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2026
	- Summary: Machine learning models were developed to predict infliximab and anti-drug antibody concentrations in IBD patients as an alternative to complex population PK models.

- **[Drug Clearance in Neonates: A Combination of Population Pharmacokinetic Modelling and Machine Learning Approaches to Improve Individual Prediction.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34041714/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2021Nov
	- Summary: The paper combines population pharmacokinetic modeling with machine learning approaches to improve individual drug clearance prediction in neonates.

- **[A machine learning approach to personalized dose adjustment of lamotrigine using noninvasive clinical parameters.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33692435/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Model selection
	- Published: 2021Mar10
	- Summary: Machine learning algorithms, particularly extra-trees regression, are used to predict lamotrigine dose-adjusted concentrations for personalized dose optimization using clinical parameters.

- **[Utility of the 13 C-pantoprazole breath test as a CYP2C19 phenotyping probe for children.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35099109/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models, Feature selection
	- Published: 2022May
	- Summary: Random forest models with feature engineering were used to predict CYP2C19 metabolizer phenotypes from breath test data to guide personalized PPI dosing in children.

- **[Deep compartment models: A deep learning approach for the reliable prediction of time-series data in pharmacokinetic modeling.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38100092/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Time-series modeling
	- Published: 2022Jul
	- Summary: The paper proposes deep compartment models combining neural networks with ODEs for reliable pharmacokinetic modeling and drug concentration prediction.

- **[Supervised Machine-Learning Reveals That Old and Obese People Achieve Low Dapsone Concentrations.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/28575552/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2017Aug
	- Summary: Supervised ML using multivariate adaptive regression splines for covariate selection to identify complex weight/age effects on dapsone PK parameters for personalized dosing.

- **[Machine learning approach for dosage individualization of azithromycin in children with community-acquired pneumonia.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40181615/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Aug
	- Summary: Machine learning is used to predict azithromycin AUC0-24 in children with pneumonia to enable individualized dosing regimens.

- **[Development of neuro-fuzzy model to explore gene-nutrient interactions modulating warfarin dose requirement.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/27462768/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2016Aug
	- Summary: The paper develops a neuro-fuzzy model to explore gene-nutrient interactions affecting warfarin dosing requirements, combining neural networks with fuzzy logic for personalized dose optimization.

- **[Virtual patient analysis identifies strategies to improve the performance of predictive biomarkers for PD-1 blockade.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39467131/)**
	- Methodology: Feature selection, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2024Nov05
	- Summary: Uses ML-based feature selection with quantitative systems pharmacology modeling to identify and optimize predictive biomarkers for PD-1 blockade response in TNBC patients.

- **[Dose-time-concentration prediction method based on GRU-TCN with temporal-channel attention.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41201288/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Time-series modeling
	- Published: 2025Nov07
	- Summary: The paper develops a GRU-TCN deep learning model with attention mechanisms for predicting drug concentration-time profiles to support precision dosing decisions.

- **[Assessing GPT-4's accuracy in answering clinical pharmacological questions on pain therapy.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40066678/)**
	- Methodology: LLM
	- Published: 2025Aug
	- Summary: This study evaluates GPT-4's accuracy in answering clinical pharmacological questions about pain therapy to assess its potential as a patient-facing medical information tool.

- **[DeepTDM: Deep Learning-Based Prediction of Sequential Therapeutic Drug Monitoring Levels of Vancomycin.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41220794/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2025
	- Summary: Deep learning is used to predict sequential vancomycin therapeutic drug monitoring levels in critically ill patients to optimize dosing and improve treatment outcomes.

- **[Multicycle Dosimetric Behavior and Dose-Effect Relationships in [177Lu]Lu-DOTATATE Peptide Receptor Radionuclide Therapy.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40274371/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2025Jun02
	- Summary: Deep learning algorithm used for kidney segmentation in dosimetric analysis to develop personalized dosimetry-guided PRRT treatments for neuroendocrine tumors.

- **[Machine learning estimation of FVIII pharmacokinetic parameters in Chinese children with severe Hemophilia A.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41760637/)**
	- Methodology: LLM
	- Published: 2026Feb27
	- Summary: Machine learning framework incorporating language models predicts FVIII pharmacokinetic parameters in pediatric hemophilia A patients for personalized prophylactic treatment.

- **[A Deep Learning-Based Approach for Prediction of Vancomycin Treatment Monitoring: Retrospective Study Among Patients With Critical Illness.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38152042/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2024Mar08
	- Summary: Deep learning approach developed to predict vancomycin therapeutic drug monitoring levels in ICU patients to assist clinicians with dosing decisions.

- **[Hybrid Population Pharmacokinetic-Machine Learning Modeling to Predict Infliximab Pharmacokinetics in Pediatric and Young Adult Patients with Crohn's Disease.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40885855/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2025Nov
	- Summary: The paper develops a hybrid approach combining machine learning with population PK-based Bayesian methods to improve infliximab concentration predictions in pediatric Crohn's disease patients.

- **[Long short-term memory algorithm for personalized tacrolimus dosing: A simple and effective time series forecasting approach post-lung transplantation.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39510206/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Time-series modeling
	- Published: 2025Mar
	- Summary: Uses LSTM neural networks for time series forecasting to predict tacrolimus trough levels and optimize dosing in lung transplant patients.

- **[Automatic treatment planning based on three-dimensional dose distribution predicted from deep learning technique.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30383300/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2019Jan
	- Summary: Deep learning is used to predict 3D dose distributions for automated IMRT treatment planning in radiation therapy to optimize patient-specific dose delivery.

- **[A Prediction Model for Tacrolimus Daily Dose in Kidney Transplant Recipients With Machine Learning and Deep Learning Techniques.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35712101/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2022
	- Summary: This study compares machine learning and deep learning algorithms to develop individualized tacrolimus dose prediction models for kidney transplant recipients.

- **[Optimization of therapeutic antibodies for reduced self-association and non-specific binding via interpretable machine learning.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37666923/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2024Jan
	- Summary: Interpretable machine learning classifiers predict antibody mutations to optimize therapeutic properties while reducing off-target binding and self-association.

- **[Optimal use of β-lactams in neonates: machine learning-based clinical decision support system.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38917512/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024Jul
	- Summary: Machine learning-based clinical decision support system for optimal β-lactam antibiotic dose selection in neonatal sepsis treatment.

- **[Mechanism-informed machine learning for individualized tacrolimus dose adjustment in the early post-kidney transplant period.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41531243/)**
	- Methodology: Mechanism-informed machine learning
	- Published: 2026Jan13
	- Summary: The paper develops mechanism-informed ML models to optimize individualized tacrolimus dosing in kidney transplant patients during the early post-transplant period.

- **[A PopPBPK-RL approach for precision dosing of benazepril in renal impaired patients.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39663294/)**
	- Methodology: Reinforcement learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Feature selection
	- Published: 2024Dec11
	- Summary: The paper develops a hybrid PBPK-reinforcement learning system for precision dosing of benazepril in renal impaired patients using patient stratification and feature selection.

- **[Nephrocast-V: A Deep Learning Model for the Prediction of Vancomycin Trough Concentration Using Electronic Health Record Data.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41025800/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2026Jan
	- Summary: A deep learning model is developed to predict vancomycin trough concentrations using electronic health record data to optimize therapeutic dosing.

- **[Supporting Machine Learning Model in the Treatment of Chronic Pain.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37509416/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2023Jun21
	- Summary: ML model predicts chronic pain treatment outcomes using cannabis based on patient genomic polymorphisms to enable personalized therapy and avoid ineffective treatments.

- **[Improving Genotype Imputation in High-Dimensional Pharmacogenomics Using Multiple Imputation: Evaluation with Machine Learning Approaches.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41407383/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Feature selection
	- Published: 2025Dec17
	- Summary: The paper develops a multiple imputation framework using machine learning approaches including random forest and penalized regression for genotype imputation and covariate selection in pharmacogenomics.

- **[Deep reinforcement learning identifies personalized intermittent androgen deprivation therapy for prostate cancer.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38493345/)**
	- Methodology: Reinforcement learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2024Jan22
	- Summary: Deep reinforcement learning is used to develop personalized intermittent androgen deprivation therapy for prostate cancer by learning patient-specific tumor dynamics and optimizing treatment policies.

- **[A translational multimodal machine-learning prototype predicting valproate response in epilepsy treatment.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40909818/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2025Aug27
	- Summary: Develops a multimodal machine learning classifier integrating genetic and in-vitro data to predict valproate treatment response in epilepsy patients for personalized treatment decisions.

- **[In silico comparative characterization of pharmacogenomic missense variants.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/25057096/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2014
	- Summary: Uses computational methods with sequence conservation and structural features to predict and discriminate pharmacogenomic missense variants from neutral and disease variants.

- **[Leveraging Predictive Pharmacometrics-Based Algorithms to Enhance Perinatal Care-Application to Neonatal Jaundice.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36034866/)**
	- Methodology: Bayesian ML
	- Published: 2022
	- Summary: Develops a pharmacometrics-based algorithm using Empirical Bayesian Estimation to predict bilirubin kinetics in neonates for personalized jaundice management.

- **[Building virtual patients using simulation-based inference.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40809131/)**
	- Methodology: Bayesian ML, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2024
	- Summary: Uses simulation-based inference (SBI) to generate virtual patients from individual patient data for QSP models, demonstrated on rheumatoid arthritis treatment response prediction.

- **[Artificial intelligence and precision medicine: a pilot study predicting optimal ceftaroline dosage for pediatric patients.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41626576/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025
	- Summary: Machine learning models are used to predict optimal ceftaroline dosage for pediatric patients to enable individualized therapy and overcome age-related physiological variability.

- **[Comparison of population pharmacokinetic modeling and machine learning approaches for predicting voriconazole trough concentrations in critically ill patients.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39732295/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Feb
	- Summary: This study compares machine learning approaches with traditional population pharmacokinetic models for predicting voriconazole trough concentrations in ICU patients.

- **[Machine learning-guided clinical pharmacist interventions improve treatment outcomes in tuberculosis patients: a precision medicine approach.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41487272/)**
	- Methodology: Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2025
	- Summary: Machine learning techniques are used to identify TB patient subtypes and optimize clinical pharmacist interventions for precision medicine treatment approaches.

- **[Optimizing warfarin dosing for patients with atrial fibrillation using machine learning.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38402362/)**
	- Methodology: Reinforcement learning, Deep learning
	- Published: 2024Feb24
	- Summary: This paper uses deep reinforcement learning with a semi-Markov decision process to optimize warfarin dosing for atrial fibrillation patients to achieve therapeutic INR range.

- **[Explainable cluster-based learning for prediction of postprandial glycemic events and insulin dose optimization in type 1 diabetes.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40956852/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Unsupervised learning, Tree-based models, Explainable AI, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2025Sep
	- Summary: Uses cluster-based ensemble ML with explainable AI to predict postprandial glycemic events and optimize insulin dosing in type 1 diabetes patients.

- **[A supervised machine-learning approach for the efficient development of a multi method (LC-MS) for a large number of drugs and subsets thereof: focus on oral antitumor agents.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37606251/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024Jan26
	- Summary: The paper uses supervised machine learning to efficiently develop LC-MS assays for therapeutic drug monitoring of oral antitumor agents to support individualized medicine.

- **[Identification of eight genomic protective alleles for mitochondrial diabetes by Kinship-graph convolutional network.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38157301/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2024Jan
	- Summary: Uses kinship-graph convolutional networks to identify nuclear genome variants that influence clinical phenotypes in mitochondrial diabetes patients with m.3243A>G mutation.

- **[Optimising the therapeutic response of statins using real-world evidence and machine learning: Personalised precision dosing recommends lower statin doses for some patients.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39513324/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Jan
	- Summary: Machine learning is used with real-world evidence to optimize statin dosing for personalized therapeutic responses, recommending lower doses for some patients.

- **[Machine learning-based prediction of sertraline concentration in patients with depression through therapeutic drug monitoring.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38510645/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Ensemble learning, Neural networks, Model selection
	- Published: 2024
	- Summary: Machine learning algorithms including XGBoost are used to predict sertraline plasma concentrations for personalized dosing in depression patients using therapeutic drug monitoring data.

- **[A Machine Learning Model to Predict Risperidone Active Moiety Concentration Based on Initial Therapeutic Drug Monitoring.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34867511/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Feature selection, Model selection, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2021
	- Summary: Machine learning models, primarily XGBoost, are used to predict risperidone active moiety concentrations for therapeutic drug monitoring optimization based on initial TDM data.

- **[Identification of Candidate Therapeutic Genes for More Precise Treatment of Esophageal Squamous Cell Carcinoma and Adenocarcinoma.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35664298/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Feature selection, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2022
	- Summary: Uses ensemble feature selection and random forest classification to identify differentially expressed genes as therapeutic targets for personalized esophageal cancer treatment.

- **[A Rapid Pharmacogenomic Assay to Detect NAT2 Polymorphisms and Guide Isoniazid Dosing for Tuberculosis Treatment.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34375564/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models
	- Published: 2021Dec01
	- Summary: Random forest models predict NAT2 acetylation genotype from SNP data to guide personalized isoniazid dosing for tuberculosis treatment through a point-of-care pharmacogenomic assay.

- **[Pharmacogenetic Analysis of the Model-Based Pharmacokinetics of Five Anti-HIV Drugs: How Does This Influence the Effect of Aging?](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/29205871/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2018Mar
	- Summary: Random Forest ensemble methods were used to analyze pharmacogenetic-aging interactions affecting antiretroviral drug pharmacokinetics for precision dosing in HIV patients.

- **[An interpretable stacking ensemble learning framework based on multi-dimensional data for real-time prediction of drug concentration: The example of olanzapine.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36238557/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Feature selection, Explainable AI, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2022
	- Summary: Develops an interpretable stacking ensemble ML framework using multiple tree-based models to predict olanzapine drug concentrations in real-time for personalized dosing.

- **[Infliximab monitoring in Crohn's disease: a neural network approach for evaluating disease activity and immunogenicity.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39664232/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2024
	- Summary: This paper uses neural networks to evaluate disease activity and immunogenicity in Crohn's disease patients receiving infliximab for therapeutic drug monitoring.

- **[Parameterized SVM for personalized drug concentration prediction.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/24111054/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2013
	- Summary: The paper proposes ParaSVM, combining SVM with analytical models to predict personalized drug concentration-time curves that can be calibrated using TDM measurements.

- **[Hybrid Population PK-Machine Learning Modeling to Predict Infliximab Pharmacokinetics in Pediatric and Young Adult Patients with Crohn's Disease.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40654807/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Neural networks, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2025May07
	- Summary: Combines machine learning algorithms with population PK Bayesian methods to improve infliximab concentration predictions in pediatric Crohn's disease patients for better dose individualization.

- **[A genetic risk score is significantly associated with statin therapy response in the elderly population.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/27943270/)**
	- Methodology: Tree-based models, Feature selection
	- Published: 2017Mar
	- Summary: Random forest models were used to develop a weighted genetic risk score for predicting statin therapy response and LDL-C reduction in elderly populations.

- **[Machine learning and individual variability in electric field characteristics predict tDCS treatment response.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33049412/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2020Nov-Dec
	- Summary: Machine learning models predict working memory improvements from tDCS treatment using MRI-derived electric field characteristics for precision cognitive intervention.

- **[A hybrid machine learning/pharmacokinetic approach outperforms maximum a posteriori Bayesian estimation by selectively flattening model priors.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34270885/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2021Oct
	- Summary: A hybrid ML/PK approach uses machine learning to identify when flattened priors outperform MAP Bayesian estimation for precision dosing, improving vancomycin PK predictions.

- **[Model-Informed Deep Q-Networks to Guide Infliximab Dosing in Pediatric Crohn's Disease.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41189499/)**
	- Methodology: Reinforcement learning, Deep learning
	- Published: 2026Feb
	- Summary: Deep Q-Network reinforcement learning is used to automate and optimize infliximab dosing decisions for pediatric Crohn's disease patients using population PK models.

- **[A Generative and Causal Pharmacokinetic Model for Factor VIII in Hemophilia A: A Machine Learning Framework for Continuous Model Refinement.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38372445/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Bayesian ML
	- Published: 2024Apr
