	- Published: 2011
	- Summary: The paper uses Support Vector Machines to predict drug concentrations for dose individualization, comparing SVM performance to traditional pharmacokinetic models.

- **[Enhancing targeted tumor treatment: A novel fuzzy logic framework for precision drug delivery strategy selection.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39146841/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024Sep
	- Summary: The paper introduces a fuzzy logic framework for selecting optimal drug delivery strategies in tumor treatment, enhancing precision medicine approaches.

- **[A simulation-based framework for modeling and prediction of personalized blood pressure trajectories in hypertensive patients after antihypertensive treatment.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40209154/)**
	- Methodology: Gaussian processes
	- Published: 2025
	- Summary: The paper develops a simulation framework for blood pressure trajectories using PK/PD modeling and evaluates Gaussian processes for predicting steady-state blood pressure under treatment.

- **[A Machine Learning-Based Identification of Genes Affecting the Pharmacokinetics of Tacrolimus Using the DMETTM Plus Platform.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32260456/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Feature selection
	- Published: 2020Apr04
	- Summary: Machine learning algorithms including decision trees, random forest, and LASSO regression were used to identify genetic variants affecting tacrolimus pharmacokinetics for individualized dosing.

- **[Variational autoencoders for generative modeling of drug dosing determinants in renal, hepatic, metabolic, and cardiac disease states.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38949489/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2024Jul
	- Summary: This study uses variational autoencoders to generate synthetic data for physiological determinants of drug dosing across multiple disease states for precision medicine applications.

- **[Beyond Algorithms: Machine Learning and Clinical Determinants of Voriconazole Plasma Levels in Therapeutic Drug Monitoring.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41288921/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2026Jan
	- Summary: Machine learning is used to predict subtherapeutic or supratherapeutic voriconazole plasma levels for therapeutic drug monitoring optimization.

- **[Machine-learning model to predict the tacrolimus concentration and suggest optimal dose in liver transplantation recipients: a multicenter retrospective cohort study.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39198694/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Tree-based models, Time-series modeling, Neural networks
	- Published: 2024Aug28
	- Summary: LSTM and GBRT models predict tacrolimus concentrations and suggest optimal dosing in liver transplant recipients to improve therapeutic outcomes.

- **[Harnessing adaptive novelty for automated generation of cancer treatments.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33217377/)**
	- Methodology: Reinforcement learning, AI Agents
	- Published: 2021Jan
	- Summary: The paper presents an AI agent-based model using adaptive novelty and machine learning to automatically generate and optimize nanoparticle cancer treatments for heterogeneous tumors.

- **[Continuous action deep reinforcement learning for propofol dosing during general anesthesia.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34998516/)**
	- Methodology: Reinforcement learning, Deep learning
	- Published: 2022Jan
	- Summary: The paper uses deep reinforcement learning to automate propofol dosing during general anesthesia for precise control of patient unconsciousness levels.

- **[Impact of sex and pathophysiology on optimal drug choice in hypertensive rats: quantitative insights for precision medicine.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33870137/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2021Apr23
	- Summary: The paper integrates quantitative systems pharmacology models with machine learning to predict optimal antihypertensive drug selection based on sex and pathophysiology.

- **[Insulin dose optimization using an automated artificial intelligence-based decision support system in youths with type 1 diabetes.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32908282/)**
	- Methodology: AI Agents
	- Published: 2020Sep
	- Summary: An AI-based decision support system was tested for automated insulin dose optimization in youth with type 1 diabetes, showing non-inferiority to physician-guided adjustments.

- **[Targeting the glabellar frown lines with OnabotulinumtoxinA: In-silico evidence supporting concentrated, low-volume injection protocols.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40907830/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2025Nov
	- Summary: Uses machine learning for off-target risk prediction integrated with mechanistic PK/PD modeling to optimize BoNT-A dosing protocols for glabellar frown line treatment.

- **[Machine learning-optimized non-invasive brain stimulation and treatment response classification for major depression.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39473014/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024Oct30
	- Summary: Machine learning is used to optimize personalized tDCS dosage for depression treatment and classify treatment response by accounting for individual anatomical differences.

- **[Application of Machine Learning Classification to Improve the Performance of Vancomycin Therapeutic Drug Monitoring.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35631610/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Model selection
	- Published: 2022May09
	- Summary: Machine learning classifier is developed to select optimal vancomycin PK models for therapeutic drug monitoring, improving Bayesian estimation performance in individual patients.

- **[A Comparison of AI and Population PK Models to Predict the Concentrations of Antiepileptic Drugs Using Therapeutic Drug Monitoring Records.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41127895/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Deep learning, Tree-based models, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2025Oct
	- Summary: This study compares AI models (including ensemble and deep learning methods) with population PK models to predict antiepileptic drug concentrations using TDM data.

- **[A machine learning model for predicting blood concentration of quetiapine in patients with schizophrenia and depression based on real-world data.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37005382/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2023Sep
	- Summary: Machine learning techniques are used to predict quetiapine blood concentrations in schizophrenia and depression patients using real-world data to assist clinical dosing decisions.

- **[DL-ADR: a novel deep learning model for classifying genomic variants into adverse drug reactions.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/27510822/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2016Aug10
	- Summary: A deep learning model using generative stochastic networks and hidden Markov chains to classify genomic variants (SNPs) in CYP genes for predicting adverse drug reactions.

- **[Applying machine learning to the pharmacokinetic modeling of cyclosporine in adult renal transplant recipients: a multi-method comparison.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36386138/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Feature selection
	- Published: 2022
	- Summary: This study compares seven machine learning algorithms with population pharmacokinetic models for predicting cyclosporine concentrations to guide precision dosing in renal transplant patients.

- **[Optimal dynamic regimens with artificial intelligence: The case of temozolomide.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/29944669/)**
	- Methodology: Reinforcement learning
	- Published: 2018
	- Summary: Uses Monte-Carlo tree search algorithms to optimize temozolomide dosing regimens by minimizing tumor size while maintaining acceptable toxicity levels through PK/PD modeling.

- **[Machine Learning-Based Algorithm for Tacrolimus Dose Optimization in Hospitalized Kidney Transplant Patients.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41374329/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Deep learning, Neural networks, Ensemble learning, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2025Nov21
	- Summary: ML models (XGBoost, CatBoost, LightGBM, MLP) with ensemble approach predict tacrolimus concentrations for precision dosing optimization in kidney transplant patients.

- **[Development of PBPK Population Model for End-Stage Renal Disease Patients to Inform OATP1B-, BCRP-, P-gp-, and CYP3A4-Mediated Drug Disposition with Individual Influencing Factors.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40871097/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Aug20
	- Summary: Machine learning was employed to identify factors influencing individual clearance in a PBPK population model for end-stage renal disease patients to enable precision dosing.

- **[Reinforcement Learning and PK-PD Models Integration to Personalize the Adaptive Dosing Protocol of Erdafitinib in Patients with Metastatic Urothelial Carcinoma.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38339803/)**
	- Methodology: Reinforcement learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2024Apr
	- Summary: The paper integrates PK-PD models with Q-learning reinforcement learning to personalize erdafitinib dosing protocols for metastatic urothelial carcinoma patients.

- **[Simulating clinical trials for model-informed precision dosing: using warfarin treatment as a use case.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37927586/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Reinforcement learning
	- Published: 2023
	- Summary: The paper develops a simulation framework to compare AI/ML methods (neural networks, deep RL) with PKPD modeling for model-informed precision dosing using warfarin as a use case.

- **[Prediction of vancomycin plasma concentration in elderly patients based on multi-algorithm mining combined with population pharmacokinetics.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39511378/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Feature selection, Ensemble learning, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2024Nov08
	- Summary: This study develops an ensemble ML model combining SVR, LightGBM, and CatBoost with popPK parameters to predict vancomycin plasma concentrations in elderly patients for personalized dosing.

- **[Use of Machine Learning for Dosage Individualization of Vancomycin in Neonates.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37300630/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2023Aug
	- Summary: Machine learning is used to predict vancomycin treatment targets (trough concentration and AUC) to calculate optimal individualized dosing regimens for neonates.

- **[Toward Generalizable Data-Driven Pharmacokinetics with Interpretable Neural ODEs.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41738401/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Neural networks
	- Published: 2026Mar09
	- Summary: Proposes Uni-PK, a neural ODE framework combining molecular representations with mechanistic PK structure for generalizable drug concentration-time modeling.

## RWD phenotyping

- **[Boosting drug named entity recognition using an aggregate classifier.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/26116947/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2015Oct
	- Summary: The paper uses supervised machine learning with an aggregate classifier approach to improve drug named entity recognition performance without relying exclusively on manual annotations.

- **[Network-based disease fingerprinting with neuroinflammation PET imaging.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41282214/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2025Oct21
	- Summary: Machine learning classifiers are applied to network-based TSPO PET imaging data to identify disease-specific neuroinflammatory patterns and classify different neurological conditions.

- **[Maternal and Pediatric Precision in Therapeutic Knowledge Portal (MPRINT-KP): Landscape Analysis of Pharmacology Research in Maternal and Pediatric Patient Populations.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41730795/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2026Mar
	- Summary: BioBERT models were used to classify and curate pharmacology research papers for maternal and pediatric populations in the MPRINT-KP knowledge portal.

## Survival analysis

- **[Metabolomics and lipidomics predictor of survival in hepatocellular carcinoma patients receiving tyrosine kinase inhibitor and immune checkpoint inhibitor combination therapy.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41265377/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Feature selection, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2025Dec
	- Summary: Machine learning framework combining random forest, LASSO regression, and Cox modeling to identify metabolomic and lipidomic biomarkers predicting survival in HCC patients.

- **[DCE-MRI prediction of survival time for patients with glioblastoma multiforme: using an adaptive neuro-fuzzy-based model and nested model selection technique.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/28543885/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks, Model selection
	- Published: 2017Sep
	- Summary: Uses Adaptive Neuro-Fuzzy Inference System (ANFIS) with pharmacokinetic parameters from DCE-MRI to predict survival time in glioblastoma patients.

- **[Predicting overall survival from tumor dynamics metrics using parametric statistical and machine learning models: application to patients with RET-altered solid tumors.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38919267/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024
	- Summary: Machine learning models are used to predict overall survival from tumor dynamics metrics in a pan-indication approach for RET-altered solid tumor patients.

- **[Development and validation of a deep learning-based model to predict response and survival of T790M mutant non-small cell lung cancer patients in early clinical phase trials using electronic medical record and pharmacokinetic data.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38736496/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2024Apr29
	- Summary: Deep learning model using mixture-of-experts and Cox algorithm to predict treatment response and survival in T790M mutant NSCLC patients using EMR and PK data.

- **[Model-based prediction of progression-free survival for combination therapies in oncology.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37300376/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2023Sep
	- Summary: Uses machine learning-guided analysis to reduce unexplained variability in joint models predicting progression-free survival for oncology combination therapies.

- **[DN-ODE: Data-driven neural-ODE modeling for breast cancer tumor dynamics and progression-free survivals.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39089112/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2024Sep
	- Summary: Uses Neural ODEs to model individual breast cancer tumor dynamics and progression-free survival from early clinical trial data to predict later phase outcomes.

- **[Bayesian non-parametric survival regression for optimizing precision dosing of intravenous busulfan in allogeneic stem cell transplantation.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31467455/)**
	- Methodology: Bayesian ML, Gaussian processes
	- Published: 2019Apr
	- Summary: Uses Bayesian nonparametric regression with dependent Dirichlet process and Gaussian process to determine personalized optimal AUC intervals for busulfan dosing in stem cell transplantation.

- **[Impact of Machine Learning With Multiparametric Magnetic Resonance Imaging of the Breast for Early Prediction of Response to Neoadjuvant Chemotherapy and Survival Outcomes in Breast Cancer Patients.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30358693/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2019Feb
	- Summary: Machine learning is applied to multiparametric MRI data to predict pathological complete response to neoadjuvant chemotherapy and survival outcomes in breast cancer patients.

- **[Machine Learning Models of Early Longitudinal Toxicity Trajectories Predict Cetuximab Concentration and Metastatic Colorectal Cancer Survival in the Canadian Cancer Trials Group/AGITG CO.17/20 Trials.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40215447/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Apr
	- Summary: Machine learning models analyze early toxicity trajectories to predict cetuximab concentration and survival outcomes in metastatic colorectal cancer patients.

- **[Big Data Toolsets to Pharmacometrics: Application of Machine Learning for Time-to-Event Analysis.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/29536640/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2018May
	- Summary: This paper compares machine learning methods to Cox regression for time-to-event analysis using simulated data, showing ML outperforms in high-dimensional scenarios.

- **[Machine learning-guided covariate selection for time-to-event models developed from a small sample of real-world patients receiving bevacizumab treatment.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35851999/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Tree-based models, Feature selection, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2022Oct
	- Summary: Machine learning methods including LASSO, survival trees, and random forests are used to select important covariates for time-to-event models predicting survival outcomes in cancer patients.

## Reviews / Tutorials / Perspectives

- **[A Novel Perspective on Using Artificial Intelligence and Nanoinformatics to Develop Nanomedicines.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41568483/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2026Jan09
	- Summary: This review surveys AI/ML applications in nanomedicine development, focusing on QSAR/QSPR models and PBPK modeling for optimizing physicochemical properties and predicting biodistribution.

- **[A Review on AI-Based Data-Driven Models for Optimization of Nanocarriers as Drug Delivery Systems.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41668335/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Unsupervised learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2026Feb10
	- Summary: This review surveys AI/ML approaches for optimizing nanocarrier drug delivery systems, covering supervised/unsupervised learning and hybrid models for PK/PD, toxicity prediction, and personalized medicine.

- **[A Review on Artificial Intelligence Approaches and Rational Approaches in Drug Discovery.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37132148/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2023Jun06
	- Summary: This review surveys AI/ML approaches in drug discovery, covering virtual screening, neural networks like CNN/RNN/GAN, and applications in drug design and toxicity prediction.

- **[A Review on New Frontiers in Drug-Drug Interaction Predictions and Safety Evaluations with In Vitro Cellular Models.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40574059/)**
	- Published: 2025Jun06
	- Summary: This review surveys conventional and AI-enhanced methods for predicting drug-drug interactions, highlighting artificial intelligence applications in DDI safety evaluations.

- **[A Systematic Review of the Efforts and Hindrances of Modeling and Simulation of CAR T-cell Therapy.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33835308/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2021Apr09
	- Summary: A systematic review of modeling approaches for CAR T-cell therapy, including mathematical, statistical, and machine learning methods for predicting outcomes and survival.

- **[A comprehensive review of artificial intelligence for pharmacology research.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39290983/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Unsupervised learning, Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2024
	- Summary: A comprehensive review of AI applications in pharmacology research covering drug discovery, pharmacokinetic prediction, and clinical pharmacology with discussion of AI model strengths and limitations.

- **[A comprehensive review of deep learning-based approaches for drug-drug interaction prediction.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39987494/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2025Jan15
	- Summary: This review surveys deep learning approaches for predicting drug-drug interactions, covering similarity-based, network-based, and integration-based methods.

- **[A decade of machine learning-based predictive models for human pharmacokinetics: Advances and challenges.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34592448/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2022Feb
	- Summary: This review surveys machine learning-based QSAR models for predicting human pharmacokinetic parameters, examining advances and challenges over the past decade.

- **[A multiparametric organ toxicity predictor for drug discovery.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31618094/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2020Mar
	- Summary: The paper presents a multiparametric in silico model combining physicochemical properties, QSAR predictions, and docking scores to predict organ toxicity in drug discovery.

- **[A new chapter in pharmacology: Artificial intelligence's expanding role in pharmacokinetics, pharmacodynamics, and pharmacovigilance.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41093670/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Deep learning, Neural networks, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2025Sep16
	- Summary: This narrative review surveys AI applications in pharmacokinetics, pharmacodynamics, and pharmacovigilance, highlighting machine learning and neural network approaches.

- **[A new perspective on antimicrobial therapeutic drug monitoring: Surface-enhanced Raman spectroscopy.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40154051/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Sep01
	- Summary: This review surveys SERS techniques for antimicrobial therapeutic drug monitoring, including machine learning data processing methods for personalized treatment.

- **[A review on integrated machine learning and deep learning driven artificial intelligence models for pharmacokinetics and toxicokinetics predictions, and their application.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41763192/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Supervised learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2026Jan22
	- Summary: This review surveys AI/ML methods including hybrid deep learning and machine learning models for predicting ADMET properties and pharmacokinetic-toxicokinetic parameters in drug development.

- **[A tutorial review of mathematical techniques for quantifying tumor heterogeneity.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32987550/)**
	- Methodology: Bayesian ML, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2020May19
	- Summary: This tutorial reviews mathematical techniques including machine learning and Bayesian methods for quantifying tumor heterogeneity to aid precision medicine modeling.

- **[AAV Gene Therapy Drug Development and Translation of Engineered Ocular and Neurotropic Capsids: A Systematic Review Using Natural Language Processing.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41316525/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Dec
	- Summary: This systematic review uses Natural Language Processing to identify and categorize engineered AAV capsids for neurotropic and ocular applications from PubMed abstracts.

- **[ADMET modeling approaches in drug discovery.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30890362/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2019May
	- Summary: This review surveys machine learning approaches for ADMET modeling in drug discovery, covering the evolution from traditional chemometrics to advanced ML methods.

- **[AI for NONMEM Coding in Pharmacometrics Research and Education: Shortcut or Pitfall?](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41187068/)**
	- Methodology: LLM
	- Published: 2025Dec
	- Summary: This paper evaluates seven Large Language Models for generating NONMEM code in pharmacometric modeling tasks and discusses AI applications in pharmacometrics education.

- **[AI in Clinical Trials and Drug Development: Challenges and Potential Advancements.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39473260/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Deep learning
	- Published: 2025
	- Summary: A comprehensive literature review examining AI/ML applications in clinical trials and drug development, covering patient stratification, biomarker identification, and dose selection.

- **[AI screening of nuclear medicine safety breaches: patterns, causes, and opportunities for improved protocols: a systematic review.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41166047/)**
	- Published: 2026Jan
	- Summary: A systematic review examining AI applications for screening, predicting, and preventing nuclear medicine safety incidents and occupational exposure risks for healthcare professionals.

- **[AI-Driven Enhancements in Drug Screening and Optimization.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37676604/)**
	- Published: 2024
	- Summary: This review paper surveys AI applications in drug discovery including biological screening, PK optimization, toxicity prediction, and target analysis for improved pharmaceutical development.

- **[AI-driven precision in prostate brachytherapy: A systematic review of 70 studies.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41474559/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Reinforcement learning
	- Published: 2025Oct01
	- Summary: Systematic review of 70 studies evaluating AI/ML applications in prostate brachytherapy for imaging, treatment planning, and outcome prediction using deep learning and reinforcement learning.

- **[AI-enhanced therapeutic drug monitoring for vancomycin and β-lactam antibiotics in critical care: from population PK to bedside algorithms.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41760398/)**
	- Methodology: Bayesian ML, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2026Mar02
	- Summary: Reviews AI-enhanced therapeutic drug monitoring approaches for vancomycin and β-lactam antibiotics, focusing on population PK models and bedside dosing algorithms.

- **[AI-powered in silico twins: redefining precision medicine through simulation, personalization, and predictive healthcare.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41441931/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2025Dec24
	- Summary: This review explores AI-powered in silico twins that integrate mechanistic modeling with ML for personalized medicine, predictive diagnostics, and therapeutic optimization.

- **[Accelerating Therapeutics for Opportunities in Medicine: A Paradigm Shift in Drug Discovery.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32694991/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2020
	- Summary: This perspective paper describes the ATOM consortium's machine learning-based drug discovery platform that simultaneously optimizes pharmacokinetics, toxicity, and molecular design.

- **[Active metabolites combination therapies: towards the next paradigm for more efficient and more scientific Chinese medicine.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38698817/)**
	- Published: 2024
	- Summary: Review paper on traditional Chinese medicine combination therapies that mentions AI as an emerging technology but does not focus on AI/ML methods or applications.

- **[Adoption of Machine Learning in Pharmacometrics: An Overview of Recent Implementations and Their Considerations.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36145562/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Unsupervised learning, Deep learning, Tree-based models, Gaussian processes, Bayesian ML, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Feature selection, Model selection, Surrogate modeling, Neural networks, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2022Aug29
	- Summary: This review surveys ML applications across four pharmacometrics pipeline stages: data preparation, hypothesis generation, predictive modeling, and model validation.

- **[Advancements in radiation dose reduction for pediatric CT head Imaging: A scoping review of emerging Technologies, Protocols, and optimization strategies.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41567817/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2026Mar
	- Summary: A scoping review of radiation dose reduction techniques in pediatric CT head imaging, highlighting deep learning image reconstruction algorithms as advanced AI-based solutions.

- **[Advances and Challenges in Drug Screening for Cancer Therapy: A Comprehensive Review.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41463612/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Dec01
	- Summary: This review surveys AI-enabled modeling for cancer drug response prediction, biomarker development, and rational drug combinations in precision oncology screening platforms.

- **[Advances in Cytotoxicity Testing: From In Vitro Assays to In Silico Models.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41303685/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025Nov19
	- Summary: This review surveys advances in cytotoxicity testing including AI/ML methods like QSAR and machine learning for drug toxicity prediction and computational toxicology.

- **[Advances in Modeling Approaches for Oral Drug Delivery: Artificial Intelligence, Physiologically-Based Pharmacokinetics, and First-Principles Models.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39204323/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024Jul24
	- Summary: This review surveys AI/ML approaches including machine/deep learning alongside other modeling methods for oral drug absorption and pharmacokinetic modeling.

- **[Advances in PET imaging of cancer.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37258875/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2023Jul
	- Summary: This review discusses advances in PET imaging for cancer, including machine learning-based data analysis for personalized medicine applications.

- **[Advances in Surface-Enhanced Raman Spectroscopy for Therapeutic Drug Monitoring.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39795073/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024Dec24
	- Summary: This review surveys SERS advances for therapeutic drug monitoring, briefly mentioning machine learning as one emerging technology to improve analytical efficiency.

- **[Advances in flux balance analysis by integrating machine learning and mechanism-based models.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34471504/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models, Mechanism-informed machine learning
	- Published: 2021
	- Summary: This review surveys the integration of machine learning with flux balance analysis and mechanistic models for analyzing genotype-to-phenotype relationships in metabolic systems.

- **[Advances in intelligent mass spectrometry data processing technology for in vivo analysis of natural medicines.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39428182/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Unsupervised learning, Deep learning
	- Published: 2024Oct
	- Summary: This review surveys AI/ML techniques for intelligent mass spectrometry data processing to analyze natural medicines in vivo over the past two decades.

- **[Advances in nanorobotics for gastrointestinal surgery: a new frontier in precision medicine and minimally invasive therapeutics.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40660043/)**
	- Methodology: AI Agents
	- Published: 2025Jul14
	- Summary: This review surveys AI-integrated nanorobotics for precision gastrointestinal surgery, focusing on intelligent autonomous agents for minimally invasive therapeutics.

- **[Advances, opportunities, and challenges in methods for interrogating the structure activity relationships of natural products.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38912779/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Explainable AI
	- Published: 2024Oct17
	- Summary: This review surveys computational methods including machine learning and interpretable AI for analyzing structure-activity relationships of natural products in drug discovery.

- **[Advancing Drug-Drug Interaction Prediction with Biomimetic Improvements: Leveraging the Latest Artificial Intelligence Techniques to Guide Researchers in the Field.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41589955/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Deep learning, Neural networks, Ensemble learning
	- Published: 2026Jan05
	- Summary: This review comprehensively surveys AI/ML methods for drug-drug interaction prediction, covering classical ML, deep learning, graph-based models, and biomimetic approaches.

- **[Advancing PROTAC Discovery Through Artificial Intelligence: Opportunities, Challenges, and Future Directions.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41471282/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Supervised learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2025Nov25
	- Summary: This review surveys AI/ML applications in PROTAC drug discovery, covering structure prediction, degradability prediction, linker design, and ADME property estimation.

- **[Advancing Precision Medicine: A Review of Innovative In Silico Approaches for Drug Development, Clinical Pharmacology and Personalized Healthcare.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38543226/)**
	- Published: 2024Feb27
	- Summary: A review of in silico approaches including computational tools and mathematical models for advancing precision medicine in drug development and personalized healthcare.

- **[Advancing antibiotic discovery with bacterial cytological profiling: a high-throughput solution to antimicrobial resistance.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40018674/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2025
	- Summary: This review surveys bacterial cytological profiling for antibiotic discovery and discusses how AI/deep learning techniques could enhance analysis of BCP data.

- **[Advancing computer-aided drug discovery (CADD) by big data and data-driven machine learning modeling.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32663517/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning, Supervised learning
	- Published: 2020Sep
	- Summary: This review paper surveys how machine learning approaches, particularly deep learning, can leverage big data to predict drug efficacy, pharmacokinetics, and adverse effects in drug discovery.

- **[Advancing drug development with "Fit-for-Purpose" modeling informed approaches.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40952534/)**
	- Published: 2025Sep15
	- Summary: This review surveys MIDD approaches across drug development stages and explores the evolving role of AI/ML in enhancing traditional pharmacometric modeling methodologies.

	- Summary: This review paper surveys AI/ML applications and molecular simulations across drug discovery processes to enhance R&D efficiency and reduce development costs.

- **[Tumor organoids on-a-chip and the role of AI in predictive oncology and personalized cancer medicine.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41512327/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2026Jan09
	- Summary: This review surveys AI applications in tumor organoids-on-chip for predictive oncology and personalized cancer medicine, focusing on drug response prediction.

- **[Two heads are better than one: current landscape of integrating QSP and machine learning : An ISoP QSP SIG white paper by the working group on the integration of quantitative systems pharmacology and machine learning.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35103884/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2022Feb
	- Summary: A white paper reviewing the integration of quantitative systems pharmacology and machine learning approaches in drug development, identifying four categories of current research activity.

- **[Understanding gut microbiome-based machine learning platforms: A review on therapeutic approaches using deep learning.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38491814/)**
	- Methodology: Deep learning
	- Published: 2024Mar
	- Summary: This review surveys deep learning applications in gut microbiome-based machine learning platforms for therapeutic approaches and treatment optimization.

- **[Unleashing the future: The revolutionary role of machine learning and artificial intelligence in drug discovery.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39515559/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Deep learning, Neural networks
	- Published: 2024Dec15
	- Summary: This review surveys AI/ML applications across drug discovery stages, from target identification to preclinical studies, highlighting transformative impacts and future directions.

- **[Unlocking Antimicrobial Peptides from Marine Invertebrates: A Comprehensive Review of Antimicrobial Discovery.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41009902/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Deep learning
	- Published: 2025Sep12
	- Summary: This review examines antimicrobial peptides from marine invertebrates and highlights AI's role in accelerating AMP discovery, design, and production through predictive modeling.

- **[Unlocking the potential of advanced large language models in medication review and reconciliation: A proof-of-concept investigation.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39257533/)**
	- Methodology: LLM
	- Published: 2024Sep
	- Summary: This proof-of-concept study investigates the potential of large language models for medication review and reconciliation processes in healthcare.

- **[Update on antithrombotic therapy and body mass: a clinical consensus statement of the European Society of Cardiology Working Group on Cardiovascular Pharmacotherapy and the European Society of Cardiology Working Group on Thrombosis.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39237457/)**
	- Methodology: Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2024Nov06
	- Summary: Clinical consensus statement on antithrombotic therapy across body weights that includes AI simulations from in silico PK/PD models to optimize dosing regimens.

- **[Use of Artificial Intelligence in Current Fight Against Antimicrobial Resistance.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40354275/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2025May12
	- Summary: This review surveys AI/ML applications in antimicrobial resistance, focusing on optimizing dosing through PK modeling and therapeutic drug monitoring to improve outcomes.

- **[Use of Artificial Intelligence to Identify New Mechanisms and Approaches to Therapy of Bone Disorders Associated With Chronic Kidney Disease.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35402468/)**
	- Methodology: Reinforcement learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2022
	- Summary: This review proposes combining quantitative systems pharmacology, model predictive control, and reinforcement learning to develop individualized precision therapy for bone disorders in CKD.

- **[Use of machine learning approaches for novel drug discovery.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/26814169/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2016
	- Summary: This review surveys machine learning applications in drug discovery, covering target structure prediction, biological activity modeling, and ADMET profiling.

- **[Using machine learning surrogate modeling for faster QSP VP cohort generation.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37328956/)**
	- Methodology: Surrogate modeling
	- Published: 2023Aug
	- Summary: This tutorial demonstrates using machine learning surrogate models to efficiently generate virtual patient cohorts for quantitative systems pharmacology modeling.

- **[Utility of life stage-specific chemical risk assessments based on New Approach Methodologies (NAMs).](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38844066/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2024Aug
	- Summary: This review discusses the utility of AI/ML and computational models for life stage-specific chemical risk assessment using PBPK modeling and other NAMs approaches.

- **[Utilizing public and private sector data to build better machine learning models for the prediction of pharmacokinetic parameters.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35973660/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2022Nov
	- Summary: Reviews technical aspects of public-private partnerships for collecting data to build machine learning models that predict pharmacokinetic parameters.

- **[Vancomycin resistance in gram-positive infections: evolutionary strategies of survival.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41563485/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Hybrid mechanistic–ML models
	- Published: 2026Jan21
	- Summary: This review examines vancomycin resistance mechanisms and highlights AI/ML applications in antimicrobial discovery, resistance surveillance, and rational drug design.

- **[What Are the Current Approaches to Optimising Antimicrobial Dosing in the Intensive Care Unit?](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32645953/)**
	- Methodology: Bayesian ML
	- Published: 2020Jul07
	- Summary: This review surveys antimicrobial dosing optimization methods in ICU patients, including novel software utilizing Bayesian forecasting and AI for personalized therapy.

- **[Where to next prostate-specific membrane antigen PET imaging frontiers?](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32701718/)**
	- Published: 2020Sep
	- Summary: This review surveys recent developments in PSMA PET imaging including the introduction of artificial intelligence in nuclear imaging applications.

- **[[Advances in warfarin's anticoagulation therapy in Chinese population after mechanical valve replacement].](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38548617/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2024May01
	- Summary: This review surveys advances in warfarin dose prediction algorithms for Chinese patients, highlighting machine learning methods that improve upon traditional regression approaches.

- **[[Application of drug-target prediction technology in network pharmacology of traditional Chinese medicine].](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/28868850/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning
	- Published: 2016Feb
	- Summary: Reviews drug-target prediction technologies including machine learning methods for network pharmacology applications in traditional Chinese medicine.

- **[[Generating knowledge from complex data sets in human experimental pain research].](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31478142/)**
	- Methodology: Supervised learning, Unsupervised learning
	- Published: 2019Dec
	- Summary: This review surveys machine learning applications in pain research for drug discovery, patient phenotyping, and clinical outcome prediction from complex biomedical data.

- **[[Is possible to decrease the risk of development of undesirable effects of medications applying computer technologies? (a review)].](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37642104/)**
	- Published: 2023Jul
	- Summary: This review surveys AI applications in pharmacotherapy for reducing medication side effects, covering drug selection, interaction prediction, and dosage optimization.

- **[[Methods of artificial intelligence: a new trend in pharmacy].](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/16124196/)**
	- Methodology: Neural networks
	- Published: 2005Jul
	- Summary: This review paper surveys artificial neural networks and genetic algorithms applications in pharmaceutical data analysis for property prediction and toxicological modeling.

- **[[Phenotypic drug discovery promotes research and development of innovative drugs based on traditional Chinese medicine].](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39041072/)**
	- Published: 2024Jun
	- Summary: This perspective paper discusses how AI and phenotypic drug discovery can advance traditional Chinese medicine drug development, but does not detail specific AI/ML methods.
